user_persona: New Orleans local who values quick, convenient service, consistent comfort food, reliable delivery, friendly staff, and satisfying casual spots for family-friendly meals.

history.name: The Creole Creamery
history.snippet: Sweet Jesus. Does it get any better? I wanted to give four stars because they are making me fat but that seems unfair. Maybe. Best banana split of my life. Service always with a smile. Never leave me C.C.

history.name: The Rum House
history.snippet: The service is always good but the food...hit or miss. Bartenders are quick. Always a plus. Wish there was more consistency with the food though. Sometimes super yummy and sometimes very bland.

history.name: McDonald's
history.snippet: Ok. So they always forget something..no shocker. It's been years of getting a hair and grabbing something. Something is always missing. Fine. The sauce or straws or whatev. Today was the winner. Driving home from work and want a monster truck. Swing in for a happy meal. Sit in t...

history.name: The Notary Shoppe - New Orleans
history.snippet: Yes it's one guy and yes he is no nonsense. But he gets it done and this spot is extremely convenient. Totally worth the extra $.

history.name: New China
history.snippet: Thank you for fast delivery always! This place has always come through. Solid spot and consistent food.

history.name: Warby Parker
history.snippet: How is it I was in and out in half an hour and ordered glasses that didn't make me look like a monster face? Well easy. Because the employees here know how to pick frames for your weird not symmetrical dork face. You're welcome.

history.name: Magazine Pizza
history.snippet: Ordered thru postmates cause corona. Got a bunch of things but the Greek salad? What a nice surprise. We've all ordered salads to go and been disappointed before. So big and satisfying. I also ordered pizza and mac and cheese bites for the kids. They loved it. But that salad? I...

user_persona: Brentwood diner who likes cozy, reasonably priced breakfast and lunch spots, specialty coffee, and dependable casual restaurants with strong food and service standards.

history.name: The Perch
history.snippet: A wonderful breakfast or lunch spot. Cozy and cute without being overpriced. Food is amazing. The honey lavender latte makes even Monday bearable.

history.name: The Stillery, Midtown
history.snippet: The stillery has been one of my favorite spots to visit in Nashville. The hot chicken options are always delicious. I was however, very disappointed with a visit over the weekend. The bartender was not wearing a mask for most of our dinner, and the bar did have guests sitting at...

user_persona: Indianapolis nightlife diner who prioritizes lively atmosphere, attentive service, standout desserts, good-value pizza, and menus with vegetarian or vegan options.

history.name: Princess Confections Bakery
history.snippet: Not only is the owner professional and great at what she does, but the treats and confections she makes taste amazing! I've had a birthday cake and birthday cupcakes done by her, and every time friends are blown away by presentation and taste. Would highly recommend her bakery t...

history.name: CharBlue
history.snippet: Great atmosphere, wonderful staff and the food speaks for itself. They even have vegetarian options! Great food includes their crab Mac and cheese, lobster pops, steak frites, and the dessert is pretty awesome as well. The servers are always on point and are good at checking in...

history.name: Bakersfield
history.snippet: Food was mid to be honest. Nice atmosphere, great decor, but food isn't anything too special.

history.name: Bazbeaux Pizza
history.snippet: BOMB ASS PIZZA. That's all you really need to know. Good prices and options for all kinds of pizza lovers ( and vegan options too). Highly recommend them for sure.

user_persona: Tampa food explorer who likes good-value tacos, brunch, coffee, happy-hour drinks, local markets, outdoor-friendly spots, and quick service for groups.

history.name: AJ's Press
history.snippet: What a friendly and cute little restaurant!! I had the Cuban with their chips and both were delicious. I got a free order(by accident) of their famous hush puppies and they were just the right amount of heat (make sure to try the sauce it comes with!). This isn't the place to go...

history.name: DI Coffee Bar
history.snippet: Meets every check box for a great coffee spot: Drinks, food, wifi, outlets and friendly staff! I can't recommend this place enough. The furniture and artwork in the shop has a rustic look and some of it is for sale (at a high price tag). Unlike the artwork, the coffee and food i...

history.name: Escape Countdown
history.snippet: Some friends and I decided to go here since we have gone to other Escape rooms in the area but unfortunately left disappointed. The man at the front was very welcoming and the decor was pleasant. We did the mad hatter room. We got a strange vibe from the place and the rooms appe...

history.name: Green Lemon
history.snippet: Love coming here for Taco Tuesday! Great prices ($2 tacos!) and drink specials. The customer service is always great and quick no matter how large of a group I have been with. I can always rely on tasty food and quality drinks. They have a nice selection of tacos but definitely...

history.name: Mini Doughnut Factory
history.snippet: Great sweet tooth fix! I was really craving something sweet and wanted to try something new. I've been meaning to try this place out and I'm glad I did! I got 3 mini doughnuts for less than $4. Not only is the price right but the staff is friendly and willing to help new comers...

history.name: MIDFLORIDA Credit Union Amphitheatre
history.snippet: I can't believe it has taken me this long to review this venue! This is my absolute favorite place to see a country concert. I really enjoy the lawn seats however there are definitely a few pros and cons you should weigh before buying a ticket there. The lawn can get VERY crowde...

history.name: Weedon Island Preserve
history.snippet: This is a little slice of heaven! I have been wanting to come here and explore and I am so glad I did. The trails are well marked and kept up nicely. There are multiple trails- both of which I went on were boardwalks. One of them leads to a 3 story observation tower with beautif...

history.name: Brunchery Restaurant & Catering
history.snippet: Cute little brunch spot to take friends and family! Food choices were your classic breakfast and lunch options but all was tasty. There was a wait on a Sunday morning so make sure to give yourself some extra time. Plenty of parking as it is in a shopping center.

history.name: Lolis Mexican Cravings
history.snippet: What an authentic Mexican dive! I ordered the ribeye and pastor tacos and both were very tasty. The tacos don't come with any extras, however they can be added for small additional price. I recommend the Pico and avocado. The tacos also came with a delicious green sauce. I defin...

history.name: California Tacos To Go
history.snippet: You can't go wrong with classic fish tacos! I love their tacos but the only issue I have is how quickly it adds up to get toppings added. The service is quick and the food never disappoints but it tends to run higher than other local taco joints.

history.name: Los Comparres
history.snippet: This little food truck is truly authentic food. I stopped for steak tacos and was pleasantly surprised!! The smells when you pull in to the parking lot are incredible. The tacos are fresh and come with cilantro and onions. They also have 2 salsas available that are a little spic...

history.name: Royal Palace Thai
history.snippet: Lovely Thai restaurant in SoHo. I came with some friends on a Sunday night and it was packed. The place is pretty small so a lot of people were in line for takeout. They must have been short staff because we waited a good amount of time to get drinks and place our orders. They s...

history.name: The Stone Soup Company
history.snippet: Lovely eatery in ybor! The menu is pretty extensive with some original offerings that made it hard to choose. There is a deal to do soup and a sandwich but only during the weekdays. The food was tasty and was a large portion. I got the Cuban and while it was good, I was not blow...

history.name: Coppertail Brewing Co
history.snippet: I have been wanting to try this brewery based on all of the great things I've heard from friends. It definitely did not disappoint! I only went for a short time but I had a group of 8 of us who wanted to go on the tour. It costs $7 and includes the tour, samples, a pint of beer...

history.name: Cooper's Hawk Winery & Restaurants - Tampa
history.snippet: $5 wine!! Do I need to say more?! This is a great spot for a nice dinner, date night or happy hour spot. I went for happy hour (3-6pm) around 4:30 on a Thursday and had a short wait to be seated. They is an area for open bar but it was very full and I'm not sure if you can order...

history.name: Fresh Market of Hyde Park
history.snippet: My favorite Sunday of the month! This is one of the best markets I have been to in the Tampa Bay area. The vendors range from fresh produce (at a good price) to local food stands (my favorite is Mouna's Delicacies) and food trucks. There are also some handmade goods as well as l...

history.name: The Dunedin Smokehouse
history.snippet: My parents and I went here on a Sunday afternoon while at the arts festival. It was perfect weather outside so we chose to sit outside and had a 20 minute wait. Luckily happy hour was still going on so we started off with $2 Yuenglings while we looked over the menu. There was al...

history.name: Cafe Dufrain
history.snippet: Daily happy hour, delicious food and great views! I have been here once before ( a few years ago) and was blown away by the food so I can't believe it has taken a 2nd visit for me to finally review it! Even after a few years, the food is just as tasty. The menu has changed a bit...

history.name: The Family Friendly Farm
history.snippet: Great selection of produce and locally sourced goods. I came near closing time so there were not as many offerings but I will definitely check it out again. I've passed this place so many times and have been wanting to stop- and I was not disappointed! The staff was friendly and...

history.name: Brick House Tavern + Tap
history.snippet: A more than average place to go in Tampa. With all the options of great food spots, this place didn't strike me as somewhere I need to go again. The atmosphere was good (especially for watching sports games as there were plenty of tvs) and there was some cool couch seating. The...

history.name: Channel Park Trailhead
history.snippet: I love reviewing outdoor parks because I am very rarely disappointed! I wanted to go for a walk on a nice day and this was the perfect spot to do so! If you enter the upper Tampa bay trail using this trailhead there is a large parking lot (costs $2) with a large facility with ba...

history.name: The Blind Goat Food and Drink
history.snippet: Great FSU bar in south Tampa! This place is super easy to miss and when you walk in it feels like a house turned into a bar but has a very local feel to it. It is pretty small but can hold more people than you wold think. It is a great atmosphere for watching fsu football games!...

history.name: Miguelitos Taqueria Y Tequilas
history.snippet: On a nice day, ask to sit outside! It is such a cute area and is covered nicely so it doesn't get too hot (or cold during fl winter). The drinks were a little pricey for what you get and I was disappointed with the 'skinny margarita' as it tasted like straight lime juice. The ot...

user_persona: Tampa restaurant seeker who values fair prices, welcoming crews, quick service, fresh food, and comfortable spots for Chinese, Peruvian, or steak meals.

history.name: Ray's Café & Teahouse
history.snippet: Ray's Café & Teahouse is incredible. That's all. Just amazing. I have zero complaints fair prices, absolutely worth for what you get. After reading through the reviews, I tried Vegetable Dumplings, and I have to say it was a solid winner. The crew was fantastic, literally every...

history.name: Cuenelli's Peruvian Restaurant
history.snippet: Everything about Cuenelli's Peruvian Restaurant is great. Excellent mood, and service is quick. Cuenelli's Peruvian Restaurant has an incredible Pollo a La Brasa, that was my first time giving it a try here, and I was surprised with how good it was. Service was amazing and every...

history.name: Bern's Steak House
history.snippet: Thanks to yelp for highly recommending Bern's Steak House. It's comfy, clean and attractive. Pretty much every thing that we have purchased was really fresh and tasty. The crew was really welcoming and service is very good. I love Bern's Steak House and will certainly come back.

user_persona: Granite City diner who favors affordable Mexican restaurants with house-made salsa, margaritas, consistent cooking, clean spaces, and attentive service.

history.name: El Torero Family Mexican Restaurant
history.snippet: My favorite place to eat in GC. The salsa made in house is my favorite, the food is great 9 times out of 10, excellent service and you can't beat the prices! Great margaritas.

history.name: Pizza World
history.snippet: This place used to be the best pizza in GC but, like other reviewers have stated, pizza may be disgusting undercooked or over cooked. I won't risk them again.

history.name: Club Fitness - Granite City
history.snippet: Not as big as the other gyms, but clean, plenty of machines with little to no wait (except for extremely busy times). It can take awhile to fix broken machines, but it's never kept me from my workout. Friendly staff.

history.name: Si Senor Mex Mex Grill
history.snippet: The food isn't awful but is fairly tasteless. The service was bad every time we went. We were often ignored by wait staff while dining and one time, we were kept waiting an incredibly long time to be seated while several servers avoided eye contact with us so we left. Will not g...

history.name: Buffalo Wild Wings
history.snippet: The last time I went, I was served a very cold burger. I told the waitress, who used a paper bowl to take just the hamburger (with my bite mark in it) away to the kitchen, then brought the same burger (now microwaved) back to me, while a manager watched. I will never go to any B...

user_persona: St. Petersburg area foodie who likes lively date-night spots, craft cocktails, burgers, pizza, Mexican food, breweries, generous portions, and attentive service.

history.name: The Sandwich on Main
history.snippet: This is hands down the best little sandwich place n Pinellas county! A gem! I have been going for years and it seems to get better and better! Never disappoints!

history.name: Noble Crust
history.snippet: Always good food and great drinks! Only complaint is the tables are small and feels very crowded at times. The service is always good and attentive!

history.name: Stillwaters Tavern
history.snippet: Love the food, atmosphere and drinks! Service is sometimes a little slow but we presume it's because of the split areas of outside and inside...and busy downtown location! Highly recommend for a fun and great date location!

history.name: Hawkers Asian Street Food
history.snippet: It's a good place but between the price and small portions we limit our visits. we have hit and miss on the service which definitely limits the visits. The food is normally good without complaints.

history.name: Buya
history.snippet: Love this place! Hands down best crispy duck ever! The best selections of bourbon and whisky....and saki! Service is always good, bar area has great service and bartenders! Highly recommended!

history.name: Copper Shaker
history.snippet: Let me say first and foremost, we were on the hunt for a great place to have cocktails and enjoy time with each other and friends! Found it! Love this place! Our bartender Jason was amazing and truly an artist of his craft! Love! Love ! Love this place!! Five stars all the way!...

history.name: The Wheelhouse
history.snippet: We thoroughly enjoy this place! They have good food, service and drinks! The atmosphere is causal and local! Rachel is a great at providing suggestions and opinions! Recommend for a relaxing meal and drinks! Family friendly!

history.name: The VIP Mexican Restaurant & Lounge
history.snippet: Hands down the best Mexican restaurant in Pinellas County! It's small but worth the wait! The skinny margarita is our favorite! The queso and 3 amigo's are scrumptious! If you haven't been it's a must try!

history.name: Bodega
history.snippet: Love this place for everything but my favorite is the wheatgrass shots! The food is always delicious and the juice bar is fantastic! Highly recommend, you will NOT be disappointed!

history.name: The Mill Restaurant
history.snippet: Great place for events, groups and date night!! We love the food and drinks. It can be. Little expensive if you enjoy just hanging! They have a happy hour but we didn't find it cost effective in the end! Worth the price.

history.name: Engine No 9
history.snippet: Great burgers and sides! Great beer selection and kids love the tater-tots! So many different specialty burgers it makes your mouth water! Best hangover place!

history.name: Grand Hacienda
history.snippet: The place has a great Coconut Margarita! However, the food is not consistent, service lacks when it is busy and the margaritas are liquor weak, it's getting 3 stars because I think it has room to be better! We will go back but not anytime soon!

history.name: Treasure Island Beach
history.snippet: I love this place! It's necessary for all locals to enjoy. I love the food, the beautiful beach and the vibe is amazing! Favorite beach from a Pinellas County Floridian!

history.name: Oak & Stone - St. Petersburg
history.snippet: I cannot say enough good things about this place! And it's ONLY the soft opening! Wow! Hands down the best thin pizza in the area!! And the beers on tap...Jalapeño Scotch Ale..on tap! Heaven help me! The drinks ...JAM YOU is amazing!! And we had GREAT service! Thanks Ian!!! I ca...

history.name: Maple Street Biscuit Company - Downtown St. Pete
history.snippet: Great food and fun atmosphere! we will be going back again! Lots of different types of biscuit dishes to try! The Chai Latte was good. The prices were slightly higher than expected, but the food was delicious!

history.name: BRGR Kitchen & Bar
history.snippet: This place has a great opportunity to be amazing! The burger options and build your own is fantastic, however we experienced some hiccups with servers and delayed service. The drinks were pretty good but went empty fast. Overall good food but needs better service! We will be bac...

history.name: Oak & Stone - St. Petersburg
history.snippet: I cannot say enough good things about this place! And it's ONLY the soft opening! Wow! Hands down the best thin pizza in the area!! And the beers on tap...Jalapeño Scotch Ale..on tap! Heaven help me! The drinks ...JAM YOU is amazing!! And we had GREAT service! Thanks Zak!!! I ca...

history.name: Callaloo
history.snippet: This is a place to come & enjoy getting your food on! Loved the food choices, the taste and the portions! Great for groups! Will be going back with friends! Had drinks which were good, but nothing spectacular, that won't deter us from visiting and trying something new.

history.name: Happy Salmon
history.snippet: What can I say...Perfection! Love love love everything about this place! It's fresh food, delicious drinks and quiet atmosphere! The dumplings melted (yes melted) in my mouth! It's an amazing and quaintly perfect place! If you love sushi...Try it, I promise you...you will love i...

history.name: Crooked Thumb Brewery
history.snippet: It's a brewery! Great people! Ask for Josh! The beer is better than most brewers and it's a huge space that is pet friendly and kid tolerable (my words)! Will be a frequent visitor...great place to enjoy people, weather and chillaxin!!!!

history.name: Gigglewaters
history.snippet: What a great change of pace!! This place has lots to offer and we LOVE their smoked Manhattans! They do several variations of smoked drinks! Such a much needed difference in the scene! The food is over the top...meaning LOTS of it! Will be back again and again!!

history.name: Bar Fly Safety Harbor
history.snippet: I want to give it great reviews but it's ok! Great for a Saturday afternoon hang out with friends while drinking! But a little surprised by the lack of goodness. It's ok, but nothing that I have to have!! The atmosphere is fun and outside is good for relaxing and enjoying a few...

history.name: Oak & Stone - St. Petersburg
history.snippet: Well it was much better when it first open, I am sad to report that every time we have returned I The quality of people and food has gone down. While the pizza is still fantastic, we haven't been able to get the same level of enthusiasm we had!

history.name: Flamestone American Grill
history.snippet: We enjoy this place! Great cocktails and amazing food! Such a great variety of food and drinks! Quaint atmosphere and attentive staff! We think this is a great place for date night and just a quick bite! Highly recommend!

history.name: Mountaineer Coffee
history.snippet: This is the best coffee place in the area! OMG! We get iced latte with extra shot and homemade flavorings! I highly suggest y Trying the brown sugar cinnamon with your coffee! Whatever they do ...they do it right! We are spoiled and cannot get enough! Try it, you will like it! F...

history.name: Craft Street Kitchen - Oldsmar
history.snippet: This is a good place to hang with friends, watch games and/or just spend an evening eating good food. It only gets 4 stars because the menu is great but still eclectic which makes it hard to choose or try. Kinda foodie with a great bar! The service is decent, just haven't been w...

history.name: The Little Lamb
history.snippet: Hands down best place for a foodie!! Wow! Try it! If you like unique flavors and delicious food, you will not be disappointed! The cauliflower and short ribs are to die for! The beet salad is the best I have ever had! Everything is fresh and made in house! Five glowing stars!

history.name: The Black Pearl
history.snippet: I will measure all things against this place!!!! This is perfection for anyone who loves food and tastes!! By far the best meal we have had ...ever!!! Pablo was the most amazing waiter and emphasizes the amazing-ness of this place! The food, the Wine, the dessert....we have no m...

history.name: TacoSon
history.snippet: What I can say...other than BOOM! Delicious on all scales and levels!! We shared the Nachos Supreme with barbacoa, side of guacamole and salsa! So much we could barely finish! Everything we saw around us looked amazing! Everyone was happy feeding their faces! Says a lot! The sta...

history.name: Pizza Box
history.snippet: Well after reading many many reviews we finally made our way to the Pizza Box! It does not disappoint at all! We shared a large (medium in comparison from other places)..split 1/2 Lover Boy and 1/2 Beer Garden! We ate it all! Cooked to perfection...the crust was absolutely amazi...

history.name: Dr. BBQ
history.snippet: We give it a five star because while we always have great food, the atmosphere and people are always great no matter what! We are frequent visitors, love their Frito Pie and combination plates! Their drinks are fantastic, as are their desserts! The little weird stuff could NEVER...

history.name: Mazzaro's Italian Market
history.snippet: Everything is great! If you have a bad experience here - it's your own fault! I could never imagine a better place in the area for great Italian foods, cheeses and wines...and wonderful people who work there! Been going there for years...as long as they are open, I will always v...

history.name: Whiskey Cake
history.snippet: After many people recommending this place we finally made it there! AND NOTHING was disappointing!! We could not make up minds but stayed with recommended items...goat Cheese Fondue and the Local Board (large charcuterie) OMG...it was delicious and super delicious! We highly rec...

history.name: Troubled Waters Brewing
history.snippet: Great brews on tap! And they have done a great job with the change over! It's a great local brewery for meeting friends and hanging out. There is something for everyone!

history.name: The Local Draught House
history.snippet: Well we finally made it...only 8 minutes away from the house...and our new favorite place to hang! They have everything from craft beer to full liquor bar...AND cigar bar area (separated)! Great bartender, Joe! Open, friendly and just what we needed! Looking forward to many more...

history.name: Gen X Tavern
history.snippet: I love the idea of this place! I want to give it more time to get its groove on! I am an 80's girl...love so many things about it BUT....too many windows make it feel awkward. The food was Lukewarm at best. We had 3 things: the Waffle Fry Nachos, Pimento Dip, and Chicken salad....

user_persona: Nashville diner who likes welcoming neighborhood restaurants with generous portions, polished service, standout pasta, and health-conscious shops that tailor advice.

history.name: Porter House Bistro
history.snippet: Had a great dinner at this new neighborhood spot. Pasta of the day was superb. Awesome service, outstanding food and a great menu. The owners and staff are so welcoming and friendly. Wanted to try the chocolate cake with sorbet but my dinner portion was huge! Enough to take home...

history.name: Mid 10 Nutrition
history.snippet: I have been using Mid10 for around 4 years for all of my protein/supps/bars and much more! They are amazing at tailoring a program just for your specific needs. Make an appt with Anna to go over your diet and she will set you straight! Haven't been to GNC or Vitamin shop in year...

user_persona: Edmonton wellness seeker who needs responsive staff, clean studios, helpful instructors, fair prices, and reliable service before committing to fitness or beauty providers.

history.name: Aradia Fitness
history.snippet: Most unprofessional company out there. Called for 3 weeks straight not one person picked up or return my voicemails. Even sent countless emails. Heard from other people they had the same problem. And if you actually end up getting a chance to take a class, if ever the instructor...

history.name: Aradia Fitness Edmonton _ Whyte ave
history.snippet: They will not answer there phones to take in new customers. Go ahead and try yourself you won't get through to them tried everyday for a week. Beyond useless at running a business.

history.name: The Yoga Center
history.snippet: I love this yoga studio. It is very clean it smells amazing. The instructors are great and very helpful. The prices are great to! I recommend going to this studio to everyone. I leave feeling so relaxed and happy!

history.name: Chatters Hair Salon
history.snippet: Worst salon for getting hair done. Made me cry destroyed my hair then told me I couldn't come back again for crying. I have had several other girls leave with burnt or damaged or destroyed hair to. The only good thing is that they sell all the best hair products but don't get yo...

user_persona: Lahaska-area diner who avoids crowded, disappointing buffet meals and prefers early dinners with good service, strong Italian food, and comfortable seating.

history.name: Cock 'n Bull Restaurant
history.snippet: I have never had a good dining experience at Cock 'n Bull and had hoped to eat at Hart's Tavern for lunch today with my daughter; however, because of the very long wait for a table there, we thought we'd give Cock 'n Bull another chance. Since it was Sunday, it was a buffet brun...

history.name: Bacco Centro
history.snippet: Went for an early dinner during the week and it was pretty empty when we arrived but was quite full by the time we left. The service was good and the food was great. The rustic decor is very nice. My only complaint is those orange metal chairs are REALLY uncomfortable!

user_persona: New Orleans visitor who enjoys friendly bars, small dessert cafes, spicy specialty foods, Cajun seafood, markets, and worthwhile local stops at fair prices.

history.name: Tropical Isle
history.snippet: Awesome bar. Very nicely decorated and the bartenders were very nice. Must try this bar if you visit New Orleans.

history.name: Churros Cafe
history.snippet: Best churros I have every eaten. Small place but the definitely worth checking out.

history.name: Pepper Palace
history.snippet: Cool place to check out. Have various sauces to try and have a great selection of sauces and seasonings to pick from.

history.name: Palace Café
history.snippet: Very nice restaurant. The food is delicious and the staff is very friendly. Definitely recommend for anyone visiting New Orleans.

history.name: French Market
history.snippet: Cool place to check out. Have anything you can think of and the prices aren't that bad.

user_persona: Madeira Beach vacation diner who wants fresh homemade food, excellent service, seafood, Thai, live-entertainment views, happy hour, and creamy desserts.

history.name: Thai-Am 2
history.snippet: Food was incredible! Fresh, delicious, homemade everything, even lemonade for my kids. Service was incredible, brought us a free appetizer because they thought we waited longer than expected. Eclectic, full of character. Sushi was delicious! Wow, we are so glad we found this tha...

history.name: Shrimpys Blues Bistro
history.snippet: Food was awesome, drinks were awesome, service was great, live entertainment. Parking can be tough. Nice sunset view, really good happy hour. Caesar dressing was delicious and po boy was amazing!

history.name: The Boardwalk Grill
history.snippet: Amazing food. Best lobster roll ever had- must try! Expect to wait because it's small but WORTH EVERY MINUTE!

history.name: Ice and Cream Creamery
history.snippet: Homemade, so fresh, and creamy. Service was outstanding. Amazing 48 flavors, super cute decor!

history.name: Larry's  Ice Cream &  Gelatos
history.snippet: I've cream was icy not creamy at all. With all of the options nearby, this is not a good choice. The donuts looked delicious though, we just didn't try!

user_persona: Carmel consumer who expects reliable delivery, undamaged installs, clear timelines, and responsive service, while tolerating waits when quality is excellent.

history.name: Lowe's Home Improvement
history.snippet: Never again will I buy an appliance from Lowes. I was told it would be in by the end of the week. Two weeks later it arrived at the warehouse. However, it was damaged. So, I wait another week. I was given an extra 10% off, so that was nice. I then have the dishwasher installed ....

history.name: Fratelli's Ristorante
history.snippet: This was an absolute amazing meal. Bread was piping hot. Service was excellent. The Roasted Chicken Cannelloni was mouth watering. The wait time of 30 minutes was well worth the service and meal. This is a MUST stop.

user_persona: Newtown-area food researcher who favors fresh ingredients, Thai, Indian, Italian, BYOB dinners, helpful servers, reservations, and adventurous menus with low-spice options.

history.name: Thai Tida By Mike & Natta
history.snippet: Two words... Fresh Ingredients, and Organic. Wow, have been coming here for years and this time the food was how food should taste and look. Passion goes into the recipes along with the fresh ingredients. We shared a lovely salad with spiced and an order of spring rolls. The mix...

history.name: Thai Tida By Mike & Natta
history.snippet: Had a great meal... Snapper dish was tasty and moist. Tried a coconut broth chicken dish. Staff is always so pleasant.

history.name: Indian Garden
history.snippet: Not often we hand out 5 stars as a dinning experience is more than just the meal, it's the entire experience. For us, Indian Garden delivers. We shared three dishes.

history.name: Concerto Fusion
history.snippet: First time was a solid 4, we ate in-restaurant. We started off with the Crawfish Spring Roll, very nice presentation and tasty... just let it cool down a little. We jumped right in and burnt the tops of mouths. Oh well. Was worth it. While the Thai Red Snapper dish was really ni...

history.name: International Noodles
history.snippet: Just okay. Was hoping their ramen would fulfill my quest to fill in for the great ramen is get in NYC. Was a safe meal at best.

Dogs are for the most part on leash but there is the occasional off leash dog running abou...

history.name: Sweetwater Organic Community Farm
history.snippet: Very cute! Exactly what you expect a Farmers market to be and huge upside they actually have a natural dog treat vendor on site (my dog enjoyed gobbling up all of the free samples). The food was a really high quality and although the location itself was small a lot was going on...

history.name: The Breakfast Nook
history.snippet: I've been here both with and without my dog for brunch and both times were great experiences! The food is really great with large servings - honestly it reminds me of a home cooked meal. The outdoor seating is a huge bonus and the tables are very nicely spaced from one another t...

history.name: Honeymoon Island State Park
history.snippet: My all time favorite dog beach (yes even over Fort de Soto!). The big draw here is that not only is it a huge area with shallow water for dogs to romp and play its also on leash. Dogs and dog owners are able to enjoy their own space and play without having to be worried about ot...

user_persona: Clearwater foodie who likes seafood, sour beers, breweries, sushi, ramen, pizza, healthy grab-and-go lunches, happy hour, and friendly attentive service.

history.name: Salt n Pepper Bistro
history.snippet: Went here for dinner on Friday. Food was great. We ordered the appetizer special which was a cheesy ham and mushroom dish with pastry on top. My boyfriend ordered a savory crepe and I ordered some fresh made pasta. I had originally wanted the Bolognese but that was not available...

history.name: Cuvée 103
history.snippet: Cuvee is a solid 4.5 to me. We went here on a Friday night. Very nice atmosphere and setting. My boyfriend and I shared the Charcuterie platter. Nice selection and very good. We split a bottle of wine. It was just so-so. We ordered a special, the tuna seared with asparagus and m...

history.name: Juli's Restaurant
history.snippet: Had lunch here the other day. Wasn't great, but wasn't horrible. I had the green enchiladas, just very acidic and not very flavorful. Beans and rice were pretty bland. Boyfriend got a burrito that was alright, bland but not bad.

history.name: West Bay Tap House
history.snippet: Great place to have good beer. Great selection with a lot of choices. Have been here a few times recently and each has been a good experience. Bartender was super knowledgeable and recommended beer to my friend who is new to the craft beer world.

history.name: Casa Tina
history.snippet: I come here and eat every couple months. Great salsa and food. Sangria is yummy etc. Probably one of the better mexican restaurant settings in the Clearwater/Dunedjn area.

history.name: Lat 28
history.snippet: Yum! We ordered the Bianca pizza with chicken added. Was very good!! I have eaten a lot of different pizza places in area and this was one of the best I have had. Good beer selection and service was great! Update as of early 2016: I haven't gone back here in a while. I was going...

history.name: Bavaro's Pizza Napoletana & Pastaria
history.snippet: We came here for dinner. It had a neat atmosphere but the service was mediocre. Our server wasn't very attentive. That being said, the pizza wasn't anything to write home about but was enjoyable nonetheless.

history.name: Asian Pearl
history.snippet: This place is great. I got the salt and pepper tofu. It was amazing. The tofu was perfect and had a fresh mix of onions, peppers cilantro and other ingredients as a garnish with it. We got the clay pot of chicken, sausage and mushrooms which was also amazing. Then got the groupe...

history.name: Parts Of Paris
history.snippet: I have been wanting to come here for a while. We ordered the steak tartare and French onion soup to start. I really enjoyed the steak tartare and the French onion soup was good but a bit too sweet for me. After that we both ordered from the specials. My boyfriend had the barracu...

history.name: FuKu Japanese Cafe
history.snippet: I came here last night wit my boyfriend. When we got there only about 6 other people were in the restaurant and it looked like 2 waiters. We picked a table and sat down after standing and wondering for like 15 seconds on if it was seat yourself. No one said anything so we just s...

history.name: Boca - Tampa
history.snippet: Went here for a pre Valentine's dinner. I ordered the Duck and Dumplings and my boyfriend ordered the staff meal. We also got mussels as the appetizer. Everything we got was sooo good. I liked the atmosphere and the service was great. A bit pricey, but I enjoyed everything I ate...

history.name: La Trattoria Restaurant Da Gaetano
history.snippet: Yum! Came to this place after reading the reviews on yelp. No menus like the other reviewers, which I wasn't sure how it would work. Pretty much you can see all the meat choices right on the cart that he brings up with any preparation of your liking. Amazingly great. You can tel...

history.name: The Carthage Grille
history.snippet: Was a bit hesitant to come here at first. I hadn't heard anything about this place but it is near me and seemed like it wouldn't be too busy. I enjoyed everything we got from here. i ordered a chicken tangine that was great, and my fiance ordered a fish dish. Nice atmosphere, bu...

history.name: The Little Lamb
history.snippet: Yum! Came here for dinner last night and it was Really good. The place is not super big, but the interior is cool and industrial feel. We had the Poutine to start - Salty in a good way and had cheese curds mixed in. Next, we had the wedge salad. This had a lot of flavor that I w...

history.name: The Breakroom Bar & Grill
history.snippet: My friend found this place to meet up for happy hour on Friday. Bartender was super friendly and attentive. Good beer options and deals for happy hour. Menu looked yum and I ordered a chicken philly that was good. My friend got loaded fries that looked amazing. Overall it was a...

history.name: Arkane Aleworks
history.snippet: I came here on Friday night for the first time after hearing about this place for a while. It is in a strip mall in an unexpected location. They had plenty of seating and a lot of space. I am really into the sour beers right now and they had about 5 on tap. I tried three of them...

history.name: Ichicoro Ramen
history.snippet: Came here for lunch today after hearing about this place for a while. The interior is pretty cool. It's a bit tight to walk around by the tables though. Good music but a bit too loud. Overall a nice atmosphere. We ordered the Karaage, Tonkotsu and mushroom ramen. Was good but fe...

history.name: Ava
history.snippet: My fiancé and I came to this restaurant last Friday. I was a bit apprehensive because of the reviews but once we got there was pleasantly surprised. We started with the meat and cheese platter. The sausage on that that they cure in house was amazing. We then got the Filetto pizz...

history.name: Chicken Salad Chick
history.snippet: Yum! I have been here a few times now. I have tried a few of the different chicken salads and the pimento cheese. Everything has been super good. The staff here is very friendly. We were greeted immediately when walking in on our first visit and given samples. Also very convenie...

history.name: Big Storm Brewery- Clearwater
history.snippet: Came here on a Friday night. My fiancé and I both ordered a flight for ourselves. I ordered a sour flight. We also got a couple things off the menu ( there is a food cart outside that offers food which is handy). They were good overall. The tuna poke wasn't really poke but it wa...

history.name: Newk's Eatery
history.snippet: I love the ultimate salad from here. I will get it once a week for lunch. I especially appreciate their ordering online/app capabilities. I can add and remove any item and their speed it very quick. My go to lunch place when I am not sure what I want.

history.name: Cueni Brewing
history.snippet: Came here a couple months ago with a couple people. I love the location and it being right on the trail. It has a really nice relaxing outdoor setting. I had a tart cherry type beer and it was pretty good. I would definitely come back.

history.name: Barley Mow Brewing Company
history.snippet: I have been to Barley mow a few times. They have a great building and setting in an old pub building. Great place for meeting up with people. Each time I have come there has been a beer I enjoyed. I typically like more fruit and sour beers and some places don't offer those. They...

history.name: Clear Sky Draught Haus
history.snippet: I can always find something I want with their menu. Very diverse and tons of options! I have been here many many times. They have a great beer selection with a lot of local selections as well. I am also super happy that they are on uber eats. Only draw back is that they are alwa...

history.name: Nature's Food Patch Market & Café
history.snippet: Love natures food patch. I live really close to this place and go here often. My favorites are the juice bar, the veggie and fruit selection and the prepackaged foods that you can grab and go. A lot of good selections and reasonable prices for most everything. Has a lot of healt...

history.name: Los Mayas Mexican
history.snippet: Mediocre Mexican food. Chips and salsa are not bad. Everything else is just ok. Kind of bland but cheap. I do really enjoy the taco salad here. The cheese they use on it is goat cheese and it is a nice taste to add when everything else is a bit lacking.

history.name: Bros Pizzeria
history.snippet: My favorite pizza in the area. I have been going here for years and it is consistently great. I like the white pizza and the margarita pizza the best. So yummy. I judge all other pizzas against it and it's not often they match up.

history.name: Poke' Cafe'
history.snippet: I've been here a couple times now. The first time was a few months ago and the last time was just a couple nights ago. This past time I was way impressed by the service and the food. I really love poke, ceviche, and sushi so I'm always up for one of those and this poke totally h...

history.name: Lucky Lobster
history.snippet: My fiancé and I came here on a Friday recently. Great location and we were able to walk next door to cueni brewery after. We started with a couple drinks and they had a good selection of beers to choose from. We got an appetizer that was one of the specials, a seated tuna with f...

history.name: Hog Island Fish Camp
history.snippet: I have eaten here a few times now. I had a grouper sandwich the first time and it was great. very tasty. Appetizers ate also great. I definitely enjoy the atmosphere and the different tables I have sat at. The first was outside and i enjoyed the setting there. Inside it was also...

history.name: Happy Fish
history.snippet: I love this place! I have been here 3-4 times now. I live in Clearwater and would gladly make the drive to eat here. I am a big ceviche fan and the Peruvian style ceviche is amazing. I love the spiciness and variety of corn and sweet potatoes you get with it. The ceviche itself...

history.name: Sea Sea Riders Restaurant
history.snippet: Came in here for dinner last night. We got seated and it took like 5 min for them to come take the drink order. We ordered a couple waters and two beers and the avocado fries. It took about 5-10 min for the waitress to come back with the water and beer. I asked for a lemon with...

history.name: Soggy Bottom Brewing
history.snippet: Always happy to try a new brewery in the area. I had the blackberry ale and my fiance had the pale ale. I wasn't a fan of the one he got but enjoyed the blackberry one. They had a lot of beers on tap and I appreciated all the options. Even some wine. Will come back and try their...

history.name: Crooked Thumb Brewery
history.snippet: I've been here a couple times. Great hidden location and building. It has a nice layout inside and plenty of room outside. They even have a little nook with a TV. I appreciated that they had a few different options for gose and other sour beers. I really enjoyed the two I got. W...

history.name: Poochie's Woof & Wine
history.snippet: My friend and I were looking for a nice place to grab a couple glasses of wine and unwind last night. I found this place on yelp and realized that I have driven by here many times before going to other places in Dunedin. I got there and was warmly greeted. We were able to sit ou...

history.name: One Family Korean Restaurant
history.snippet: Came here with my fiance this past weekend. Was really excited to try this place after reading all the reviews. We found this place relatively easy after reading the other reviews as it is a bit hidden in the complex it is in. We were warmly greeted and sat down. The place is co...

history.name: Old Farmer's Creamery
history.snippet: I came here this weekend with my family. We each got different flavors and there were a lot of options to choose from. I settled on cookies and cream. It was very creamy and good. It is cash only though and the location is a bit rundown inside. It also felt a bit confusing as pe...

history.name: Red Mesa Restaurant
history.snippet: My family and I went here on Saturday. We got there early, around 5:15 and waited for a table for 5 for about 20 minutes. We were sat and the service was great. We tried a couple different sangria's. The strawberry one was very good and had muddle mint and strawberries in it. Th...

history.name: 1933 Pub & Grill
history.snippet: I have visited this place in the past and recently ordered takeout via Uber eats. Each time I have come I have gotten a burger. They have very good burgers and plenty of options. I enjoyed the fries and the cobb salad I also got. Only complaint was that my burger was overcooked...

history.name: Pico Rojo
history.snippet: Came here for dinner last week. Very quiet and there were plenty of tables open even though it isn't the biggest space. I ordered a quarter dark chicken with fries and fried cheese. So yummy! The sauces are great, one is a spicy Mayo based one and the other is a jalapeno one. Th...

history.name: Cafe 405 South
history.snippet: Came in here the other day after being in the complex to get a pedicure at aquastone. The staff and owner were very friendly. The Krio cap was recommended and I opted for almond milk instead of the regular milk and they told me it was less frothy due to that. I didn't mind! Was...

history.name: Buya
history.snippet: I came here a few weeks back for lunch on a Sunday. Very cool with murals in the wall and trendy interior. I had the mushroom ramen with side of seaweed salad. Was very good. My fiancé got the duck ramen with a tuna cold noodle salad. Was also very good. The service was good and...

history.name: Clear Sky on Cleveland
history.snippet: I live near downtown Clearwater and I had no idea this was here until I looked at yelp for ideas on where to eat one night. The location and space is great. It was very busy but we were sat right away still. We started with the octopus which was amazing!! Not rubbery at all and...

history.name: The Restorative
history.snippet: Came here on a weeknight last week. Unassuming location but fixed up very nice inside. We started with a smaller plate for an appetizer - lamb meatballs. They were great. and then had a couple larger plates. We ended up getting another appetizer- watermelon gazpacho with shrimp....

user_persona: Nashville visitor who values clear parking guidance, friendly staff, sushi or Thai food, relaxed wine tasting, dog-friendly energy, and social atmosphere.

history.name: The Stillery, Midtown
history.snippet: Food was fine but no ones warns you about the parking and paying. My car and 2 others got fined $95. It totally ruined our meal.

history.name: The Eastern Peak - North Gulch
history.snippet: Good food. Menu is too extensive. Hard to pick what works. Sushi was delicious. Staff was very nice

history.name: Natchez Hills Winery at the Market
history.snippet: So friendly and so nice. The wine is wonderful. The company is even better. People are so friendly and kind. I love seeing all the dogs and making friends with everyone. Kendra is fantastic. She makes our time here fun. If we lived in the area we'd come all the time.

user_persona: New Orleans nightlife fan who likes cheap dive bars, country jukeboxes, memorable bartenders, easy conversation, and friendly knowledgeable service.

history.name: Brothers III Lounge
history.snippet: My absolute favorite bar in New Orleans, which says a lot. Cheap drinks, great 80's-90's country music on the jukebox and the coolest bartender you'll ever meet - Charlie. Want to get drunk for $20? Go here. Want to have conversations with dudes who used to roadie for Aerosmith...

history.name: SunBodies Tanning Salon
history.snippet: SunBodies is the best tanning salon I have been to. The owner and all the girls working there are so friendly, helpful and knowledgeable of their products. I also had a spray tan here that turned out stunning! Not one hint of orange. Just a beautiful golden brown. I highly recom...

user_persona: Oro Valley customer who expects accurate orders, timely service, warm food, proactive refills, and clear product guidance before committing money.

history.name: Carrabba's Italian Grill
history.snippet: We arrived at 1pm. Food arrived at 2:15pm. We were waiting for my daughter who arrived at 1:30. 2:15 when my food arrived cold ( just me, ordered the salmon ) I had to ask for refills for everyone's drinks and for my food to be reheated .My husband got a filet when he asked for...

history.name: Sears
history.snippet: I bought my dishwasher 5/29/2018 and when the delivery guy came to deliver it he told me that Bosch is slightly taller then regular dishwashers and that it won't fit. I called my sales guy and he said thats not true. The measurements are taller in the specifications and in the s...

user_persona: Franklin diner who likes local bakeries, chicken salad, soup, and breakfast variety, but expects fresh fruit, included coffee, and comfortable seating.

history.name: Merridee's Breadbasket
history.snippet: Favorite place to go in Franklin! Chcicken salad is awesome and the soups are delish!

history.name: Shoney's Restaurant
history.snippet: I went for the breakfast bar and the food was just ok. Good variety but some of the fruit was mushy and the coffee was not part of the deal. The staff was friendly but the waitress sat me in a booth with massive holes in the seating and thought that was ok. Its not too bad of a...

user_persona: Nashville vegan-leaning traveler who likes yoga, healthy wraps, smoothies, inventive margaritas, late-night pizza, sophisticated desserts, walkable spots, and attentive service.

history.name: Empower Yoga
history.snippet: Came here with a girlfriend before dinner and it was wonderful! The customer service is incredible - because the teacher was a little late, we were given a free yoga class card. She even sent us an apology email (which we didn't need - we knew we'd come back!). Cute little studi...

history.name: Farmicia
history.snippet: I wanted to eat everything on the menu!!! Settled for a veggie burger, which was delish. Kinda odd they don't carry ranch dressing. Came here with co-workers after an event and the staff was very pleasant and attentive. In a great central part of Old City too.

history.name: Avo
history.snippet: So so so good! You have to get the avocado margarita. Creamy and smooth and delish . I also ate the walnut burger which was good, and the kimchi wraps were SO good. Cool volleyball available outside too.

history.name: Five Daughters Bakery - East Nashville
history.snippet: I liked the decor more that the doughnuts. Tasted more like cronuts and I went around 12pm and they seemed a little stale. I had the s'mores one. Great atmosphere but donuts so so.

history.name: Hot Yoga Of East Nashville
history.snippet: Cute studio! Came here while in town for the weekend. You can drop in for $15, or, if you think you will come back a second time before you leave, pay $20. Deal! Instructor Rachel is so cute and great! Lotsa sweaty bodies jammed into one room - prepare to be close to your neighb...

history.name: Sprocket Rocket Party Bike
history.snippet: Concept is great but lots of rules, which is understandable, but the group that we were partnered with did not comply with all of them and it made the ride annoying for us. I would not get on if you are wasted or try to get wasted bc you are more likely to break a rule. Our driv...

history.name: Jeni's Splendid Ice Creams
history.snippet: Delicious!!! Get the half and half scoop. I got salty peanut butter and then a coffee flavored one. Very sophisticated flavors.

history.name: The Post East
history.snippet: Great food! Loved the Nutella Fitzgerald latte and the live music playing on a sweet Sunday morning. Service was a little slow. Also yummy egg and cheese sandwich. Cool atmosphere and easy to walk to if you're in East Nashville.

history.name: Five Points Pizza
history.snippet: Vegan pizza is AMAZING. Super busy place bc it's so popular but the walk-in option is nice. I got a slice of Meditteranean to go and it was delish. Then came back at 2:30am (they're open til 3am on weekend!!) for more .

history.name: Lynne Lorraine's
history.snippet: Delicious baked tofu wrap. I also had a Purple Heart smoothie. I like how they let you pick your color straw :). Great service and yummy healthy food options. A casual dining atmosphere so great after a yoga class or on-the-go.

user_persona: New Orleans nightlife visitor who prioritizes live music, Cajun fine dining, prompt service, clean venues, strong pours, and memorable bar atmosphere.

history.name: The Maison
history.snippet: If you don't mind the cockroaches crawling on the wall, under poured drinks and subpar service, the music is awesome. I stayed for the music.

history.name: Emeril's
history.snippet: Wow!! What a great experience. Promptly seated, great service, fabulous food. My husband did the tasting- nothing to write home about. But the menu --- the menu!! We had beef tartare- fabulous! Andouille sausages- butter! And I had the pork chop entree- WOW!!!!! Shout out to Kei...

history.name: The Carousel Bar & Lounge
history.snippet: Cool bar, nice bartenders, slow service. Sit in the lounge near the stage and watch the bands play. 3 stars for the slow service. Staying in the hotel, I would have preferred prompter service and follow through. Other than that, great place.

user_persona: Santa Barbara diner who values beautiful settings, polished seafood, strong wine lists, fresh salads, high-quality Mexican food, and consistently edible dishes.

history.name: Arts & Letters Cafe
history.snippet: One of the prettiest lunch spots in Santa Barbara-- an courtyard with palms, banana trees and stone fountain with flowers all around. Visually you could be a the Alhambra with a touch of Bali and Santa Barbara. Excellent menu, great salads and delightful service. You enter throu...

history.name: Arch Rock
history.snippet: First rate chef, service and wine list. Excellent sauce on the cioppini---one the best I've ever tasted.( It might be a bit too spicy/hot for some people, but I thought it was perfection.) Miso Cod on bok choy delighted my 85 year old mom and the sea bass was perfection. Clearly...

history.name: Beachside Bar-Cafe
history.snippet: Took my 86 year mother, wife and brother to lunch on Sunday. Great setting, great atmosphere etc. Our third visit. This should be a GREAT dining experience, but it was ruined by two out of four dishes being inedible. We ordered a cobb salad and a seafood salad ( about $30 ) --th...

history.name: Los Agaves
history.snippet: This is a great, casual place. Wonderful ambiance and the food is indeed high quality, and not your usual gringo mexican place.

user_persona: New Orleans visitor who seeks lively local spots, Cajun seafood, oysters, street-facing vibes, nice servers, short waits, and authentic city experiences.

history.name: Nice Guys Bar and Grill
history.snippet: Saw this place on TikTok and had to give them a try since it's black owned, and we were visiting the city and wanted to try all that NOLA had to offer. We parked right on the street, and we didn't have to wait long in line. The waiters were very nice, and they checked our vaccin...

history.name: Pier 424 Seafood Market
history.snippet: This is my favorite spot to eat! We went twice in one weekend. I really enjoyed the oysters and the vibes. Sat right in front of the window facing the street. Our waiters were nice. Also no one asked for our vaccination cards or Covid test. We had them on us. But overall this is...

user_persona: Mount Holly diner who likes family-friendly Italian, local brews, pub food, strong bagels, breakfast sandwiches, and inclusive service without regular-only barriers.

history.name: The Local Eatery & Pub
history.snippet: Great food and drinks, but any time that we go there is an excuse for why the whiskey room isn't open. One time when we tried to go, they said that "you can't go in, that's where the regulars hang out".

history.name: Russo's Pub & Italian Restaurant
history.snippet: This restaurant is a staple for my family and I. We go here for any occasion, from regular nights, to party nights, and formal events. Not only is the food amazing, but the customer service is delightful. And finally, they have local brews available. This is a wonderful restaura...

history.name: The Local Eatery & Pub
history.snippet: Great food and drinks, but any time that we go there is an excuse for why the whiskey room isn't open. One time when we tried to go, they said that "you can't go in, that's where the regulars c hang out".

history.name: Wicked Bagelz
history.snippet: Awesome bagels! I got an everything and it was loaded up with spices. My companions got the breakfast sandwiches and they were amazing. The coffee variety was great and the cream cheese selection was big. Only bad thing I have to say is that the cashier was more focused on chatt...

user_persona: New Orleans visitor who wants fresh casual classics, burgers, beignets, jazz atmosphere, distinctive cocktails, fair prices, and avoids stale tourist-trap food.

history.name: Tony Moran's Restaurant
history.snippet: Of the several places we ate at during our week in New Orleans, this was the most disappointing. The appetizer bread rolls were very dry, obviously not prepared from scratch. My iced tea was murky, with that off flavor of tea that's a day old (how do you mess up tea?). I asked f...

history.name: Yo Mama's Bar & Grill
history.snippet: We ate here for lunch on a Saturday. My coworker and I both ordered a hamburger with (loaded) baked potato. An interesting aspect of this is the butter on the potato melts, coating the bun. Though the burger was cooked more than I'd asked for (medium instead of medium-rare), the...

history.name: Old Absinthe House
history.snippet: We visited twice to sample different kinds of absinthe. There are 100, 106 and 136 proof varieties, we tried the 100 and 136. Both are prepared the French Traditional way, except the sugar cube is ignited for show. (It's doused before being put in the cup). Cost for the 100-proo...

history.name: Cafe Beignet on Bourbon Street
history.snippet: Like many others, we visited after dinner for some Beignets. They're pretty cheap - like less than $3 for three - and prepared fresh. While we were waiting, we enjoyed the jazz band that was playing that night. (Having walked past "Edison Park" several times, there's always a ba...

user_persona: King of Prussia diner who wants fresh hot burgers, fair pricing, accurate waits, reliable delivery, and attentive service without mediocre sports-bar food.
context: restaurants in King of Prussia, PA

history.name: Champps
history.snippet: I think it's time for this place to close. It used to be a decent sports bar for a beer and quick bite, but we quit going about three years ago because of the mediocre food and high prices. Tonight we decided to try them again and it was a mistake. The waiter was very attentive...

history.name: Shake Shack
history.snippet: It's Shake Shack! As always, great burgers and fries served fresh and hot.

history.name: Seasons Pizza
history.snippet: The pizza is generally good, but their delivery system is very hit or miss. Lately, it's been more of a miss. The last two orders took over an hour and arrived cold. Tonight, I ordered ahead an hour and half, and at the designated delivery time I received a call indicating the o...

user_persona: Reno traveler who likes quick friendly breakfast stops, reasonable prices, hidden Greek finds, and carefully prepared dishes without spoiled ingredients.

history.name: Siam Elephant
history.snippet: I would have given this place a 4, as the food was decent and the prices reasonable. However, they lost a star when I ordered the mango and sticky rice. To the horrible dismay of my demanding tastebuds a malignant taste and texture was encountered. I flipped over the mango, only...

history.name: IHOP
history.snippet: We pulled over at this IHOP on our way home from Reno. For an IHOP, it was good. we were worried that because it was sunday morning, the wait would be long but we arrived at 9:45 ish and we were seated right away. The parking is ample, the service was quick and friendly. If its...

history.name: Nikos Greek Kitchen
history.snippet: we found this place based on the yelp reviews. so thank you yelpers. its like this tiny whole in the wall restaurant inside a food court/market place type of establishment so at first it took a second glance to find. we only realized that because of one of the pictures another y...

user_persona: Tampa Asian-food seeker who prioritizes authentic dim sum, Chinese seafood, Korean grill, generous side dishes, and flavor over polished decor.

history.name: China Yuan Restaurant
history.snippet: This place is a bit run down, but def. better and more authentic than Yummy House. I got 6 different kinda dum sum, they were all pretty good, except the deep fried taro had green onion in it which is a bit strange.

history.name: Yummy House China Bistro
history.snippet: Dim sum was pretty good. Their dishes could be a little more authentic tho.

history.name: Rice Market and Restaurant
history.snippet: I think this is the only place that has korean grill in Tamap. The ribs were really good. They give you lots of side dishes; however, I couldn't really find one that I really liked (I usually find a few that I like in Korean restaurants).

user_persona: Indianapolis local explorer who likes craft coffee, inventive burgers, breweries, tacos, vegetarian options, friendly service, community events, and cozy creative spaces.

history.name: The Cake Bake Shop by Gwendolyn Rogers-Broad Ripple Village
history.snippet: It's like walking into Breakfast at Tiffany's; the shop is absolutely gorgeous. You get a sugar high the minute you walk in the door. That being said, I will do a follow up review. As for now 2 stars is all I can give. I walked in and wasn't greeted at all. There were several gi...

user_persona: Santa Barbara regular who appreciates dependable seafood, oysters, wine, Mexican food, fair prices, knowledgeable staff, professional service, and businesses that communicate clearly.

history.name: Val's Bakery
history.snippet: The bakery went downhill lately. While most pastries remained of the same quality, some things changed drastically, and not for the best. history.name: Chattime
history.snippet: I like the place. The interior is silly in a way that it is a little night club-ey in the back. But I do enjoy their soups and their sushi, so every time I am invited out to lunch, I suggest this place, and we always go there. Yum!

history.snippet: I love this place! The food is incredibly delicious, I can't think of a single dish that I've had there that I did not absolutely love. history.snippet: We stopped there for lunch yesterday, it was a Wednesday, and the restaurant was pretty empty. There were several waiters working the floor, though, so service was not a problem. history.snippet: I love this place! The prices are below average, the selection of flowers is excellent, the arrangements are lovely! They also deliver within a very reasonable amount of time.

user_persona: Eastampton casual eater who likes huge burgers, crispy wings, family-friendly ice cream, good fast food, packed local grills, and simple fun atmosphere.

I really like the cockaleekee pie, which can best be described as the love child of cream of chicken soup and chicken pot pie. Fish chips are also good. Oh and the hous...

history.name: Forest Park
history.snippet: Oh forest park, i have spent so many hours in you. I live right next to you, so it only makes sense. And how I adore you. Why the four star rating you ask? Two words: water fountains. My first few times at the park, i found out the hard way that working water fountains were few...

history.name: St. Louis Coffee Oasis & Mediterranean Café
history.snippet: I freakin love this place. Funny thing is I have only had one menu item..the gyro. Honestly its the best gyro i have ever eaten. So good. I have to eat it with a fork cause its to fat to eat otherwise. Bonus-it comes with a hot pepper and does not skimp on the tomato or lettuce....

history.name: Pho Grand Restaurant
history.snippet: so i admit, i know nothing of authentic vietnamese food. however, authentic or not...this place is good indeedy! however, i need to branch out and try more dishes as i have been stuck on the bun tom thit nuong, which is noodles-beef--shrimp-vegetables-topped off with lemon grass...

history.name: Imo's Pizza
history.snippet: okay, im giving one star just out of respect for st. louis...cause i love you. but i don't love your pizza. so so wrong. i seriously won't even eat it. and im not a pickey gal. the other star is for the toasted ravs. and salad, which are at least edible.

history.name: Duff's Restaurant
history.snippet: i really enjoy duffs. i have only been here 3 times, but all three times i left happy. the last time i went they had a speical. it was salmon served on crab stuffing thing with a creole sauce. i usually don't order salmon cause i make it at home all the time. but, i went out on...

history.name: Joanie's Pizzeria
history.snippet: I have spent many a hungover morning (acutally afternoon) here. I dig the thin crust...never had the thick. Toasted ravs. are good-but its kinda hard to mess those up. Its pretty laid back, hence why I fruquent this place during hangovers. No complaints.

history.name: The Majestic Restaurant & Bar
history.snippet: This place is pretty average. I used to get the gyros here until I dicovered the coffee shop across the street that has the best gyro I have ever stuffed in my face. The breakfast is alright-nothing special.

history.name: Bissinger's at Maryland Plaza
history.snippet: I was sad as hell when they opened it because the old one in the same neighborhood was quaint and approachable. This place tries to be all cool and hip. Despite this, I still love it. The desserts are fantastic....i have had many of them and always find myself comming back to th...

history.name: The Schlafly Tap Room
history.snippet: Schlafly beer is one of my favorite things about St. Louis. Ihave had alot of microbrews from all over the U.S. and this one can definetly hold it own. I like takin out of towners here, which inevitadbly turns them into schlafly fans. The food is not bad here, but Im more about...

history.name: Companion
history.snippet: Okay so this place would get 5 stars if i was only reviewing the bread...cause its awesome. However, the food here is only okay. I mean if i'm gonna spend $8 on a sandwhich, it better be a damn good sandwhich but it just never has seemed to be. The sandwhiches always seem to be...

history.name: Llywelyn's Pub
history.snippet: This place is just average. THere is one sandwhich that i really love. You can get chicken or steak served on a hogie roll, its covered in cheese sause and topped with fried onions. I really like the chips too. I have been getting this sandwhich for 4 years and it seems like eac...

history.name: El Scorcho
history.snippet: Meh. I could seriously make it better myself. No really, I could. I had chips, salsa and guac. It was good, but its hard to F that up...although sometimes places still manage to. Drinks were good. Had three tacos-steak, chicken, fish. They were okay, but they were SUPER spicy. I...

history.name: Kampai Sushi Bar
history.snippet: This place was just changed over, it used to be Sansui. I think it has new ownership/management. They have redecorated it, but its still recognizable. Just looks a little cleaner and fresher. There is also a new sushi chef. So now for the sushi. I have just eaten there once sinc...

history.name: Whole Foods Market
history.snippet: there are many great things about this store. the bakery is the shit. produce is fresh and abundant. fish selection is great...however stay away from the sushi. the prepared food selection is flippin delicious. its expensive. some of the products are worth it, but some you can g...

history.name: Kaldi's Coffee Roasting Company
history.snippet: my friend who grew up in clayton introduced me to this place. she informed me this is the only kaldis to frequent. i actually have not been to other locations, just this one. i like the coffee but to be honest...i am not too pickey when it comes to coffee-seriously i will drink...

history.name: Coffee Cartel
history.snippet: i f'n love this place. i come here all the time cause it provides me with my favorite people watching spot in the cwe. me and a friend have actually sat here for hours just people watching and rating how much money it would take for us to sleep with all the guys walking by. seri...

history.name: TFA The Future Antiques
history.snippet: TFA is so kickass. You can spend DAYS in here looking at all the stuff they have. Almost anything you can think of is packed inside this lil place. Last time i was here, I scored a 1950's patten leather clutch for $16. Sweet!

history.name: Meskerem Ethiopian Restaurant
history.snippet: I was really suprised by how much i LOVED this place. My friend suggested it and i was a lil scared. Honestly I was thinking "WTF do they eat in Ethiopia??" They have combo platters, one for meat eaters and one for the veg. heads. Its alot of food for one person, but I could not...

history.name: Big River Running Company
history.snippet: If you are a runner, than you probably already know about this place. If your not a runner, but looking to start running or walking..this is a great place to start. The two guys that own this store are young, but they know thier stuff-I believe they both are fomer pro. runners....

history.name: Cassie's
history.snippet: Wow, im on a five star roll today. This is a great little store. They have all kinds of great gift items. Lots of unique cards, lotions, potions, candles, sprays, jewelry...all kinds of shit. My fellow CWE dwelling friend s and I come in here on many lazy Saturdays just to brose...

history.name: The Royale Food & Spirits
history.snippet: This is one of my favorite bars in STL. If it were in walkin distance from my house, I would probably be here every weekend. I have never eaten the food here, but they make a damn fine (stiff) cocktail. They don't serve AB products here (which some don't like) but I could care l...

history.name: St Louis Union Station
history.snippet: My grandpa, a former railroad engineer, obviously has lots of memories of Union Station. What was once the hub of U.S. railroad transportation, is now a tacky mall. The utter disapointment on my grandparents faces as they walked through this place was really enough to break anyo...

history.name: India Rasoi
history.snippet: holy delishiousness! rasoi is my favortie indian resturant in STL. i have been comming to this place for about 4 years....even when they were in their old diggs on laclede. The new spot is more upscale and modern. but the food has not changed, maybe even gotten better. as other...

history.name: Breadsmith
history.snippet: Despite this fact that this place is a chain...i really love it. I stopped here one morning in need of coffee. This place is right next to a Starbucks, but i just wanted a cheap cup o joe...nothing fancy. In addition to all the breads, they have some great morning pastries. I di...

history.name: The Daily Bread Bakery & Cafe
history.snippet: I went through a time period where i was quite addicted to this place. I work just down the street, which is how i discovered it. I really like the salads, which is what i usually order. They are really big and the ingredients are nice and fresh. If you check this place out, be...

history.name: Trader Joe's
history.snippet: I could never live in a town that does not have a trader joes...i love it that much! Not that anyone cares but these are the products I buy from there: cheese-great selection. if your having a cheese board, you can pick up small amounts of different kinds. wine-CHEAP! always hav...

history.name: Oberweis Ice Cream and Dairy Store
history.snippet: i had not had oberweis in like a year and shit, i just remember how great this icecream is. the plain chocolate might be the best icecream i have ever eaten. i want some NOW! I don't drink milk, but if i had to...i would want to drink it from here.

history.name: Farotto's
history.snippet: good thin crust pizza. you can get motz. cheese instead of provel. yay! I also like the house salad and the toasted ravs. i have been here multiple times with a group and everyone always seems pleased.

history.name: The Boathouse at Forest Park
history.snippet: Atmosphere- great, allowing dogs-great, service-great. However, the food kinda sux. I also did not like how they served my wine in a plastic glass. I hate drinking wine out of a plastic glass!!! I don't give a shit if I am drinking a $6 bottle or a $100 bottle...wine glasses are...

history.name: Pita Plus
history.snippet: The gyro is pretty standard- i prefer Med. Oasis in the CWE for a gyro. Probably the best place to get a falafel in STL. Hummus is good and i really love the pitas here. I bought some to go last time i was here. I like how they have the sauces in bottles for you, so you can add...

history.name: Terrene
history.snippet: I have only been here twice, but i really enjoyed it. Not sure why i have not been back. Crazy as it sounds, the fries were super good. They came w/ 2 house made sauces that were delish! And Tofu on a stick was enjoyable. My friend was less impressed w/ her entree, which was the...

history.name: Racanelli's
history.snippet: Im no pyschologist, but Racanelli's is totally bipolar. Seems like in the past few years this place has been more depressive, than manic. When i first moved here (4 years ago), i really enjoyed it. However, the past few times I have eaten it...i have been far less impressed. And...

history.name: Straub's
history.snippet: Yes, this place is expensive. Some of the prices are borderline ridiculous. Example-$8 for a box of Tampax....im not even joking. So, I NEVER come here for the basics. However, there are many things that I like about this store. 1. The meat Okay, so i don't eat meat any more but...

history.name: Serendipity Homemade Ice Cream
history.snippet: icecream is probably my favoirte food ever. so, i am a little bias when it comes to reviews of icecream places. it is gonna be hard for me to not like it. last time i was here, the chick working had a very unfriendly attitude. now, i don't excpet everyone to be super cheery and...

history.name: Ted Drewes Custard
history.snippet: dear ted, i thought you should know that your delicious custard made me gain 10lbs. when i moved to STL. seriously, its a your fault. your little custard stand was right by my work and always conveniently open when my shift ended. about 4 times a week, i stopped in for a concret...

history.name: Culpeppers Grill and Bar
history.snippet: yeah, like others have said....this place is greasetastic. i have nursed many a hangover here. great spot for people watching. if the weather is not nice, be sure to grab a seat near the windows.

history.name: Bar Louie
history.snippet: this place has pretty decent bar food. seems like each time i have been here, everyone leaves happy. but be sure to get the tater tots w/ a side of garlic ranch for dipping. i know you can easily make tater tots at home, but something about theirs are super delish.

history.name: St. Louis Coffee Oasis & Mediterranean Café
history.snippet: So, i had to downgrade from a 5 star...to a 4 star. I recently tried the falafel, as I don't eat meat anymore. I had high hopes, seeing as this was the home of the best gyro I have ever eaten. I was so dissapointed. NOT GOOD! :(

history.name: Saigon Cafe
history.snippet: I am in agreement with everyone, this place is fantastic. Yes, its a little more pricey that other Vietnamese joints in STL but you pay for what you get. Went last night a friend and I split the veggie spring rolls. The sauce that came with them so good-i would eat toenails if t...

history.name: Jilly's Cupcake Bar & Café
history.snippet: i am a dedicated fan of the cupcakery in the cwe....i felt like i was cheating on them by comming here. i actually wanted to hate this place. that being said...... the cupcakes were good. they are over the top huge and piled high with sweet goodies. i could see why people would...

history.snippet: Had the best dinner I've had in awhile last night. I can't believe I have waited this long to try Aya Sofia. history.name: Saffron
history.snippet: Came here with a co-worker to celebrate my bday...what better way to celebrate than stuffing your face with copius amounts of Indian food?? We had the lunch buffet and it was really good. I agree with mike l...STL has alot of great Indian choices so this is just another one to a...

history.name: Joanie's Pizzeria
history.snippet: Thanks you Joanie's for a vegetarian toasted ravioli!!!!! I've been missing these fried pieces of heaven ever since I went veg.

history.name: Duff's Restaurant
history.snippet: Had an awesome brunch here yesterday morning. I took a group of out of towners here to get a good base before Mardi Gras. There is nothing worse than taking your guests to a place you say is really good, then it sucks. Luckily, that was not the case at Duffs. Everyone really enj...

history.name: SweetArt
history.snippet: I really like Sweet Art. Went today and it was packed! The cupcakes were good, but the cookies really stood out. How can a cookie be you ask? My definition of the best cookie is crunchy on the edges and soft in the middle. I had a chocolate chip and a sunshine cookie -lemon w/ w...
