history.snippet: I had no idea what OR when I saw they were opening right near my house. I assumed it was another one of those chain smoothie places. I was delighted to find out that they are locally owned AND use organic ingredients! AND on top of that wonderfulness....they have A LOT of vegeta...

history.snippet: Hot, soft and delicious. These were all the adjectives I was expecting to use describing my first experience at Gus's. However, my experience can really be summed up as FAIL. The pretzel was not really bad, it just was not that good; nor was it hot, soft and delicious. It was co...

history.name: Anthonino's Taverna
history.snippet: I have to admit, I don't know if I ever would have tried this place if it were not for two things 1. Yelp and 2. iPhone. I noticed the good reviews and made a mental log that I wanted to try Anthoninos. One night while driving around a few friends and I wanted to eat, I thought...

history.name: SweetArt
history.snippet: I finally was able to try to the lunch items at Sweet Art. Amazing. The vegan chili was really spicy and tasty. It had sweet potatoes in it, which i thought was a nice addition. Like others have said the star of this show was the quiche. I am not much of a quiche eater, or egg e...

history.name: Venice Cafe
history.snippet: wow, this place is rad...and unique! Where else can you play, "i spy with my little eye" and have such an easy time stumping your opponents? I think my friend is still at Venice Cafe trying to find He-Man! hahaha!

history.name: Katie's Pizza
history.snippet: Nice take on toasted ravioli, not the same old that your used to getting. Its filled with artichoke and gorgonzola and served with pesto and cherry tomato on the side which provides a nice balance. The last time I went they had a pumpkin pizza. I almost reneged on my initial dec...

history.name: St. Louis Spinning & Yoga
history.snippet: After a very long hiatus from spinning, I was just reminded why I love this place. Spin class at St. Louis' Original Spinning are my favorite winter indoor workout. I HATE treadmills. In fact, I almost always prefer to workout outside. However, when its below freezing I let moth...

history.name: Pi Pizzeria - Central West End
history.snippet: I can't even begin to express how excited I am to have Pi in my neighborhood!!! I have already been three times. Welcome to the hood, Pi..you and I are going to be great friends! On my very very first trip to the Pi on Delmar, I will admit...it was not what I was expecting. I wa...

history.name: Eau Bistro
history.snippet: I have only eaten the Sunday brunch at Eau Bistro....and I am a fan indeed! The price is a bit high, but for me it the $40 is worth it. I like to go and spend three hours taking my time and taking advantage of the all you can drink bloody marys and mimosas. The first time i went...

history.snippet: This is by far my favorite run in STL. I have done this race for the past 3 years and it is a blast! There are always a lot of people at all skill levels so if you can walk 5 miles, you can participate. This race is very entertaining. There are kids, adults, people in funny cost...

history.snippet: Okay, time for an update. I avoided this place for a year due to a horrid visit, but I decided to give them another go. And, my experiences have gotten better. The service has been much improved, said waitress (jn previous review) has not been around the past few times..so that...

history.snippet: This is where the Ladue house wife's past season clothing and accessories come to die...how do i know? because i am carrying her purse. Actually they have amazing variety and on any given day you really have no idea what you will find. Last time i was in I scored the most old la...

history.snippet: For the past 3 years injury or sickness made my races quite miserable but i still had a great time! I had the green curry with chicken and instead of actual chili peppers as I'm accustomed too with real authentic Thai restaurants they just added chili flakes for heat. If you go, you'll see there really isn't anything special. The best tasting things are the salads and the gluten fre...

I was in so much pain I couldn't eat dinner later or breakfast and lunch the next day. And I was up all nigh...

history.name: The Hungry Greek
history.snippet: I was really impressed with this fast casual restaurant. My chicken souvlaki was delicious and the tzatziki sauce was yummy. It's a great place to get decent food at a decent price and there are many gluten free options. They're a little cheap with their tzatziki sauce and it's...

history.snippet: I'm so disappointed in this place. I have never tasted a better GF DF blueberry crumb muffin than here but I'm not going to lie their cinnamon buns and donuts were tough and disgusting. I didn't even try their bread as I heard it's tough. I'm from NY and my mother bakes GF so it...

user_persona: Morrisville diner who loves authentic Italian, polished bar nights, catered-event quality, chic breweries, friendly clean breakfasts, and consistently prepared local meals.

history.name: Non Solo Pasta
history.snippet: This place is phenomenal! True authentic Italian that is consistent. Love Non Solo Pasta and highly recommend it to everyone!

history.name: Michael's Restaurant & Lounge
history.snippet: This place is fantastic whether you're looking for a drink at the bar, an incredible meal perfectly prepared table side by Michael himself or a Saturday night out on the dance floor, Michael's is your place!! I've also been to many catered events (weddings, showers, birthday par...

history.name: Vault Brewing
history.snippet: After about a year or so people talking about this place I decided to give it a try. Stopped in for lunch and upon entering you are automatically at ease in this chic yet sophisticated atmosphere. It's cozy yet funky. I love that you can see the "behind the scenes" there is an o...

history.name: Radcliffe Cafe
history.snippet: Friendly staff, clean establishment, great breakfast food!! Walk in, have a seat and enjoy!! I have yet to try anything other than breakfast but I'm sure it's just as delicious!!

user_persona: Saint Petersburg vegetarian diner who loves friendly cafes, smoothies, acai bowls, Ethiopian vegan food, Brazilian breakfast, specialty coffee, and fast service.

history.name: Karma Juice Bar & Eatery - Saint Petersburg
history.snippet: The food is amazing, staff is so friendly. Had both cinner smoothie and love bowl, had the chili a different occasion. In love.

history.name: Queen of Sheba
history.snippet: Was in love with the Sarasota site before it closed. Finally made my way to Tampa. The food is so good that I cried a little. It somehow transformed the day of a shitty breakup to the best day ever. 5 stars.

history.name: Maria Banana Cafe
history.snippet: Food was amazing, specialty coffee was amazing, everyone was incredibly sweet and helpful, and FAST!

user_persona: Franklin local who likes summer decks, live music, friendly remembered service, spirited casual spots, chicken tenders, and upbeat neighborhood restaurants.

history.name: The Bunganut Pig Bar & Restaurant
history.snippet: I love the Pig (As we call it) in the summer time! I love to sit on the deck and listen to live music! The food is great and after one visit the staff knew us and remembered us on our second visit!

history.name: McDougal's Chicken Fingers & Wings
history.snippet: My first trip tp McDougals was a sucess, I love the attitude and spirit inside and the staff are very friendly! I loved my chicken tenders, I ate everyone and then took time to breathe. YUMMMEEE!!!

history.name: Emmaline
history.snippet: I love Emmalines! It is beautiful and they have MUCH more selection of designer clothes than any of the other local boutiques!

user_persona: Reno traveler who wants vegan-friendly late-night meals, prompt service, free parking, family activities, reliable fast food, and hot correct orders.

history.name: Johnny Rockets
history.snippet: We stopped at the hotel while passing through and needed a late night meal due to the time. Fortunately JR was able to create us a vegan burger that was mighty tasty. We live no where near another JR so this was a treat that we won't get to enjoy again for a while. The team was...

history.name: House of Mexica
history.snippet: Came for dinner while traveling through. We are glad we stopped in. We were greeted and sat right away. Service was prompt and friendly. Food was outstanding. We will be back for breakfast and when we visit Reno again. There is plenty of free parking in their shared lot. Highly...

history.name: Need 2 Speed
history.snippet: Visiting Reno for the night and found this place. The 3 teenage boys loved it. They each had a couple races then we all played a round of golf. history.name: In-N-Out Burger
history.snippet: Decent food as expected for In-n-Out. The server cashier was a little confused but after 3 tries the order was completed. We still ended up with 2 messed up tickets. The order came out correct and hot. We came in at the right time and it wasn't too packed.

user_persona: Wilmington diner who loves authentic Italian food, attentive low-key service, welcoming bars, and rejects restaurants that ignore poor service feedback.

history.name: Mona Lisa
history.snippet: My absolute favorite restaurant in 302! The service is just outstanding and very low key yet always attentive. Claire is just so welcoming and Phillip at the bar works wonders. history.name: Rocco Italian Grill & Sports Bar

user_persona: East Nashville regular who likes standout brunch, smoky barbecue, oysters, patio seafood, happy-hour bars, beer, music venues, and polished date-night dining.

history.snippet: Completely agree with Matt L's assessment. Not quite a coffee shop, not quite a bakery. Went there today for lunch. Service was extremely friendly and equally slow. There were three of us and all three orders were mixed up. $10 for coffee and a sandwich with a small side salad i...

history.name: Mad Donna's
history.snippet: I keep trying to like Mad Donnas but this past Sunday was it for me. Went for the Bloody Mary bar & brunch. They start you off with a half-glass of vodka and let you make your own mix from the bar. The limes were literally spoiled. The celery was limp. There were no spoons to st...

history.name: Marché Artisan Foods
history.snippet: Took our family over for brunch this weekend and had yet another fabulous experience. The food is fantastic. The saki bloody marys are especially unique and flavorful and the overall aesthetic is outstanding. A real gem on the East side.

history.name: Tom's Elite Carryout
history.snippet: Best ribs. Best smoked chicken. Best prices. Best BBQ this side of Memphis, hands-down. As the name implies, it's more of a take-out joint than a place you would dine-in, but I would drive all the way across town for Tom's Elite. I'm lucky enough to be able to walk. Yea, I know....

history.name: Drifters
history.snippet: Very friendly staff. Greasy, under-seasoned BBQ. A little pricey considering the menu. Good chicken tacos (just added). A perfect 3-star experience. Check out Tom's Elite for the real deal.

history.name: Batter'd & Fried Boston Seafood House
history.snippet: FANTASTIC oysters. Very friendly staff. And if you're in to things that are... well, battered and fried, you'll be in heaven. Not the cheapest date in town but if the weather is nice and you can score a table on the patio, it's worth every penny.

history.name: Beyond The Edge
history.snippet: Lots of 2-for-1 drink specials throughout the week. Very fair prices in general. Good selection of "bar food." Not nearly as smokey as 3 Crow or Red Door East, the other hot spots for a brew at 5 Points. A great happy hour hang.

history.name: Watanabe
history.snippet: Great decor. We were really excited when they opened. Unfortunately, it's been all down-hill from there. The miso is near flavorless. The sushi is mostly fried w/ cream cheese filling. The service has never been good. At least not in the 10 times I've been there. Not a place I w...

history.name: The Family Wash
history.snippet: The Family Wash has legendary shepherd's pie. Pie and pint night (Tuesdays) is a great deal at $10. Fabulous roast chicken. Great selection of beers. Always a touch understaffed it seems, but it's a one-of-a-kind place that we are very proud to visit as often as our weight-loss...

history.name: Margot Cafe & Bar
history.snippet: Fine dining in East Nashville, who would have thought? OUTSTANDING menu, great service, the best mixed drinks in town (try their bleu cheese-stuffed olives/dirty martinis. SHEESH.) history.name: Urban Greek

The calamari was great. spanikopita and hummous just low average/bland wouldnt reorder that....and I love my hummous. Service: the cashier girl was interjecting and giving scattered advice about o...

history.name: Jimmy The Greek
history.snippet: Had spanikopita meal it was good, rice ok big portions. Service: workers looked harassed with no one harassing, not the friendliest as they looked like you bother them. My friend happy with his meal but i personally would not reorder.

history.name: Simit House
history.snippet: Hummous was ok nothing above average, I had some baked bread thing with feta with other veg filling a kind of fresh soft iraqi bread was really good first time having it decent size for one person and the pricing on the baked goods are reasonable. Would stop by if in the area.

history.name: LUX Steakhouse and Bar
history.snippet: ordered 2 for 1 appy hour of lobster macncheese and open face steak bites. Steak very good and would go for steaks here if I had the $$$$$$, the pasta not so much a fan really overdid it on garlic in the thick/or is it a little too little sauce and the lobster was nothing specia...

history.name: Crown & Anchor Pub & Grill
history.snippet: Wings are good decent price and many flavors. Pizza sub is ok Bathroom is at times under serviced. Did not like other food I have tried here besides fries, and got sick once after eating mushroom caps. They have pretty bad karoake and some of the regulars are annoying. I have en...

history.name: Laziza
history.snippet: I only tried hummous and pita, hummous was ok, pita: they gave me a lot of but they were prepackaged plain and a bit dry/old. The pictures on the menu did not look that appetizing and service was a bit frigid and took awhile to get me the hummous to go.

history.name: Donair Way
history.snippet: You really would walk past this place and not give it a second thought ever again unless you love hummous as much as me or had a bad craving of poutine as a local. So that happened and the hummous was a bit better than average the bread only got once peice and was bit stale for...

history.name: Chicago Deep Dish Pizza
history.snippet: Pizza was ok crust not my favorite ok A bit dry and service was less than thrilled seating just a bar stool area and small place. Wouldnt go back personally

history.name: The Burger's Priest
history.snippet: Fries ok, ordered delivery and they sent me a different drink than I ordered oh well. Burgers average to a bit better than but very pricy value/point Update second order of cheeseburger was delicious, bump up a star

history.name: Sbarro
history.snippet: Pizza looks old When I realized one slice was $5 and would need 2 we decided to spend money elsewhere. The guy behind the counter just looked at us like wtf like we were dumb asf. I have eaten sbarro across n america and this is a lackluster location.

history.name: Panini's Italian Cucina
history.snippet: Ricotta proscuitto pizza was very good and so was the pesto gnocchi We ordered pizza since and for a change. Would reorder again delivery

history.name: Vera's Burger Shack
history.snippet: Meat is good the rest of the same as the chain but the chef/cashier burnt the customer before me's burger and slightly undercooked mine which I ate. At a different location I also had the problem of worse undercooked burger so I shrugged it off. The burgers are $13-15/min which...

history.name: La Poutine
history.snippet: Fries are skinny and underdone. We ordered delivery also soggy almost to be expected. Too skimpy on the cheese curds and they had very tasty gravy, but not a fan of the fries or value.

history.name: Blowers & Grafton
history.snippet: The grunt donuts are to die for. Great poutine: crispy fries savory gravy not too much herbs and a little shy on the curds, worth the $3 upgrade with philly cheese sandwich. Took a bit to get our food order. Okay service. The sandwich was probably the least favorite thing as the...

history.name: Sushi Shop Kingsway Garden Mall
history.snippet: First time I might have rated 4 stars. Service slow both times and not busy with customers. Supposed to get free ice cream with menu item that did not happen did not ask me if I wanted to buy a drink either just rang me up the second time. Good rolls and sushi pizza but the rice...

history.name: Daawat Authentic Indian Restaurant
history.snippet: Friendly owners, best Indian food in Edmonton reminds me of my favorite spots in BC. I have tried more than a handful places and have been disappointed until I had this place here in AB. Had butter chicken its moderate in spicyness, naan, the tandori chicken is pretty spicy but...

history.name: Lan's Asian Grill
history.snippet: I really wanted to like this place more. They are friendly but always changing hours of operation. One day they happened to be open so I went in after work, and will try different menu items to hopefully boost my rating. My issue is the value. I tried the drunken noodle for $16,...

history.name: Paramount Fine Foods
history.snippet: The bbq beef wrap was worth every penny and described as magical @$10.49. The meat was so tender, lean and not dry and could taste the grill! I got the falafal wrap and was one of the better ones I have eaten @ $7 but nothing too special. Portion sizes are small but the good pri...

history.name: Chef House
history.snippet: Prices are decent. Menu is large with various cuisine but I think this is an Indian food place. Of the food we ordered the butter chicken was the best. The coconut rice was fresh is a little too sweet and robust. We got poutine wouldn't order again no curds and the fries were so...

history.name: Fatburger
history.snippet: Not memorable and not bad. Fries were ok and food was filling. If I am in the area and hungry I would order, but for the price prefer so many other places like burger priest and A&w even.

history.name: Thai Express
history.snippet: Was not asked of spice level for basil drunken noodle so it was a bit mild. Good portion size and correct noodles but a lack of basil and other veg. Chicken could have been a bit fresher but wasn't bad. Good value and better than the thai place near my house.

history.name: Khazana Restaurant
history.snippet: If the cut on the butter chicken was smaller I would rate it 4 The cut made it a bit to big and chewy but you could taste the smokyness and grill which is lacking at alot of places. It was a bit small bowl for $20 Decent spice level good sauce. The naan was some of the best I ha...

history.name: Chix Shack
history.snippet: Pad thai was good but a bit on the bland side, not too sweet. The tom kha gai soup had too many onions and not much else in the broth. Both needed more chicken, but ok prices. May reorder.

history.name: Remedy Cafe
history.snippet: The indian food I have tried so far is not so traditional but yummy. The butter chicken wrap I missed the red sauce but they had a yummy white sauce in there I could do more with. Service is friendly, I ordered a chai and got a bunch of spice in my mouth. I got the samosa chai d...

user_persona: Philadelphia office lunch diner who likes quick customizable paninis, inventive sushi, friendly delis, good beer bars, reasonable value, and organized service.

history.name: Casa Bella Trattoria
history.snippet: We went here for my mother's birthday as a chance to try someplace we hadn't been and we are glad we did. My mother can't eat wheat pasta, but they have excellent gluten free pasta and she enjoyed the pasta with broccoli rabe and sausage. The rest of us had veal dishes and they...

history.name: Citi Market place
history.snippet: Come here for lunch from time to time. Always get a great panini. You get to pick what you want meat, cheese, fixings, roll/bread. All for a reasonable price and a pretty quick turn around time. Their other sandwiches and salads that I've had have all been winners. There are oth...

history.name: Grocery
history.snippet: This place is a pretty pricey option for lunch, but always a good one. They have a salad bar, a hot Mexican food bar, a grab and go case and up front case where there are tons of options and for hot or cold lunches or dinners. They happily offer to heat it or will pack it as is....

history.name: Freshii
history.snippet: I know that it is called Chicken Noodle Soup, but really it should be more than a three chunks of chicken, some rice noodles and water. The broth should have some flavor, maybe even a vegtable or two.Not here. I've had other options here before and have been happy with the food,...

history.name: Hokkaido
history.snippet: Stopped in for a work lunch with a friend. The restaurant is clean and nice. The food was good, when we finally got it. It seems like the one waitress working the lunch crowd was overwhelmed. My sushi was good, but my friend ordered the tempura bento box, but got the teriyaki be...

history.name: Fat Salmon
history.snippet: Been here a couple times now. The sushi is fun and inventive. You can stay with the traditional options or venture into their specialties. They have options that will meet the needs of everyone; including those who don't want raw fish or even people who don't like seafood. My fa...

history.name: The Farm and Fisherman Tavern
history.snippet: The menu is limited but does still provide a number of options for patrons. The dinners were well done and had some interesting twists on American classics. The bar had a couple of interesting beers in the mix of standards. The staff was friendly and the decor was rustic and inv...

history.name: Old Nelson Food Company
history.snippet: This is a basic deli place. The sandwiches are good and interesting. The staff is very friendly. But it always seems incredibly disorganized. I rarely can get my sandwich in under a half hour. I have tried calling ahead and still end up waiting.

history.name: El Vez
history.snippet: The place is nice and the food is good, but it never seems worth the price. I've had better Mexican food at a number of places around Philadelphia but this place always seems to get undeserved praise. My friend and I both got the chicken enchiladas. They came out physically hot...

history.name: Han Dynasty
history.snippet: I went here after a movie and had a good time. Went on a Saturday night with no reservations. We had to wait at the bar for a reasonable amount of time (about 15 minutes). The bar had a good selection of beers and friendly bartenders. The bartenders offered us a menu in case we...

user_persona: Nashville diner who likes memorable lunch plates, roasted cauliflower, great guacamole, oyster po'boys, friendly sports bars, and comfortable staff.

history.name: Etch
history.snippet: We ate at Etch three days ago for lunch, and it's still on my mind. I had the pork loin roll up and my wife had the chicken. We also had the roasted cauliflower. Outstanding in all respects. I think I am now a truffle oil junkie.

history.name: Plaza Mexican Bar & Grill
history.snippet: La Plaza is the best Mexican restaurant on the north side. Guacamole is the best I've had in the Nashville area. Salsa is pretty good too. Black beans are available, which is not the case with many Mexican restaurants. HIghly recommended!

history.name: The Original Nooley's
history.snippet: Yesterday's oyster po'boy from Nooley's was so good, I had to have another one today!

history.name: Sam's Sports Grill
history.snippet: I always feel so at home here. Food is a cut above most sports bars. Staff is always friendly and helpful.

user_persona: Philadelphia budget-conscious urban explorer who likes eclectic cozy restaurants, cheap groceries, thrift finds, gelato, vegetarian food trucks, Thai food, wine bars, and danceable lounges.

history.name: Pod
history.snippet: Pod, you get 3 stars because no matter how loaded any given rich person might be, your food is absurdly priced. I like to eat here with my ex so that I can rob him through Starr. Just kidding.... The rock shrimp is a favorite among my pals, and I'm a sucker for the sea bass. Pod...

history.name: Famous 4th Street Delicatessen
history.snippet: I'm not mad at you, Caitlin. 4th Street Deli rightfully gets 3 stars. It's true that this place stuffs their sandwiches with pounds of meat. I just can't bring myself to slam down pastrami like that. I want to live past the age of 40, but hey, some folks don't really care about...

history.name: Astral Plane
history.snippet: This is my favorite restaurant. Ever. I don't know how to articulate the vibe at this place. Eclectic is accurate, but it doesn't suffice. It's warm and cozy; it's a feast for the eyes. My stomach loves this place, too. Last time I was here, my partner and I both had fish dishes...

history.name: Whole Foods Market
history.snippet: This location is superior to the South Street one. Here, it's got the space that it takes to be a super market. Baked goods are delicious for sure. Much more varied and interesting selection of products than the standard Giant/Pathmark/Acme ::shudder:: fare. But let's face it: t...

history.name: Trader Joe's
history.snippet: TJ has my vote and my heart. Where else can you get a week's worth of groceries for $25? While I usually steer clear of the very limited produce dept, you can always find me stocking up on meals in the frozen section. Jon is right on the money--it's the place to be when you're s...

history.name: Buffalo Exchange
history.snippet: I really don't understand the point of Buffalo. Clearly, it's not a thrift store. It's not about offering cheap stuff, nor is it about offering funky stuff. It's about selling clothes that are approximately 6-12 months old. I'm sorry, but I missed the point of buying things from...

history.name: Greene Street
history.snippet: At first I was going to give 5 stars for the awesome clearance section, which has prices reminiscent of Salvation Army's 50 cents tag days. But this stuff isn't 50 cents; it's a few bucks. And when a shirt is $3, that's usually for a reason. Most of the stuff on clearance is jun...

history.name: Toviah Thrift Shop
history.snippet: Disclaimer: Toviah contains a lot of crap that will probably never sell. That said, if you dig through the racks, you can find incredible steals. I'll never forget my first formal dress from Toviah: red satin Jessica McClintock for $7. I can't speak for the men's or children's o...

history.name: Second Mile Center
history.snippet: The Second Mile Center is a series of second hand stores on one block. They sell everything under the sun, and they have the space to do it. I'm sure there's an interesting story here as to how they acquired so many properties, but that's a story I have yet to learn. Today, they...

history.name: The Green Line Cafe
history.snippet: Yep, it's got the neighborhood vibe down, but there's nothing exceptional about the Green Line's products. It's a warm, clean space, which rocks. It's also a bit too busy for me to study there. Some folks like activity and noise in the background while they read or whatever. If...

history.name: Bucks County Coffee Co
history.snippet: This is the Bucks on 40th Street, which is way bigger than the one on 34th. Students adore this location because of the reliable free wireless and the late hours that it keeps. On any given weeknight, most of the seats will be filled with folks poring over books and laptops. Sur...

history.name: Urban Outfitters
history.snippet: Urban Outfitters sells crap. I like their marketing, their downtown property, and their store design. I even like their website. But I would never buy anything from them, especially not the clothing. We all realize that it's low quality, but some of us buy it anyway because we t...

history.name: Capogiro Gelato
history.snippet: It's true; this gelato is positively delicious. I can't actually remember what kinds I tried when I was there last, but I don't think it matters. Get whatever sounds good to you; you won't be disappointed. Chill atmosphere and nice ambiance. You won't mind sitting down for a whi...

history.name: Tequilas Restaurant
history.snippet: Tequila's is certainly pricey, and the bar area was very crowded on the night that I went. But I was only there for a couple minutes; we were there to eat. I was very satisfied with my birthday dinner there. Fairly generous portions and really delicious food. I'd come back if I...

history.name: American Apparel
history.snippet: I don't think the items at AA are priced fairly. That said, their clothing is designed with great cuts and amazing fit--on me, anyway. Something tells me that perhaps AA's merchandise wouldn't flatter non-athletic figures. I especially love the thigh-high socks and their halter...

history.name: Remedy Tea Bar
history.snippet: Remedy's getting 4 *s because I love the chill atmosphere. The decor is cool, and I like the down-tempo music they play. The servers are not exuberant, but they're not mean so overall-pretty whatever. Free wireless is always excellent although the signal is patchy at the smaller...

history.name: The Fresh Grocer of 56th & Chestnut
history.snippet: Though it might be more convenient, the UCity location is like a crippled version of this larger FG edition, which is on 56th Street. I don't know about you, but the cramped aisles and overall limited space at 40th Street make me crazy. It seems like the only non-peak hours for...

history.name: The Fresh Grocer of Walnut
history.snippet: The congestion here is out of this world. 4 out of 5 visits that you make to this location will find either packed aisles or disturbingly long check out lines. Granted, if this store weren't here, where would Penn students buy food? It's better than nothing, so it gets 3 stars,...

history.name: The Franklin Institute Science Museum
history.snippet: Unfortunately, George is exactly right about FI. I have magical memories of this museum, but those impressions do not match those that I have when I walk in as an adult. The permanent exhibits aren't terribly engaging. How could they be? They never change, and since they cater t...

history.name: Smith Memorial Playground & Playhouse
history.snippet: In its pre-renovated state, SMP was a purely magical place. This playground is over 100 years old & it was created by two parents who lost their child. It's actually a mansion + playground, and while the recent renovations are a great development for the park, I really miss the...

history.name: Marathon
history.snippet: When I went to Penn, I ate at this place practically every other week just because it was close by, but let me tell you - the food was a disappointment Each and Every time. It was so bland that I won't set food in any other Marathon ever. This place is really conveniently locate...

history.name: Lorenzo & Sons Pizza
history.snippet: I'm going to jump in the fray to add this: The more sober you are, the less amazing this pizza tastes. It's pretty much just cheese on dough. It's clear from the 20 reviews here that all people want at 3am on a drunken morning is cheese on dough, which is perfect for the people...

history.name: Allegro Pizza
history.snippet: Jake is so on point here. Allegro pizza is so tasty, and it is worth a couple of your hard earned dollars IF you are in the area already. However, the clientele is entirely disturbing, regardless of whether it's students or non-students. The only non-students who come here are w...

history.name: McCrossen's Tavern
history.snippet: McCrossen's is a nice, laid back neighborhood place to grab a drink or a bite when you're in the area. There's a bar side & a restaurant side, but of course you can eat on the bar side as well. It's open relatively late, which makes it really convenient for spontaneous outings a...

history.name: Self Service Storage
history.snippet: If you have items that you can't fit in your place & can put away in storage for extended periods of time, that Might mean you don't actually need those items. If you still think that you do, SSS would be glad to take your money while you store things that... you don't actually...

history.name: Long's Gourmet Chinese Cooking
history.snippet: Long's, you're cool or whatever, but don't lie to the public. You're not gourmet. Long's has been around for years and doesn't seem to be closing up shop anytime soon, which is surprising considering that 9 times out of 10 that you walk by, Long's is open yet completely empty. I...

history.name: Golden Chopstick Chinese Restaurant
history.snippet: Hooray for GC, which looks nicer than your average grimy Chinese take-out joint, but is your average grimy Chinese take-out joint at heart. You can sit inside if you like and take in the ambiance of crazy purple neon lights along the windows. They look radioactive. I think there...

history.name: Theatre of the Living Arts
history.snippet: Caitlin has nailed the age limits on this one. Haven't been back here since high school, but it's a South St staple. I feel the fun vicariously when I walk through the throng of baby goths newly arrived from the malls of NJ. I had the most fun here when with a big gang of friend...

history.name: Mineralistic
history.snippet: I'll never forget Mineralistic, the first store I ever walked into on my first ever visit to South St. It's a feast for the eyes what with wizards and fairies and unicorns glittering all over the shelves. I think that housewares, frou frou decorations, and trinkets are junk, but...

history.name: Salvation Army
history.snippet: This store is the antichrist of shopping. I have come here many, many times, so take it from me. Ever since I was born, SA has been doing the same thing on Market Street: Selling 99.9% crap in a matchbox-sized store with poor lighting. The only difference is that back in the day...

history.name: Jomar - Swanson
history.snippet: Like the Salvation Army on Market Street, Jomar is one big human rights violation. Just kidding. It only makes me cry a little. Jomar is a gigantic warehouse full of clothes, shoes, and accessories that are either overstock from other places or damaged. Their prices really vary...

history.name: Famous Dave's Bar-B-Que
history.snippet: Combine the two reviews before mine and you'll know everything you need to know about FD. The decor is freaky and so is the clientele. The food is cheap and yummy enough for non-BBQ experts. I like the food here, but the location is so out of the way that I never come. It's defi...

history.name: Forman Mills
history.snippet: On one tragic day, I came here in search of fabric thinking it was Jomar. It's not. Like Jomar, FM is a vast warehouse full of clothing and housewares, but FM has no fabric. FM also seems not to specialize in damaged clothing. There's definitely clothing here for the whole famil...

history.name: Payless ShoeSource
history.snippet: This location is right next to Liberty Place. It's a pretty small store. It can get a little too crowded when the 9-5ers go on lunch break. While the 9-5ers are working, though, it's nice and empty. Payless copies the latest trends, whatever they may be. The shoes that they sell...

history.name: Sophisticated Seconds
history.snippet: Women rave about SS for reselling designer duds for cheap. I guess SS's pricing is cheap compared to retail for Chanel, D&G, and whatever else. Here's the thing--designer does not equal attractive. I find a fair proportion of the items here to be icky, but SS doesn't cater to 20...

history.name: The Eyeglass Works
history.snippet: Here is the store where I found the glasses of my dreams, the glasses that were made for me, the glasses that brought me from the foul world of cheap wire frames to the world of designer eyewear. I love my frames, and the Eyeglass Works is the only place where I've ever seen the...

history.name: Zento Contemporary Japanese Cuisine
history.snippet: Zento is a tiny little shoe box of a restaurant with no curb appeal that's serving up some mighty fine sushi. Well, that's what the Inquirer says. I'm no sushi connoisseur, but our dishes tasted very fresh. We ordered square sushi and the green river roll. Our square sushi was m...

history.name: Miel Patisserie
history.snippet: Miel's offerings are quite tasty, but still not worth the pricey price. If you feel like wasting your or someone else's money, you can treat yourself to a little edible work of art. It's all about how they look. Sign of the times.

history.name: Pattaya
history.snippet: Just hearing the word Pattaya makes my mouth water. I'll eat anything they're serving up. Been here many, many times and never disappointed. My favorite spot is the sun room in the back with a gigantic tree, but the regular room is fine, too. Chill atmosphere & good times.

history.name: The Victor Cafe
history.snippet: Sure, opera's cool and all if that's your thing. The hottest thing about Victor's is the jaw-droppingly posh lounge upstairs. If you have any excuse to be there, that's the place to be. I usually notice families in Victor's for some reason. I wouldn't bring kids here, but whatev...

history.name: Philadelphia Museum of Art
history.snippet: Three cheers for Sunday's pay-what-you-want admissions. Once I went to the museum with not even a penny on a Sunday. That was a total accident, and let me tell you--they expect you to pay Something. It would be worth your effort to research the least busy days/times to visit. I...

history.name: Cosi
history.snippet: Cosi makes yummy salads with quality mixed greens and flavorful dressings that are to die for. Unfortunately, that's pretty much where the positives end. This location is notorious for 2 kinds of servers: totally inattentive and witheringly nasty. Maybe it's something in the wat...

history.name: US Post Office
history.snippet: Surprisingly, the wackness of the Fairmount post office comes not from disgruntled postal workers, but from very bizarre members of the public who come here to purchase postal services. For some reason, every time I come in here, I get the distinct pleasure of listening to someo...

history.name: KC's Pastries
history.snippet: For some reason, I prefer this KC to the one on 11th, but I don't really remember why. Same simple bubble teas & same vast array of mysterious pastries that I'm too scared to try without the guidance of a veteran. Hooray for cheap sweets!

history.name: Bath & Body Works
history.snippet: I think it's funny that a store where most items are $10 has a security guard at the door. Seriously B&B, no one wants to steal your stinky lotions. It's interesting how B&B used to use a kitschy theme in its packaging, but they've changed to a different, more 'mature' looking a...

history.name: US Post Office
history.snippet: If you brave the dank underground to use this post office, which I often do, be forewarned: at any given time of day, the odds of coming here & finding a long line are 99:100. But have no fear; several of the workers at this location are hardened veterans who have streamlined op...

history.name: Reading Terminal Market
history.snippet: My last meal before dying would have to be red curry from the Thai place, freshly blended juice from the juice place, and a piece of cake from the Amish. If I could figure out when this place isn't packed, that would be my favorite time to go. However, such a time does not seem...

history.name: Bui's
history.snippet: Yes indeed, the only thing I've ever purchased from Bui's is the egg sandwich. Fresh roll + perfectly cooked eggs + made to order + *cheap* = bliss. I keep it simple: egg whites with cheese on a long roll. They don't look at me funny if I ask for just 1 piece of cheese or black...

history.name: The Bridge: Cinema de Lux
history.snippet: The Bridge is pretty clean, and I believe the parking across the street is free with a validated ticket. I like the whole assigned seating bit. I've never been to the bar in the back, but the Bridge definitely markets it like it's totally hip. There's often no one here for matin...

history.name: Su Xing House
history.snippet: Su Xing offers vegetarian Chinese. It's a pretty small restaurant that gets most of its business during the weekday lunch hour. Their lunch special is a great value--you get a soup, a side, and an entree for $7. I've had better vegetarian Chinese, but their mushrooms are really...

history.name: Penang
history.snippet: Judging by these reviews, the mango dish is one of the most popular. I've ordered it in Philly & DC and I have to say I'm fundamentally not feeling it. They go way overboard with the sauce. It's too thick, too strong, & there's too much of it. Ugh. This place can get so insanely...

history.name: Loie
history.snippet: Went to Loie late night on Saturday. Am somewhat traumatized from the experience. Just kidding.... I think. Loie's property is a long, narrow rectangle. This probably works fine as an eatery, but not as a 'club' or dance floor. The amount of folks here was good--full without bei...

history.name: Vintage Wine Bar
history.snippet: If I were a restaurant, I would be Vintage. Very modern, but less trendy & more classy. You can find exposed brick walls anywhere downtown, but where else will you find votives tucked into niches in those brick walls? The painted walls glow in deep yellow and the floors glow in...

history.name: Greek Lady
history.snippet: With the constant racket emanating from this place and the overwhelming COLLEGE vibe, it can't get more than 3 stars. But GL earns those 3 on the strength of the chicken sandwiches alone. My favorite is chicken with spinach & honey dijon. On a long roll, it's easily 2 meals if y...

history.name: Izzy & Zoe's
history.snippet: I'm not sure why Izzy's menu is a bit overpriced, but when I feel like springing for it [which is hardly ever], I do love the cobb salad. Order for sit-down service; your portion will be larger than the take-out size. I appreciate how hearty the salad is, but what makes it excep...

history.name: Ben & Jerry’s
history.snippet: Since in theory, each scoop shop is selling the same product, the only thing to distinguish each from the other is staff, decor, and location. There's nothing outstanding about this one on 40th. Of course, lots of Penn students come anyway. The staff are amiable enough. Some peo...

history.name: New York & Company
history.snippet: I think it's safe to say that NY&Co is trying a little bit too hard to be hip & metropolitan by naming all of its pants for various NYC locales. Calm down, little clothing store; no one's trying to say you're not urban. The staff here tend to be friendly and approachable. Pricin...

history.name: Philly Diner
history.snippet: Dave C. says it all. I'm not going to the new location, but the old one was very eerie indeed. Needless to say, the food was crap as well. There's only one circumstance where the diner is worth attending: your first time being there on a Fri/Sat night at 2am. There is much hilar...

history.name: House of Beauty
history.snippet: HB deserves no stars. In fact, it deserves to be burnt to the ground. This is a dingy store chock full of millions of hair products with no guidance to be had from the employees. The owner, not a person of color, is usually standing around staring at customers, virtually all wom...

history.name: Pathmark
history.snippet: This PM has come a long way. Within the last couple years, the store has undergone a much-needed redesign & it now features brighter lighting. It feels brighter & more spacious in here than ever before. They also have those nifty self-checkout lanes, although I don't appreciate...

history.name: Clark Park
history.snippet: Every urban jungle needs an oasis or three. Clark Park is West Philly's respite from the built environment. It's huge. There's more than enough space for little kids, big kids, running dogs, hipsters, farmers markets, and a couple of crazies to coexist peacefully. After you know...

history.name: Gap
history.snippet: Positives: First off, I'm loving the spaciousness of this giant, 2 story property. This store uses its racks to break up the floor space into smaller 'alcoves,' if you will. It's clean, bright, and very neat. For some reason, bigger Gaps show up on listings as Gap Kids or Gap Bo...

history.name: University of Pennsylvania Bookstore
history.snippet: This is definitely the spot for Penn gear, not that I ever buy any, & for housewares, not that I buy them from here.... If you're a student with someone else's credit card and without a ride to Ikea/Target, you can furnish your room with the bookstore's wares. It's a rather larg...

history.name: Kiehl's
history.snippet: I actually don't use Kiehl's products & I think there's too much hype about them, but I'm still giving this place 4 stars for incredible customer service. I visited the store with a friend who buys this stuff, and a store associate approached me to talk about what I was looking...

history.name: HotelBar
history.snippet: Interesting experience a couple Fridays ago -- the whole city was shut down b/c of the ridiculous sleet, but this bar & restaurant was open. There were about 10 people inside & the place was playing music like it was any other night. I liked the ambiance; there's a lot of dark w...

history.name: Bare Feet Shoes
history.snippet: BFS apparently strives to keep one of every shoe in inventory in the front window. Whoever is in charge of stacking 500 heels and flats in such a way that you can clearly see every single one is a geometric genius. Once you get inside, things are much more simple: knee-level row...

history.name: Naked Chocolate Cafe
history.snippet: After a long, long day, I decided to hit up Naked with a friend. Tuesday nights feature live jazz from 8 to 10, which I didn't know. Really nice performance that added to the atmosphere. I knew that having just one chocolate might send me sliding down a slippery slope, so I stuc...

history.name: Hahnemann University Hospital
history.snippet: Hi, Hahnemann. You SUCK because I was born in your rinky dink hospital, and yet you have no record of this. Do you dunk birth records in your coffee in the morning & eat them for breakfast? I do not appreciate that strange, disturbed practice. Thank God I've never been in your E...

history.snippet: First off, you should know that I am notoriously unforgiving of wack vibes in lame clubs. When the undanceable song comes on, I will sit down. After multiple undanceable songs, I will BOO THE DJ. All of this is merely to say that I don't give any pity points, so when I say a clu...

history.name: Tiffany's Bakery
history.snippet: I hope that you all know even without reading this review that Tiffany's in the Gallery is the shiznit. They sell strawberry shortcake by the slice for $3, and this slice can't reasonably be eaten in one sitting. That's not to say that your average Philadelphian wouldn't do it a...

history.name: Finn McCool's Ale House
history.snippet: Finn MacCool's suffers from a cripplingly weird vibe. In other words, this bar sucks. They do serve up a mighty strong, mighty large long island iced tea for $7. However, when I walked in with my friend on Friday night, every single person sitting at or standing behind the bar t...

history.name: Fork
history.snippet: For a mind blowing price, I expect a mind blowing experience. Fork doesn't blow, but it doesn't blow my mind either. Sure, there's an element of lameness in giving a mediocre review to a place surrounded by as much hype as Fork is, but what kind of person would I be if I failed...

history.name: ONE at Rittenhouse
history.snippet: [In the voice of Bill Lumbergh] Mm yeeeeeeeeeah I'm gonna have to uh go ahead and uh disagree with you on that. The Mansion may be beautiful architecturally, but it feels like a haunted house instead of sexy and the hip hop floor sucks, so if you could uh go ahead and uh not rec...

history.name: Bump
history.snippet: Bump, how do I love thee? Let me count the ways. Over 10 martinis at the jaw-dropping, wallet-opening price of $3 from 5-7 on 6 nights of the week. This makes me a happy lady. I left before it got late, but the early evening was very chill with plenty of space in the house for t...

history.name: MAC Cosmetics
history.snippet: You've got to hand it to MAC--their luscious eye shadows are pumped full of pigment, and it's to die for. What's lamer than a shadow that looks vivid in the pot and actually shows up on the skin like a pastel baby powder? You know what I'm talking about. None of that nonsense at...

history.name: Magic Carpet
history.snippet: This is the second location of Magic Carpet, which is also located on Spruce Street. There are 2 main things to know: It is all vegetarian. If you are not addicted already, you should be. If it's your first time and you swing by during the lunch hour, be warned that a long line...

user_persona: Whitestown food lover who likes brewery patios, half chicken, smoked tuna sandwiches, hush puppies, fresh seafood markets, and relaxed atmosphere.

history.name: Moontown Brewing Company
history.snippet: Great atmosphere! Beautiful outdoor patio area. The first time I went we split the half chicken with beans and French fries... everything was great! Chicken was very very good! Had great flavor! Second time we got wings for an atmosphere... we were not thrilled with the wings......

history.name: Caplinger's Fresh Catch
history.snippet: AMAZING smoked tuna sandwich! I love love love me a tuna sandwich and this was probably THE best tuna sandwich I have ever had! And the hush puppies! Ooo so good!

user_persona: Reno family diner who likes excellent sushi, dependable grocery stops, helpful service, kid-friendly Vietnamese cravings, app ordering, and correct pizza delivery.

history.name: Golden Flower Vietnamese
history.snippet: I go here at least once a week with my kids. My son especially LOVES this place. I really don't see what all the fuss is about. If you have a craving for pho in the middle of the night, this place is probably open. The service however is definitely hit or miss. I really don't ca...

history.name: 7-Eleven
history.snippet: This is my sev! I've been coming here for 10 years. Parking sucks unless you know how to maneuver the lot. Plus they let you use the bathroom. How many sevs let you do that???

history.name: Sushi Rose
history.snippet: Nothing to rave about at Sushi Rose. I went here for dinner with the BF about a week ago. The restaurant is small and LOUD. At first I thought it was the party of about 8 drunk people having some kind of party. But even after they left, I still couldn't hear what my BF was sayin...

history.name: Save Mart
history.snippet: I've been shopping here for about 8 years. Love the employees. Love that they haven't changed the layout in yeeeeeears. I can get in and out quickly because they arent constantly changing shit around. The music is usually pretty good. Like 70s adult contemporary Fleetwood Mac an...

history.name: Save Mart
history.snippet: I can't believe I have to do this but I'm taking away a star. My beloved Save Mart, what have you done??? You've changed and my heart is broken. I know you've got to keep up with the changing times but WHYYYY? Why did you change your soundtrack? Instead of walking in to the mell...

history.snippet: Yes! Omg yes! I have tried pretty much every sushi restaurant from South Reno to Northwest. This place blows any of my old favorites out of the water. The thing I like about Tokyo Sushi is that they bring your order out as its being made. So instead of waiting 15-20 minutes for...

history.name: MAC Cosmetics
history.snippet: I have always received consistently good/helpful customer service at this location. Today I decided that I couldn't handle my rosacea anymore and I needed something better than cheap drugstore foundation to cover it up. I thought I would go into MAC with a totally naked face and...

history.name: Domino's Pizza
history.snippet: Eh, honestly not bad at all. I order Dominos a few times a year. I always order through the app and they've never gotten it wrong. The food (pizza and wings) has always been fresh and hot. Delivery took a little longer tonight than usual but we got 2 hot pizzas. One was thin cru...

user_persona: Voorhees diner who wants fresh customizable Japanese food, healthy options, dietary flexibility, tasty sushi or ramen, and avoids empty poorly run restaurants.

history.name: Black Olive
history.snippet: ZERO stars. Had a 7:30 reservation for a party of 4 on a Friday night. First red flag was that there was only 1 party dining there. Friday night is a popular time for dinners, yet the place was empty. Upon being seated I presented a coupon. Waiter brought it to the back, then re...

history.name: Oki Maki
history.snippet: This place is amazing! Food is so fresh and tasty. Lots of healthy options and food is customizable for those with specific tastes or dietary restrictions. Reasonably priced for the high quality product they produce. One of the best fast, casual dining places around.

user_persona: Philadelphia late-night diner who likes acknowledged dive bars, good beer, tiny gritty spaces, cheesesteaks, pork rinds, and avoids disappointing takeout pasta.

history.name: Cafe Olé

history.name: Doobies
history.snippet: The bartender will actually acknowledge you. The food is really good, the beer yummy, the bathroom incredibly tiny with great graffiti reading, and the natural wood is perfect for a bar. It can get loud, but grab a seat and you won't care. Seriously. Oh, and the juke box kicks a...

history.name: Grilladelphia
history.snippet: It's 12 am. I'm pregnant. In Mt. Airy. Where do I send my boyfriend for a cheesesteak? Grilladelphia. It's that good. (Get a bag of pork rinds on the side and you're golden)

history.name: Johnny Brenda's
history.snippet: One more reason why hipsters should be deported back to New Jersey and the 'burbs and get the hell out of my city. Pff.

history.name: The Dive Bar
history.snippet: A rickety staircase to a pool room, complete with bathroom, darts, cigarette machine, and two tables. Oh, dive bar. So many fond memories. It was a trek to get there, but worth it once I did. I stuck with PBR, because I was there to be cheap and enjoy awesome people. One of the...

history.name: Pizzacato
history.snippet: I work in Olde City and was craving fettucine alfredo. I searched around on grubhub and saw that they did take out. Cool! I ordered the alfredo, which the unhappy woman on the phone repeated as bolognese and I had to correct her. I told her I only wanted the plain fettucine alfr...

user_persona: Saint Louis regular who likes friendly bar grills, chicken spedini, wings, comfortable sushi, craft beer, cocktails, lunch specials, and pre-event meals.

history.name: Fenton Bar and Grill
history.snippet: This is a bar and grill in the industrial park in Fenton, along the south side of I-44. As such, this is a place that caters to the local clientele. I go here frequently for lunch. They have excellent lunch specials each day, priced reasonably and also excellent soups. The barte...

history.name: Sidebar
history.snippet: A fun place to hang out for beers in the evening. Great place to have lunch. They have a great Chicken Spedini sandwich, and I really like the wings as well. The food is well prepared and the girls behind the bar or serving you are pleasant, efficient and attractive. Great place!

history.name: Mizu Sushi Bar
history.snippet: Very nice people, great service and great Sushi. Had the Hurricane Rolls. Excellent as always. Also the Sexy Girl Rolls. Place very comfortable. history.name: The Royale Food & Spirits
history.snippet: A great place to get an outstanding meal or grab craft beers or cocktails with friends or your significant other. I frequently visit for their lunch specials, which always feature great locally sourced, seasonal vegetables and meats. I always enjoy their seasonal salads, and gre...

history.name: The Note Bar
history.snippet: I've gone to The Note several times, either before a Blues Hockey game or before concerts at Enterprise Center or Stifel Theater (Kiel Opera House), which are both nearby. The atmosphere is great, the bar and restaurant staff are fantastic, including two of their bartenders, bot...

user_persona: Philadelphia-area diner who likes BYOB dinners, attentive group service, fair portions, quaint desserts, hard-to-get reservations, margaritas, cheap pho, and low-key Mexican.

history.name: Susanna Foo Gourmet Kitchen

Upon arrival, we learned that we lost our table on the second floor but the rest...

I like the concept of this movie theater but I don't really enjoy coming here that oft...

user_persona: Fairview Heights diner who loves home-cooked Southern portions, Korean appetizers, Saint Louis pizza, Caribbean soul food, gumbo, great flavor, and consistent quality.

history.name: Lotawata Creek Southern Grill
history.snippet: One of my all time favorite restaurants. The food is great. Tastes like a home cooked meal. Large portions. The quality is always the same. I never leave unsatisfied

history.snippet: Good representation of St Louis style pizza. The wings could be better but the pizza is A1. Up there with Imos

history.snippet: I had a random taste for Caribbean/African cuisine. I found this restaurant online. The food is great. Heavy portions of sides. Great flavor and great customer service. I've already been back and I'm going again!

history.name: CJ Mugg's
history.snippet: Recommended by a friend. I went here for dinner one night. The gumbo was delicious. Pretty quiet but it might've been because we went late. Perfect spot for cold weather

user_persona: New Orleans visitor who wants convenient seafood with real flavor, strong drinks, diner-style atmosphere, and better value than tourist-view restaurants.

history.name: Cafe Pontalba

user_persona: Reno newcomer who wants better Vietnamese food, helpful service, quick errands, cash-friendly local businesses, and restaurants that live up to reviews.

history.name: Pho

user_persona: Saint Louis diner who likes poke, Korean noodles, vegan brunch, boba, cozy cocktails, reasonable prices, spacious cafes, and polished special-occasion restaurants.

history.name: Nami Ramen
history.snippet: The food took long to come out. I feel like the ramen could have been more reasonably priced because right now I don't think it's worth it. The quality was okay but the broth was kinda too salty. Would not come back for a second time.

history.snippet: I remember a few years back when I had my first poke in LA, I was so in love with the dish but there were none in St. Louis. I'm overjoyed now because of the growing popularity of poke in St. Louis so couldn't be more excited to have Poke Munch opening. Their food was pretty goo...

history.snippet: I picked this place for my birthday and I could not be more satisfied! I love everything about this place, the decors, the food, the environment and the servers. history.snippet: Another authentic Korean restaurant is in town!!! I enjoyed my experience eating here a lot. history.snippet: Kung Fu Tea is here and everyone is excited to get it from St. Louis! history.snippet: I LOVE THIS PLACE! They have so many good vegetarian options. I'm not a vegetarian myself but enjoy the food anyway. It is a good way to eat healthy and support the environment by eating no meat! I would definitely come back again and try other dishes.
