history.snippet: Fantastic food, but only one problem: it's always busy, and once we reserved a reservation and it took over 30 minutes for us to sit down at one of those tables. But very good place to eat for a special occasion.

history.name: Pantry Restaurant
history.snippet: Have eaten here eaten here several times at this retro place for Breakfast and never, ever disappointed, recently had a Pantry hamburger for lunch and surprised as to the burger, it was not a frozen patty but a fresh hand made burger, I spoke to the owner and he stated it was RR...

history.name: Fresh Off The Hook
history.snippet: I had the salmon special is it was cooked just right with a touch of the raspberry sauce, the shrimp bisque was great also, place is a little small and in a strip mall but do not let that fool you, it is a great place for dinner. Servers were quick even though the place was busy.

history.name: Chipotle Mexican Grill
history.snippet: its okay, I do not like the layout of the interior but ok place to eat, I usually eat my tacos at a taco truck which I think is much better

history.name: QDOBA Mexican Eats
history.snippet: This place serves marginal Mexican and it is way overpriced, I do not know what all the hype is about this company, go to a local taco truck for a good taco and reasonable price.

history.name: Sparklight
history.snippet: I too have been overpaying $50/month for 5mb of internet service. I too am waiting for google internet. Cable One is obtuse to their customers so I'll be a former customer soon, I'll call customer service once more and give them a last chance.

history.name: Cottonwood Grille
history.snippet: Very nice interior, nice location by the river, I had the trout and my wife had the Halibut Onate and friends had tenderloin and Atlantic Salmon, All were perfect and nicely presented and we all enjoyed our meal. Limited desserts but we had the Sorbet and French Silk pie, all ou...

history.name: Tucanos Brazilian Grill
history.snippet: I went in with my wife and kids for the first time. We have always heard about this place. The service was great, and the servers serving the meat were very efficient. But, we were disappointed in the quality of the meat. It felt like we were being fed scraps and pieces at a zoo...

history.name: Proto's Pizza
history.snippet: Absolutely love this place. Everything was perfect. Not noisy at all, which was very good. Not very busy, no lines, enough seats to fit everyone in. TV's for entertainment, and very, very good pizza for dinner.

history.name: Tree City Juice & Smoothie Cafe
history.snippet: I love this place, I always order a "Go Orange" and it is my favorite, the employes are all very nice too. I would say you need a bigger place to park bikes as this place is close to the greenbelt. As General Douglas MacArthur once said, "I shall return".

history.name: Hugo's Deli
history.snippet: I usually get a ham and bread always fresh and ham is fresh sliced, the potato salad is good too. All the food here is fresh and very good, that's what make this place stand out. Staff are very friendly also.

history.name: Liquor Stores
history.snippet: Pretty sterile establishment but what do you expect from a government agency, employees are also sterile, only good thing is they sell liquor.

user_persona: Tucson diner who likes affordable tortas, agua frescas, authentic Chinese specialties, office takeout, and dependable Italian pub food.

history.name: Taqueria El Pueblito
history.snippet: This is my favorite affordable Mexican restaurant. They do not serve beer but their Agua Fresca's are great. The Barbacoa Torta (all their Tortas are great) is great as well as the Tacos Dorado. Breakfast Burros are huge and amazing. The patio is a great place to sit as well as...

history.name: Chariot Pizza
history.snippet: This place has been an institution on the west side and a go to for great Italian pub food. I usually get take for the office everyone loves it, I am giving it 3 stars because the service is terrible.

history.name: Sky Dragon
history.snippet: Finally authentic Chinese cuisine. SF Cumin Lamb, Braise Chicken Feet, Dry-Stirred Fried Pork Intestines are highlights. This is a large menu which is often hard to execute, the more common dishes Mongolian, Hunan, Kung Pao w/ name your protein are done well. All the Duck dishes...

user_persona: Wesley Chapel shopper who values clean stores, friendly help, easy parking, fair prices, consistent family restaurants, thrift finds, and dependable local service.

history.name: Capital Tacos
history.snippet: I would say average for what we ordered. Portions were nice but pricey. Taste was good but no wow factor. Ordered a bucket of chips but only half were eatable. For what we paid, we could have went to a full service restaurant. Not sorry we went but probably won't be going back.

history.name: Noble Crust
history.snippet: Not a fan. Italian food is pretty straight forward. I get trying to come up with something new but this was a swing and a miss. Portions were not what you would expect at an Italian restaurant. If you are looking for a more traditional place, this isn't it. Seems more of a hipst...

history.name: The Hungry Greek
history.snippet: Good fast Greek food. Not a big place but we had no trouble finding a seat. Staff seemed friendly and the place was clean. Will be going back.

history.name: Cracker Barrel Old Country Store
history.snippet: Good home style food. A good variety, something for everyone. Portions were nice. Staff was friendly and came by often to check on us. Worth a go.

history.name: Hungry Harry's Family Bar-B-Que
history.snippet: Good BBQ. We go often and always get good food. Hit and miss as to the portion size, a noticeable difference. Everything served is good, no bad choices. A few sides to choose from, all are good.

history.name: The Shops at Wiregrass
history.snippet: Very nice, clean , outside mall. A good variety of shops and places to eat. More security being seen would be nice. I never see any.

history.name: Tampa Premium Outlets
history.snippet: Newer outside mall in the area. Many shops to choose from. With the area growing, something new pops up quite often. Places to eat are near by which is nice.

history.name: Grove At Wesley Chapel
history.snippet: A few big name stores here. Clean and plenty of parking. Bigger than you may think. If one of the stores are one you need, they will usually have what you are looking for. They are all big and we'll stocked.

history.name: Target
history.snippet: Gone downhill big time. Even before the remodeling their stock was going down and the whole store seemed dirty. Not sure what is going on but with other stores so close, we don't even bother any longer. Was our go-to store but times change. Staff seems not to happy to be there.

history.name: Costco Wholesale
history.snippet: Always clean with friendly staff. A good variety of items which change often enough to notice. They do have samples but I'm not a fan of them. They seem to bring out the walmart in people. The items seem a bit more healthy than the average store which I like.

history.name: Piccola Italia Restaurant
history.snippet: Good Italian food with different dishes to choose from. Always clean with friendly staff. Portions are on par with what you would expect, plentiful.

history.name: The Great Catch
history.snippet: A nice place to go when wanting fish. Shrimp was good as was the fish platter. Friendly wait staff who came by often to check on us.

history.name: Sergio's Restaurant & Pizzeria
history.snippet: Great pizza. We have been getting pizza here for years and have never been disappointed. They have other options to choose from which have all been good as well.

history.name: Wawa
history.snippet: Newer place so cleanliness is not a problem. They are consistently the best price around if not a member of a club. Getting in and out is no trouble. With many pumps, they seem to always have an open one.

history.name: RaceTrac
history.snippet: Newer gas station, with better prices than most. Enough pumps so no waiting to get to one. Easy in and out to the main highway. Always has been on the cleaner side.

history.name: Walgreens
history.snippet: On par for most other Walgreens. Clean with mostly friendly staff. Each one is stocked with items for that area so you should find what you need. Easy in and out to the main drag.

history.name: PDQ Wesley Chapel
history.snippet: Better than average food and usually a clean restaurant. Staff is always friendly and actually talks to you when you speak to them.

history.name: Yamato Japanese Steakhouse & Sushi Bar
history.snippet: Not bad but a bit pricey for what you get. The cooks put on a good show but don't portion out the food equally. It is more than noticable. Won't be going back. To many other places to eat in the area.

history.name: Chick-fil-A
history.snippet: Good food if you are wanting chicken. Fast service every time. They are boxed in so hard to get a parking spot at times. Staff is helpful and friendly.

history.name: Panera Bread
history.snippet: Food is good but may not be as healthy as you may think. Tables are usually in need of a wipe down before being seated. Don't see staff just hanging out so it may be a short staff most of the time. A way to save some money. Who knows.

history.name: Five Guys
history.snippet: Good burgers, with a bunch of fries. Always fresh. Staff always friendly and in a good mood. Clean tables. Peanuts to eat as you wIt for your order. A regular stop for us.

history.name: Ulta Beauty
history.snippet: They seem to have a wide variety of products. Staff is usually friendly and ready to help with any questions you may have. Stock is well organized and easily accessible.

history.name: Sally Beauty Supply
history.snippet: Wife and daughter come here when supplies are needed. They usually find what they are looking for. If not, the staff is helpful to give you a hand.

history.name: Sunoco
history.snippet: Not bad if you do all the gimmicks they run to get discounts. Easy in out which is nice. Not my first choice but have no problem stopping here if need be.

history.name: T J Maxx
history.snippet: Typical for these type of stores. Usually clean with lots of stock. As with most, if they have what you want or need, this is the place to go.

history.name: JCPenney
history.snippet: If you can catch a sale for what you want or need, this could be the place for you. Clean with lots to choose from. With all the stores in the area, definitely worth a stop.

history.name: Marshalls
history.snippet: Go here always hoping for better but usually leave disappointed. Not a bad place, just never have what I am looking for. If I actually found more I wanted to buy, I would give four stars.

history.name: Walmart Supercenter
history.snippet: Typical Walmart but newer so that helps. Always a mix of people you would expect to see here. I go to get a good deal like others but come one people.

history.name: Happy Hangar Cafe
history.snippet: Does not disappoint. Cubans have pulled turkey with a slice of ham steak. Hamburger was large and juicy. Place was clean with great, friendly service. Watching the planes come and go is good as well. Will be going back.

history.name: Tech N Cell
history.snippet: Wife's cell phone battery died so we went here to get a new one. Long story short, they put a used battery in the phone and it never was right. For some reason my wife said nothing of the problems and things were never right. It was some time ago so I guess I just get to eat thi...

history.name: Gamestop
history.snippet: We live somewhat close so we pop in often. Staff seems always ready to help but not over bearing. Lots to choose from on hand. They run specials on games which is nice. Worth a stop for your gaming needs.

history.name: The Salvation Army Family Store & Donation Center
history.snippet: A go to shop if looking for good used clothes and such. Helpful staff, clean shop and well organized. Prices are very reasonable. Along with clothes, they have furniture, and other household items. The clothes are usually clean and in good shape. Lots of glassware if you're in n...

history.name: Outback Steakhouse
history.snippet: Steaks were very good, seasoned and done the way we ordered. Service was top notch and the place was clean.

history.name: Gulfside Regional Hospice Thrift Store
history.snippet: Worth a stop. They have different stuff somewhat now and then. Like most places like this, they are limited as to what they have but if they do, it is well worth the effort.

history.name: AAA Gold & Jewelry
history.snippet: This guy is a total b.s.er. Brought a piece in for a cleaning. It was not done correctly and he broke the piece I had him cleaning. It was not visible, so I didn't realize it for some time after. I was also quoted a price for work done on another item. After the work was done he...

history.name: Collins Street Junction
history.snippet: Better shops in the area. Seems to be under staffed. Waited for a good time to get help to look at an item. After finally getting the lady to come look at what we wanted to look at. She said she needed to go get the key. She never returned. Place wasn't that busy, but either way...

history.name: Tung Tung
history.snippet: Doesn't have the best location but they have better than average food. The pork fried rice was dry, very dry but all else was quite good. Chicken was good and was all good meat chunks. A bit on the pricier side but you get plenty. We were there for dinner and the take out was no...

history.name: Los Vallarta Mexican Restaurant
history.snippet: Have gone here off and on for some time. Never a disappointment. Friendly staff and always clean. They have plenty to choose from. Fair prices with good portions. Will continue to go.

history.name: Tokyo Grill & Sushi
history.snippet: Good food at a good price. If you like sushi, they have lots of rolls to choose from. We go often and they have all been very good so far. They have other good dishes as well. Chairs are a little wore but the place is always clean. Well worth a stop if you like Japanese food.

history.name: Big Top Flea Market
history.snippet: Typical Florida flea market. Lots of flea market items and lots of interesting items. If you look, you may find that older whatever you have been looking for. The outside vendors seem to have different varieties each weekend. Not drastic changes but noticable. The vegetable vend...

history.name: The Great Catch - Zephyrhills
history.snippet: Good food and friendly service. Has an older look/feel to it but is very clean and has a relaxing feel to it. They have live music and karaoke on certain nights. They also have inside and outside seating. Lots of locals, a sign they are doing something right. Looking forward to...

history.name: Big Top Flea Market
history.snippet: They seem to have run most of the vendors off. Many open spaces. Worth a go for some walking around but very little to look at. Outside vendors seems to be a bit better for deals. I wouldn't make a special trip to go here any longer but if you have time to kill, and want to walk...

history.name: Picker Place Vintage Market
history.snippet: Not sure I would call this place a antique store. More of a yard sale outside and I was looking for Fred G. Sanford on the inside. And the G stands for Got to be kidding me. Half and half flea market stuff and antique stuff, as in wore out household stuff. Not sorry I went but w...

history.name: LongHorn Steakhouse
history.snippet: Have been a few times. Never disappointed. Food was either what you would expect, or wow, way above par. More times than not, above par, very nice. Wait staff is always right on top of things. Clean place with nice surroundings. Easy in and easy out for the location. One of our...

history.name: Walgreens
history.snippet: Not sure what has happened here. Seems as if all the better employees have been run off, quit, or moved on to something better. I tried giving this store a few chances. Not going to give a rating on one or even two bad experiences, but this seems to be the morn now. Cashiers are...

history.name: Walgreens
history.snippet: Started trying this store, along with another, after getting tired of the poor service from my usual store. This store is clean with friendlier staff. Have not run into any real troubles to give a real test. The pharmacy has definitely been better. Have been a few times now and...

history.name: Walgreens
history.snippet: Started trying this store a while ago, after changing from the shabby one down the road. Everyone seems eager to help. Clean as it is a newer store. May be my go-to, much better, needed change from the one I usually have gone to.

user_persona: Sparks diner planning celebrations who wants warm greetings, attentive anniversary service, good brewhouse food, and restaurants that live up to reviews.

history.name: Pietro's Famiglia
history.snippet: UNFORTUNATE-- after reading the reviews on Yelp- we were excited to celebrate our brother's 10 year anniversary. We arrived to a PACKED house- usually a good sign. We had to wait a bit for water and menus but the music and ambiance was good. Then we ordered appetizers and drinks...

history.name: BJ's Restaurant & Brewhouse
history.snippet: WOW- we enjoyed a wonderful evening at BJ's. We were greeted warmly and the food was great. The highlight- however- was definitely Brook. He took care of our table and was engaging and thorough. Such a great attitude and amazing smile to boot. Brook had no idea that we had just...

user_persona: Treasure Island local who wants sunset seating, live music, attentive beach-bar service, short waits, and basic fast food competence.

history.name: McDonald's
history.snippet: Worst McDonald's ever. Sitting in the drive thru for over 25 minutes. Awful service!!!!!

history.name: The Hut Bar and Grill
history.snippet: Great music, great ambiance. We live in the neighborhood and walk over. Twice in the last two weeks we have had terrible experiences. We love a nice glass of wine with dinner. First time out of white wine. Tonight one white wine that was awful. The buffalo shrimp appetizer taste...

history.name: Sloppy Joe's
history.snippet: My husband and I are locals and went down to sloppy Joe's to enjoy a sunset at their outdoor seating area. We were there for an hour and a half and not once did the server approach us to ask for a drink or dinner selection. My husband went to the bar to get our drinks. We were g...

user_persona: Tampa group diner who likes accommodating steakhouses, hot Tex-Mex, friendly fast-casual meals, outdoor Caribbean nights, and efficient drive-thrus.

history.name: Outback Steakhouse
history.snippet: Came in here today. This is usually the outback I visit only because I live about 5 minutes from this one. Today I came in a party of 7. They were very accommodating and I didn't wait longer than 10 minutes to be seated although they were quite busy . The hostess was a little ru...

history.name: Chick-fil-A
history.snippet: The food at chic-fil-a is great . At any chic-fil-a, I should say. I came in today via drive thru. There were only 4 people in front of me in the lane I was in and 3 people in the other lane. I don't know how I managed to stay in line at the same spot for 20 minutes . The drive...

history.name: Chick-fil-A
history.snippet: The food at chic-fil-a is great . At any chic-fil-a, I should say. I came in today via drive thru. There were only 4 people in front of me in the lane I was in and 3 people in the other lane. I don't know how I managed to stay in line at the same spot for 20 minutes . The drive...

history.name: Bahama Breeze
history.snippet: Bahama breeze at night is wonderful . You have the choice to sit inside or outside . The food there is ok but the prices I believe are a little expensive for what you get The waiter I had , I don't remember her name , I think it was korrie, she was very nice and helpful. Two peo...

history.name: Chili's
history.snippet: Chilis is great as a whole. The food was served hot and tasted great ! Great job chefs ! Our waiter was really nice, I don't remember his name but he was extremely busy and still attended to our needs as a table . He tried to speak Spanish to us which was really funny and said a...

history.name: Wow! Fresh & Fast
history.snippet: Amazing food here ! Staff was very friendly even though there was an American Sign Language event going on . I only came here because I was going to the ASL event anyways but it did not disappoint when I ate . The fries are amazing ! The burger I got was a cheese burger and it w...

user_persona: Tucson mostly-vegetarian diner who likes customizable pizza, light Greek plates, fresh Laotian flavors, spicy-but-balanced Indian snacks, and efficient friendly service.

history.name: Tanque Verde Falls Trail
history.snippet: I enjoyed it very much but my body was rebeling. I do like the fact that there are rocks everywhere to support steps, I took my time but the heat got to me eventually. I'm not a regular hiker so it's hard to judge it's intensity....and wasn't able to make it to the bottom, and g...

history.name: Royal Sweets
history.snippet: Friendly service, tasty Puri. I was craving for puri and suggested to get sev puri since I wasn't sure how many types there are. Asked for spicy but easy on the spice. It was just right and satisfying. Got a cup of chai tea as well. Really awesome snacking combo especially with...

history.name: Savanh of Laos
history.snippet: Had Crispy Rice for the first time, it's pretty good. I like the mint and parsley making the dish fresh countering the crispy rice. Appreciating variety of food available to try!

history.name: Little Greek
history.snippet: Love the flavor of the sauces on my Chicken Skewer dinner. It was what I needed, light, refreshing and yet flavorful. Stopped by because a special ran by Amex Card for small biz and will gladly return for more awesome cuisine! Very appreciative for decent food!

history.name: Blaze Pizza
history.snippet: Love the fixed price for make your own pizza. I'm a vegetarian for the most part, live the convenient of picking out my own toppings. Fairlu efficient and friendly service!

history.name: Umami Sushi. Hibachi
history.snippet: Had lunch buffet here and enjoyed it for the most part. There were minimal interaction between me and my server... by means of service. It could due to COVID, just felt service was a bit lacking since my last visit prior to Covid. I had to ask for my miso soup, which I thought w...

user_persona: Philadelphia shopper who values patient bridal service, beginner-friendly running stores, spicy Chinese food, and restaurants that accommodate gluten-free vegan guests.

history.name: Bridal Garden
history.snippet: This store is just not worth hassle. I went to the Bridal Garden because they carried the dress I was looking for and ultimately bought, and I had a lovely experience with my bridal consultant, Diana. First mistake, they told me I to order a size 8 because I am a 32C chest--I am...

history.name: Han Dynasty
history.snippet: First off, I LOVE Han Dynasty--have now been to every location and frequent UCity--but this was our first time at the Manayunk location. Overall, the other locations have it down better. Service here was slow, and one our entrees never made it out. What I was MOST disappointed i...

history.name: Fairmount Running Company
history.snippet: I am an amateur runner who just signed up for my first 1/2 marathon, so this store is just my speed, no pun intended. I admit that some of the bigger stores have more of selection in terms of apparel and super specialized gear, but I come here for sneakers because, as with a sma...

history.name: Crow and the Pitcher
history.snippet: Went in on Sunday not knowing that their brunch menu only had 6 items. It was very difficult to accommodate our one guests who is gluten-free AND vegan, although they tried. She ended up getting some vegetable concoction made up by the kitchen--there wasn't even a salad option i...

user_persona: Tampa native brunch seeker who expects welcoming bar service, worth-the-drive local restaurants, flavorful seafood or burgers, and staff who acknowledge guests promptly.

history.name: Main Course Tampa
history.snippet: This place was highly anticipated and they fell short. I was excited to see a new edition in old west Tampa as a Tampa native. We drove an hour to experience brunch and ready to taste the shrimp and grits. We walked in and there was a line out the door. The dine in area was abou...

history.name: American Social
history.snippet: I was excited about trying this place out but after the horrible experience I will never step foot in here again. Sat down and waited to be served at the bar. Watched several bar tenders talking in a circle directly in front of me. Not one looked our way or asked to help us. Aft...

user_persona: Tampa diner who loves tapas brunch, loaded pizzas, taco deals, sushi, margaritas, bottomless mimosas, and rejects rude or disorganized service.

history.name: Zukku Sushi - Tampa
history.snippet: The cashier is always rude. She has blue hair and glasses. She will not greet you, she will just stare at you awkward. This has happened all three times I have been there. As for the food, it's fantastic. The Shrimp Volcano Roll is great. I highly recommend it.

history.name: Noble Crust
history.snippet: Food was great. I got the Fried chicken and it was an enormous serving. The Gnocchi and Truffle Fries were also amazing. The restaurant is a little pricy but definitely worth it. The atmosphere alone is awesome.

history.name: Mole y Abuela
history.snippet: 10/10 Went for brunch and got the breakfast burrito, all the tapas they had and goat cheese board. Everything was fantastic. I want to try dinner out next.

history.name: Duffy's Sports Grill
history.snippet: Go anywhere but Duffy's. Came here after Training Camp with my family. Staff seemed frazzled when I asked for a table for six. Waitress was nice, but brought my brother his tortillas, cheese, sour cream and tomato out to the table 20 minutes before his fijitas came out. This was...

history.name: Oak & Stone - St. Petersburg
history.snippet: This place is excellent! 10/10! The meatball pizza was fantastic ! I also got a pizza that had pork on it which was very delicious. I also recommend sharing the Gouda Mac and Cheese, by far the best Gouda Mac and Cheese I've had. I LOVED THE SERVICE! And cool beer concept !

history.name: Taco Dirty
history.snippet: This place is fantastic! Best restaurant in Tampa hands down! If you're in the area I would definitely stop by! They have awesome deals on Tuesdays and also happy hour everyday with 2 dollar tacos. I typically get the bowls and it has never disappointed. The only negative I have...

history.name: Rice and Beans
history.snippet: This place was alright. I was super excited to come here with my girlfriend. We love going around Tampa and eating at Latin restaurants. Rice N Beans is nothing out of this world. Basic tostones (I make them better at home), basic meats and basic rice and beans. Nothing exceptio...

history.name: Miller's Ale House
history.snippet: Food is ok. Menu is kind of limited to "zingers". Service is a 0/10. Horrible hostess and horrible waiter. The bartender was very nice but that's it. I came in with a group of 8 people and won't be back ever again.

history.name: Mahana Fresh
history.snippet: Mahana Fresh has the potential to be extremely great. It's very new so I expect to visit again and hopefully my experience is better. Ordering a bowl was a solid 25 minutes because they couldn't get the food prepared fast enough. Manager was extremely nice and apologetic. The bb...

history.name: Backwaters on Sand Key
history.snippet: I would go anywhere else but here if I'm being quite honest. When my wife and I walked up to the host, the host (an old creepy looking white man) looked at us as if we were scum of the earth. I was confused, so I asked him if I needed to sit myself. And he replied in an extremel...

history.name: Marina Cantina
history.snippet: Absolutely amazing!!!! Come here!! My wife and I enjoyed every second here. We came at night and sat outside near the heater. The margaritas were fantastic! The service was impeccable! And the tacos and quesadillas were to die for! This is an awesome spot and I will be coming ba...

history.name: Grillsmith - Wesley Chapel
history.snippet: Absolutely fantastic! Stopped by for some brunch and our server took great care of us! Of course we did the bottomless mimosas which made it 10x better. I recommend the steak and eggs it was fantastic. However I will say the steak and eggs was a small portion but still really go...

history.name: Sushi Cafe
history.snippet: Great sushi and always a great staff! Monster roll is my favorite! Also really great hibachi! I usually do to-go but eating inside is alright. A little dark but nothing too crazy. Overall I'd say this location has the best sushi in the New Tampa area and it's not even close. Lov...

user_persona: Nashville transplant who likes classic burgers, fresh tacos, homestyle Italian, good bagels, specialty coffee, dim sum, and authentic Southern diners.

history.name: Rotier's Restaurant
history.snippet: Great burgers, great atmosphere. I hope places like this stay alive forever. Stopped by there on a Saturday Night, the place was packed with families, couples and friends. I wish they were in DC!

history.name: Fresh Thymes
history.snippet: Very cute place for a pleasant, healthy, and tasty lunch. I had soup and a wrap. The food was fresh and delicious. I will be back.

history.name: China Wok
history.snippet: China Wok is a good Chinese restaurant with excellent customer service. The menu is large, with over 150 dishes. China Wok is an "American Style" Chinese restaurant, so for me it loses a star for authenticity. Otherwise it is very good. I would not hesitate to eat here again. Fo...

history.name: Sky Asian Bistro
history.snippet: This airport sushi bar is better than most. The rolls are big, with good quality fish. On the downside, two rolls can cost over $25.

history.name: Local Taco
history.snippet: First taste was fantastic. Fresh, tasty and filling. Very different experience than other taco shops and an awesome addition to East Nashville. Food was fast and service excellent.

history.name: Coco's Italian Market
history.snippet: Wow, love this place. The staff is super friendly and the food is delicious. I'd call it "homestyle - red table cloth Italian". If that's what you are looking for, you won't be disappointed. The market has a trove of Italian, meats, cheeses, etc. Coco is a great place for a fami...

history.name: Bagel Face Bakery
history.snippet: When I decided to move from the East Coast. I knew there would be much to love in Nashville. One little part of me though, feared a lack of a good bagels. So when I dropped into Bagel Face, 1.5 miles from my house in East Nashville, I was hopeful. The first bite made me smile. N...

history.name: Bao Down
history.snippet: I love Bao and the two I had from Bao Down outside the Christmas Craft Fair didn't disappoint. I'll be following their twitter feed!

history.name: Crema Coffee Roasters
history.snippet: Jonesing for great coffee and a Music City hipster vibe, head to crema. As good as the best high end coffeehouses across the nation.

history.name: Barista Parlor
history.snippet: As fine coffee shops go, Barista P. is really good, so I am obliged to give it 4-stars. If you're anywhere near the gulch, this is an awesome spot. I'm partial to the East Nashville location and I think the frenetic energy there is more to my liking, but you can't go wrong with...

history.name: Local Taco
history.snippet: 2nd meal, this time with the whole family in tow. We loved it. Local Taco has gone out its way offer a large menu built around tacos, enchiladas, and quesadilla's. Super service, great flavor, makes Local Taco a likely family habit.

history.name: Rosepepper Mexican Grill
history.snippet: I first saw Rosepepper glimmering in the July dusk light. Hearing the sound of laughing and clinking glasses, it was inviting. I checked Yelp and saw it rated three stars and had some pretty vocal dissenters, so I held off for five months. I found our waitress to be warm and hel...

history.name: Portland Brew East
history.snippet: In a land of great coffee shops Portland disappoints. I'd be hitting up Portland if this was the only shop in town, but with some darn exciting places in East Nashville, and one across the street, my half finished cup left me wanting.

history.name: Lucky Bamboo China Bistro
history.snippet: Lucky Bamboo is solid Chinese food. If you like your Chinese food skewed slightly American, you'll be satisfied. I am happy that in Nashville I am able to eat dim sum on a weekend afternoon. They have about 7 plates, all but the slightly dry Bao are good. Very pleased to find Ch...

history.name: China Village
history.snippet: The bar for Chinese is set low in Nashville, and China Village falls below it. Housed in a dingy strip mall next to Princes Hot Chicken, China Village has none of its neighbor's appeal. The folks here are working hard, keeping the place clean, while turning out fried American Ch...

history.name: Elliston Place Soda Shop
history.snippet: Four stars for an authentic, southern diner. Elliston is the real deal and seems the same as it would have been 50 years ago. While many a tourist comes to visit, there is no added glitz or three star chef. Entrees are straightforward American fare. Great place doe lunch and ice...

user_persona: Clearwater breakfast diner who values excellent food quality, fast friendly service, easy parking, and delivery restaurants that actually fulfill orders.

history.name: Sunset Grill
history.snippet: Excellent. When you drive in and cannot find a paling place, you know you have picked the right place. Food quality (breakfast), excellent, service friendly, fast, and timely.

history.name: NY Pizza Grill
history.snippet: The simplest way to put this, is exact same experiance as everyone else. Never got food. Ordered through grub hub. Called many times and got run around. Steer clear

The taste ans texture of the meat is inferior, the whiz is often no...

history.snippet: This place makes the sad case for avoiding just about any restaurant on the Main Line and making the short drive into Center City. history.snippet: For not being a United Hub in any way shape or form, I am actually impressed this place exists at all. Clean, comfortable, quiet for work, nice bar, friendly help, free wifi, no complaints!

history.name: Oak Barrel of Eagle
history.snippet: We were disappointed overall. No happy hour menu so meeting a friend after work was over priced based on what we received. Hard uncomfortable seating outside, slow service at best, smokers were no stop including staff and too close to the building for being code.

history.snippet: Love the food. But tables were not kept clean and staff was a bit grumpy. I think it would be a great idea for the owner to secret shop.

history.name: Yen Ching
history.snippet: Web site says they are open until 10:00 pm. We were turned away at 9:30ish as they said they were closed. We let them know the information on the site was incorrect and they did not believe us.

history.snippet: Great food and excellent service! The car hop was cheerful, prompt, and checked back in a timely manner. My bill was less than $10 so I gave her a $5 tip for great service. She asked me if I was sure I wanted to give that much to her.

history.name: N F Landis & Son
history.snippet: We bought a dishwasher at another appliance store that never worked. NF Landis brought the new unit and installed correctly the first time. Their communication was great. They were prompt and professional.

user_persona: Clearwater family outing planner who likes well-maintained mini golf, challenging activities, clean restaurants, attentive staff, and simple Italian beach meals.

history.snippet: What a fabulous place to visit with the family. The course is greatly maintained. It is also quite challenging and fun at the same time. Additionally, you can pay to see and hold baby and adolescent alligators as well.

history.snippet: Excellent food and service. Very clean and attentive staff. The sausage paired with the spaghetti was a nice touch.

history.snippet: The food is mediocre-good but the atmosphere makes up for it. It's a great place for a date night or just to day drink on the patio with peach margaritas. I recommend the burrito and you can't go wrong with a margarita!

history.name: St Roch Market
history.snippet: So basically this is like a higher end food court. It has a cool vibe. Great coffee. Great ceviche. Good wine. I just think this place is pricey for a "market."

history.name: Parasol's

history.snippet: Prettiest park in New Orleans! Across from Tulane's campus Audubon Park sits and is home to some of the oldest oak trees. If you go here and aren't impressed by the scenery than something must be wrong! Come spend a day here and get lost in a book under a pretty tree.

history.snippet: Great place to walk your dogs. Everyone seems friendly and has well-behaved dogs (for the most part). Be careful where you step in the grass, as not everyone avails themselves of the free doggy clean-up bags that are available. Lovely view of the river. Nice, clean and quiet in...

history.snippet: Although this isn't the closest Raley's, it is my preferred store. The staff remembers me, even if I haven't been there in a few weeks. The deli manager made up some tuna salad fresh for me a few weeks ago, just because there was none in the deli case. It IS a bit on the spendy...

history.snippet: Love coming here! They are always genuinely happy to see repeat customers. The hand-painted chairs on the upper-level and in the bar area are beautiful. They make some mean drinks there, including a Cadillac Margarita that is no joke! They are very accommodating of special dieta...

history.snippet: Expensive, but clean and friendly CrossFit gym (or "box", if you prefer the lingo). history.name: Double Edge Fitness - Midtown

user_persona: Reno nightlife customer who rejects casino grime and values distinctive food sourcing, butcher quality, and venues with real taste over empty decor.

history.snippet: Wow! The decor was tasteful, staff attentive, and haute culture was to be had in spades! Nah real talk cesspool of American nightmares out here.

history.snippet: Omega Optical is a great place! I highly recommend them! Very professional and friendly.

user_persona: Reno diner who loves hot soup on rainy days, fresh sushi, personal chef service, seafood omelets, and friendly breakfast staff.

history.snippet: Have been to this new location twice and have had exceptional experience both times. Service has been great and the fish is really fresh with rolls small and really good!!

history.snippet: Amazing breakfast - the seafood omelettes are to die for. Their hash brown potatoes are perfectly done and a little different than the normal. The staff is so nice and they are all working so hard with a smile on!!! Highly recommend.

history.snippet: Great food for a great deal!! Can't beat a $5 burrito. Also, a great way for students to avoid the chipotle line!

history.snippet: Wonderful service. Our waitress, Candice, was very accommodating!! 5 stars for food and service :)

user_persona: Tampa coffee-and-beer explorer who likes walkable cafes, breweries, dog-friendly patios, vegan sweets, tacos, date-night spots, waterfront markets, and local beer.

history.name: Thai Legacy Restaurant
history.snippet: The food was delicious! My wife ordered the chicken pad Thai while I ordered the basil chicken. I am normally hesitant with Asian dishes, but I was so glad I ate outside of my comfort zone. All the servers were so sweet and we will be coming back next time we are in Brandon!

history.name: BluWave Sushi Bar & Grill
history.snippet: The sushi was fantastic! Only downside was the place was pretty dead. Granted, my wife went at around 5:30PM so maybe it was too early but the service great and the sushi was definitely excellent

history.name: World Of Beer
history.snippet: I love World of Beer! The all outside atmosphere is so fun. The food is great and the beer selection is pretty good as well. I wish they had a few more local options but nonetheless they have many choices. Definitely recommend if you want to get away from the party crowd and wan...

history.name: Buddy Brew - Hyde Park
history.snippet: Absolutely love my neighborhood buddy brew! My wife and I walk here all the time. Coffee is fantastic and all the staff are so kind and courteous. Plus, we can bring our dog in which is a huge plus! Highly prefer Buddy Brew over a Starbucks

history.snippet: Foundation has to be my favorite coffee shop in Tampa! The coffee is fantastic, the decor is super cool and their is so much seating so it's a great spot to bring your friends or a date!

history.snippet: Love the Lab! Really tucked away behind some warehouses but once you find it, it's awesome! Coffee is absolutely delicious and the food is great as well. Great place to study or just relax. Only downside is their isn't a ton of seating so it's not great for larger groups.

history.name: Green Lemon
history.snippet: Definitely a must if you're in South Tampa! Great menu, prices and drinks. Definitely need to get there early to make sure you get a table! Went at 5:00PM on a Tuesday and the wait was 45 minutes. Totally worth it, however!

history.name: Sweet Soul
history.snippet: Sweet Soul is the bomb! Such a cute little shop with a great outdoor area even including a tennis court! history.snippet: Jai Alai is one of my favorite beers so I was so excited to finally go to Cigar City. Sadly, I was pretty disappointed. history.name: Hidden Springs Ale Works
history.snippet: Hidden Springs truly is a hidden gem. Fantastic beer and a quaint tap room. Wish the space was a little bigger and had an outside seating option but I think the beer makes up for it!

history.snippet: I think Angry Chair is awesome. Definitely one of the top breweries in Florida as far as beer quality goes. Great selection and not priced to high, especially for what you get. history.snippet: 7venth Sun is a great place to visit! They have a huge inside and outside area, great food trucks and they are even dog friendly! history.name: The Blind Tiger Cafe - SOHO
history.snippet: The Blind Tiger was okay! Nice little café in Soho but the coffee and food quality is average. Definitely prefer to come here over Starbucks but it's still nothing special.

history.snippet: The C House is a phenomenal spot in Seminole Heights! My wife and I went out there for a date and we had such a great time. The outside area with live music is a blast. history.name: Salty Cowboy
