history.snippet: A Indiana classic! A Super cool place to take the kiddos! Good pizza and Breadsticks. The kids love to watch the train deliver the beverages. One hidden treasure about this place is their cookies! Yes Cookies, they have Bakery quality cookies at a cheap $.99 price, they are big...

history.name: Shallos Antique Restaurant & Brewhouse
history.snippet: The best Bar in Indy! Want a specialty Beer? They got it! If they dont ask and they will get it. Join their club, drink beers and become famous when the post your name on the wall. They have a great Atmosphere, The two story bar has a very cool theme with old signs and dark stai...

history.name: Yiayia's House of Pancakes
history.snippet: Sophias is a solid breakfast house! Great food, Good portions and good service. Order 1 egg and get 2, order 2 eggs and get 4 and so on. I highly suggest trying the ham and pancakes! Give them a try you wont be dissapointed, the best breakfast resturant in greenwood!

history.name: Johnny Carino's
history.snippet: A recent great discover... This place is great for lunch too! They have a lunch menu for $7.99 that includes a good portion meal, a salad and fresh bread. I've been here 3 times for lunch and each time service has been outstanding and the food was very good. Dinner time however...

history.name: Family Video
history.snippet: I really miss video stores! Although thus one is a bit outdated they have great customer service and a great variety of videos! Your first 30 days of membership you get 1/2 price!

history.name: Vito Provolone's
history.snippet: Vito's is an exellent "non-chain" italian resturant! Your meal comes free with their famous bread.... very, very good. Their salads are as fresh can get! Their service is excellent and food is very good... my kid also loves their pizza. Give Vito's a try!

history.name: El Meson
history.snippet: Best Mexican Resturant in Greenwood! I love their chips and spicy but not too spicy salsa. My favorite meal is the Quesdilla Relano with Grilled Chicken. Very clean and well decorated, Great service with a fair price.

history.name: Brozinni Pizzeria
history.snippet: Pizza-tastic!!!! This place is incredible! Highquality toppings on a great crust! The pizza slices are huge (NY style) the goodness doesnt stop at the high quality pizza they have a "to die for" appitizer called "knuckles" ... these things are the bomb! a garlicy goodness you mu...

history.name: Half Price Books
history.snippet: I love Half Price books however I have to only give them 4 stars due to there small store (most are larger with a better variety). This is the ONLY place to buy books or DVD's, they have a great selection at a much affordable price... hit up there clearance section for a super h...

history.name: Pita Pit
history.snippet: This place is just AWESOME!!!! They have great food, one cool coke machine and outside seating. Of course with it having the name pita in it they serve pita sandwhiches however these are not no normal Pita... they are Pita-tastic Pita's... I'm hooked on the philly pita... GIve e...

history.name: Oaken Barrel Brewing Company
history.snippet: One of my favorite Brewhouses/Resturants.. They have some great brews as well as seasonal brew. The food specials are always great as well. A must try in my book is Razzwheat beer unless the seasonal Apple Buzz is available. The idaho Nachos are always a hit in my book for an ap...

history.name: Kilroy's Bar N' Grill
history.snippet: Okay, here is my take.... this place by far has the worlds greatest Bread sticks (Seriously), they have really nice interior as well and a great variety of drinks but...WAY, WAY OVER PRICED! Really... how do you charge me 6 bucks for a Indy brewed Weemac, Sunking Beer? I get the...

history.name: Thr3e Wise Men Brewing Company
history.snippet: Yessss, I am a fan! Not only do they have Sunking beer but they have some great beer of their own! They offer FREE popcorn as an appitizer which I thought tasted awesome! Go with the Pizzza, it is hand tossed thin crust goodness. I'll be back!!!

history.name: Hal's Fabulous Vegas Bar & Grille
history.snippet: This is one of my new favorite places... They offer an excellent Bone in T-bone steak, this piece of meat has an excellent flavor. I would recommend the mac n cheese as the side... Spicy goodness! They have an excellent drink Menu ranging from local brewed Sunking to Shots in ic...

history.name: Jockamo Upper Crust Pizza
history.snippet: Just call me the duke! Why??? Because I'm an addict!!! I have to order more than one appitizer because I can't just settle for the AWESOME hummus plate because their breadsticks are as equally as AWESOME!!! I struggle to decide if I want a pizza or salad so I usually get both!!!...

history.name: Chick-fil-A
history.snippet: I've been here over 50 times and have never been disappointed! Drive through = fast and friendly service! Inside= great service, fresh food but at times a little noisy (kids) which doesn't bother me. You must try their Lemonade or Sweet tea! A+

history.name: Guitarworks
history.snippet: The only place to shop for Guitars! The owners here are great people who will do all they can to educate you and get you into the guitar you want. They have a nice on site rooms for lessons. I took lessons here for over a year and got a lot out of it. Fairly priced on lessons to...

history.name: Wingstop
history.snippet: I'm a wing addict, I mean I eat wings at least 3-4 times a week... Yes really. So this place has the word "wing" in the title so I had to give it a try. Plus the Yelp reviews were great... So when my wife ask do you want to go to wing stop my response was ABSOLUTELY! With that s...

history.name: Audio Electronics
history.snippet: These guys have the goods, they specialize in Car Audio but pretty much have it all... Back up cameras, Bluetooth connections, in car monitors, etc. I went in because I have a 2008 Silverado ext cab and I wanted a better sound. I have been wanting some Boston Accoustic speakers...

history.name: Skyline Chili
history.snippet: I love this place... not only does it bring Cincinnati chili to Indy but it brings the Reds! The place is decorated in my two favorite teams... Reds and Colts! Caution: The cheese they put on the Chili dogs is addicting!

history.name: Kahn's Fine Wine & Spirits
history.snippet: I have heard this place was the place to get wine. I really enjoy wine so I figured this is my type of place however I was a little disappointed. The inside of the building reminded me of a flea market. All concrete without any nice wine racks. After I spent some time walking se...

history.name: The Old Spaghetti Factory
history.snippet: This place is great... they have a nice antique interior with a fun atmosphere. I highly recommend Meat balls and the house "pesto" salad dressing!

user_persona: Philadelphia casual diner who likes quick uncrowded Mexican, ice cream variety, reasonable college-bar beer, fast service, and clean grab-and-go stops.

history.name: South Square Market
history.snippet: I've only stopped in here to pick up a few items. I suppose the place is clean enough, and the line has never been too long. That said, in the morning it smells absolutely AWFUL. I'm not sure what it is--some seasoning or something--but i want to kill myself the entire time I'm...

history.name: The Blarney Stone
history.snippet: Typical Penn bar filled with typical Penn kids. The beer prices are reasonable although the selection is not exactly vast. It can get quite crowded on the weekend and Thursdays. Basically, it's a decent if unspectacular college bar. If you want to hang out with Penn kids, this i...

history.name: Ben & Jerry’s
history.snippet: The first thing one notices upon entering this place is that it smells wonderful. Like all Ben & Jerry's, the ice cream is delicious and there tons of flavors & toppings from which to choose. The staff is generally fast & courteous, if a bit hipsterish. I've never had any compla...

history.name: Burger King
history.snippet: The new frozen lemonade thing is quite good, and it's pretty cool that this location has several computers with internet available for public use. I've never seen that before at Burger King, or any fast food location for that matter. Other than that, this BK is exactly what you'...

history.name: QDOBA Mexican Eats
history.snippet: I'm a big fan of this place. It is much less crowded than the Chipotle around the corner on Walnut. I normally order a steak or chicken quesadilla. Both are excellent. And on the rare occasion I indulge in a dessert, the chocolate chip cookies are also quite good. What's more, I...

user_persona: Tucson traveler who likes creative Mexican, cheap food trucks, patios, beer lists, hiking day trips, BYOB Asian meals, and ramen worth waiting for.

history.name: Cafe Poca Cosa
history.snippet: This restaurant was great and worth the hype. The portions are good and the have a limited menu that changes twice daily. The restaurant was busy and the food was delicious. We had the cod and entree sampler. I totally recommend this place!

history.name: Kimchi Time
history.snippet: Unfortunately we did not have a good dining experience. We ordered the stone pot mixed rice and spicy beef soup. The food tasted good but we found the married owners very stingy with their mini appetizer dishes. They avoided our table since they were irritated with providing mor...

history.name: Frog & Firkin
history.snippet: Just came by for drinks and appies on the patio. This is a great place for college kids to drink and hang. They have a HUGE beer selection. We ordered the cheese nachos and that's exactly what we got.

history.name: Mt Lemmon
history.snippet: Great place to escape the hellish heat. Lots of hikes, look-outs, picnic tables and most importantly ....cool temperatures. Check out the babad lookout point and observatory.

history.name: Arizona-Sonora Desert Museum
history.snippet: This is a great excursion from Tuscon or Phoenix. Come anytime from Oct to Apr so the two hour walk outside is bearable. It's a desert walk with lots to see and great for families and visitors. I recommend all parts of this place and the set-up is great for breaks, snacking, eat...

history.name: Saguaro National Park West
history.snippet: You'll only see this in Arizona. If you're in Tuscon head over for a visit to see Cactus till you drop! There's tons of hikes to choose from.

history.name: Downtown Kitchen + Cocktails
history.snippet: Had a pleasant dining experience. Enjoyed some popular appetizers and entrees. The dishes are unique to this restaurant who care about presentation and a myriad of flavors.

history.name: El Charro Cafe
history.snippet: This is a large place that can accommodate anybody. There is indoor and outdoor seating. We had the chicken nachos and street tacos. I recommend the chicken or carnitas street tacos.

history.name: Aqui Con El Nene
history.snippet: Great food truck. For something quick and cheap, this is your place. Bring cash cause nothing else is accepted. They have a condiment table that the owner keeps as tidy as possible.

history.name: Doma
history.snippet: Very nice ambiance for your Japanese/ Korean dinner. They allow you to BYOB and reserve a seat for this place. The staff are friendly.

history.name: Terakawa Ramen
history.snippet: Probably one of the best ramens I have had so far. Expect to wait for a seat at this place but it's worth especially on a cold day.

history.name: Anthropologie
history.snippet: My favorite clothing store but this flagship store is in a beautiful historic building. 4 floors of beautiful things to see. Check out the main floor, east of the cashier.

history.name: Whole Foods Market
history.snippet: Great location to get your "365" necessities and more. Went to Federal Donuts in the eatery, the donuts are freshly made and sooooo delish.

history.name: Spataro's Cheesesteaks
history.snippet: Long lines but needed to try one visiting Philly and eat indoors. Not the best but good enough.

history.name: Tommy DiNic's
history.snippet: This was a great sandwich joint. The meat is tasty and juicy. Saw tons of people enjoying this rich and tasty sandwich. Yummmm

history.name: Philadelphia Museum of Art
history.snippet: Enjoyed this beautiful museum. Take in the one hour collection your that is offered to get a quick glance if museum highlights. The museum has lots to offer and will leave you inspired.

user_persona: Edmonton food seeker who likes karaage, bubble tea, mac and cheese, chicken and waffles, and expects friendly acknowledgement at counters.

history.name: Hanaichi Express
history.snippet: Really great! My boyfriend and I went here for the first time and it was really good!! Ordered the chicken karaage and it was soft (idk if that was the intention) but it was good! My bf ordered the beef teriyaki rice bowl and I ordered the katsudon chicken rice bowl. I recommend...

history.name: Heeretea
history.snippet: Server was really rude, and idk if she was having a bad day but when we came in they didn't really say anything or acknowledge us. Then when we came to order, they were out of all the things we wanted off the menu which was about 8 items. Then I proceed to get the Oreo milk tea...

history.name: Coco fresh Tea And Juice
history.snippet: Love this bubble tea chain , and I would always come back to buy drinks here but this particular store often lacks on customer service. They are very rude sometimes and gives you look.

history.name: SugarBowl
history.snippet: Came here after a concert and the food was phenomenal. We ordered Mac and cheese and the chicken and waffles to play it safe. The chicken and waffles had watermelon slices in top which I thought were tomatoes but it was great flavouring !! Would come back here

user_persona: Santa Barbara diner who loves worth-the-wait seafood, cozy service, birthday meals, big breakfast portions, chicken fried steak, and good value.

history.name: Brophy Bros - Santa Barbara
history.snippet: After living in Santa Barbara for 3 years, I had to try the rumored amazing Brophy Bros for my birthday. Taste buds. And stomach. Mind-blown. I think this is the best restaurant I've ever been to in Santa Barbara. I've been told to that the fish and chips have fish like pillows/...

history.name: Yellow Belly
history.snippet: Yellow Belly has a cozy, cabin feel with friendly workers and great service. The ambiance is really charming. My friend and I thoroughly enjoyed the ricotta pizza, which was baked to the right crispiness, and the cheese blended the crust and toppings together beautifully. My but...

history.name: Cajun Kitchen Cafe
history.snippet: Service is great, big portions for a good price. All 5 of us got the chicken fried steak- the steak is very tender and the gravy on top is a perfect combo. It goes hand-in-hand with the choice of potatoes, eggs, and bread. TRY THE CORNBREAD!! WITH THE BUTTER OR APPLE SAUCE. Melt...

history.name: Brophy Bros - Santa Barbara
history.snippet: One of the only restaurants that I will give 5 stars. The 45 minute wait was even worth it, as the food quality is up to par, not to mention the walk around the harbor you can do to pass the time! Service is amazing, even when we felt like we bugged them one too many for more br...

user_persona: New Orleans diner who values tucked-away quality, reasonable portions, clean stores, real breakfast eggs, responsive managers, and restaurants that keep promises.

history.name: Cowbell
history.snippet: Food was pretty good and prices were OK, but as other comments pointed out, the tone of the menu (no more than xxx number of separate checks, don't even ask) is not amusing in the least at an establishment like this. That type of 'humor' is more appropriate at a family operated...

history.name: Charcoal's Gourmet Burger Bar
history.snippet: I had been eagerly anticipating the opening of this place for what seems like over one year. Unfortunately it was certainly not worth the wait. The girl at the register was very friendly, but the experience was downhill from there. First, there are two seating options downstairs...

history.name: Popeyes Louisiana Kitchen
history.snippet: Quite possibly the most bizarre experience I have ever had in a drive-thru. Let me start by saying the girl who waited on me couldn't have been either nicer or more polite. I arrived at 10:30 and ordered 5 spicy chicken tenders. I totally expected there to be a wait since they h...

history.name: Wendy's
history.snippet: I am not a huge fan of fast food, but this place actually serves what appear to be real eggs on their biscuits. They are actually different sizes each time and you can see the yolk. Also, the young lady who works the drive-thru window in the morning is quite possibly the nicest...

history.name: Rosedale
history.snippet: Excellent restaurant tucked away between City Park Ave and the railroad tracks. Very reasonable prices for the quality and quantity of food served. Truly enjoyed the Shrimp Puppies and the BBQ Shrimp. Will definitely be back there soon.

history.name: New Orleans Hamburger & Seafood
history.snippet: I have been in the drive thru for 38 minutes and still have cars in front of me. This is totally unacceptable. Go inside and place your order, or better yet, go elsewhere!!!

history.name: New Orleans Hamburger & Seafood
history.snippet: The manager (male) on duty the night I had this terrible service (12/22/2018) promised to send me a $25 gift card due to the inconvenience I experienced. I gave him my home address. Still waiting for that to arrive...................

history.name: Rouses Market
history.snippet: It's a filthy, foul-smelling store. The seafood is in the back, but you can smell it from the front door. If this store was in Houma, Lafayette or Baton Rouge, it would be closed because it would have no customers. I won't even buy dog food there. Fortunately there is a very cle...

history.name: Gianna Restaurant
history.snippet: Food was very good, but drinks were very expensive. Veal Saltimbocca was excellent and ricotta pistachio cheesecake was was heavenly! Very good service, but not a place for a quiet dinner!

history.name: New Orleans Hamburger & Seafood
history.snippet: Still waiting on that gift card..........one would think people would keep their word and not just pay lip service to a disgruntled customer.

history.name: Winn-Dixie
history.snippet: Store is clean, employees are nice and lines are usually short. The large grocery store across the street should send it's employees there for training.

history.name: Lafitte Greenway
history.snippet: Absolute waste of taxpayer dollars. It's pretty useless and causes traffic jams on major thoroughfares.

history.name: Hope Mausoleum
history.snippet: I feel terrible for the visitors seeking family and friends that had such awful experiences here. My grandparents are buried here, but I also have a couple of great aunts there as well as my sister in law's parents. I've never been approached by anyone when visiting, though I di...

user_persona: Newtown diner who likes quick high-quality pizza, local Asian takeout, shareable cheesesteaks, well-prepared seafood, patios, and attentive service.

history.name: Original Dominick's Pizza Shoppe
history.snippet: Just ok pizza. Nothing special about the ingredients that are used or the service or price. I would go to a Pizza Hut or other chain before I went here again.

history.name: Bonefish Grill
history.snippet: Very good service. We got the fish special and it was cooked well and prepared well. It was slightly fishy for a white fish but not bad enough to leave any on the plate. Good beer and wine options and a nice atmosphere.

history.name: Jade Asian
history.snippet: Don't let the old strip center fool you. This is a very good local Asian place. We got spring rolls, fried rice, and general tso chicken. The fried rice was very good and spring rolls were good as well. The chicken had a very good sauce but was the typical dark meat type you see...

history.name: State Street Kitchen
history.snippet: Great little place downtown. I got a Philly cheesesteak and it was delicious and huge. Plenty to split with someone or if you are really hungry. It was busy inside with only a few small tables but the food came out quickly and we got a table easily.

history.name: Jules Thin Crust Pizza
history.snippet: High quality ingredients and good quick service. A ton of fresh options with local ingredients for whatever you are feeling. I got a meat slice and a cheese slice. Both were delicious. The pizza is premade each day and they run it through a quick oven cycle when you order so it'...

history.name: The Green Parrot
history.snippet: Decent place with a large menu. A bit expensive for lunch but a nice atmosphere for dinner or a happy hour. Nice outdoor patio for good size parties. Consistent service and food, but nothing special overall food wise.

user_persona: Nashville diner who likes attentive bartenders, cheap beer, burgers, wings, neighborhood bakeries, morning treats, and restaurants that manage waits fairly.

history.name: Twin Kegs
history.snippet: Went in, was approached immediately by a spritely bar tender with blonde hair. Ordered the burger and wings, enjoyed both! "...cheapest beer prices in Nashville" True. Lots of variety too! I will be back.

history.name: Sweet 16th Bakery
history.snippet: Went here to get some morning treats as a thank you for a good friend. The place is small but really cute and welcoming..has a neighborhood vibe to it. The owner is amazing, very cheery and upbeat! Welcomed every customer with eye contact and a smile! ( a feat during morning rus...

history.name: Seafood Sensation
history.snippet: I just walked out-pissed. The first time me and my mom came here the food was awesome. Today I came expecting the same... I waited behind what I THOUGHT was a customer for about 10 minutes... To later find out her and the cashier were friends and just there to catch up on good t...

user_persona: Philadelphia diner who loves standout wings, mac dishes, Brooklyn-style square pizza, authentic ramen, and barbecue that avoids dry brisket.

history.name: The Blue Duck
history.snippet: The blue duck is delicious! Their wings are to die for. I love the blue moon flavored wings! Quack n Mac is a must order! I've brought tons of friends to this place from out of states and they all loved it! Hidden gem in Northeast Philly. You don't always have to go to the city...

history.name: Mission BBQ
history.snippet: this b. b. q joint is okay.... i've asked for moist brisket they gave me super dried out ones. and then i got it exchanged....and it was still super dry. they should definitely change their way of cooking the brisket. it's either over cooked or just not done right.

history.name: Joe Santucci's Square Pizza Bar & Grill
history.snippet: Awesome pizza, just awesome, I'm from Brooklyn NY. This is just like L&B but thin crusted. Cheese first then sauce!

history.name: Musashi Exotic Japanese Cuisine
history.snippet: Amazing Ramen, I've been to Japan and I've been searching for good Ramen ever since then. I've finally found it! I hardly ever write yelp review but the Ramen is a must have! The eel bowl is amazingly gooooood. Has a crazy buttery taste. Thumbs up!

user_persona: Spring City diner who likes remembered service, Queens-style pizza, craft beer, grilled cheese, honest local businesses, and restaurants that do not decline.

history.name: Crofts Tavern
history.snippet: We have eaten at Crofts Tavern a number of times. The food is very good and the waitress we usually get is fantastic. She remembers every dish and drink that we have ordered in the past, I usually don't remember what I have had so that's a hug help. Previous reviews talk about a...

history.name: Mediterraneo
history.snippet: Unless you are on 77th street and 32nd avenue in Queens, you aren't going to get a better slice. Alright, maybe if you want to take the trip to South Street and find parking there is Lorenzo's, but there is nothing else like this in the area. It's rare I get anything other then...

history.name: Crofts Tavern
history.snippet: We have been back to Croft's a few times since our last review and I hate to say it, but I think the food and service is going downhill. The staff is still very friendly and polite, I just think they are short staffed and spread a little too thin. Last time we went they didn't e...

history.name: Suburbia Family Cleaners
history.snippet: My wife dropped off my suit here to get dry cleaned without checking my pockets. We had just gotten back from a casino so I had a sizable amount of cash in the pocket. When I realized what had happened I figured it was a loss. My wife called the shop and the young lady said she...

history.name: Railroad Street Bar and Grill
history.snippet: This is one of those hidden gem type of places. If you are a craft beer person or like grilled cheese sandwiches this place is a no brainer. The grilled cheese sandwiches range anywhere from five bucks for a basic three cheese sandwich to a twenty dollar lobster grilled cheese l...

history.name: Champps
history.snippet: This review is long overdue, but I thought this place would be closed down by now. We dined here on September 24. This was by far the worst service I have ever received from a restaurant. It's rare that I write or read reviews this long, but with everything that occurred I can't...

history.name: Dunkin'
history.snippet: I have tried to give this spot a chance a number of times. It is closer than the other four DD in the area so out of convenience I have gone to this location a few times. I am very low maintenance and I always get the same thing, a black cold brew coffee. All you have to do is p...

history.name: J. Alexander's Restaurant
history.snippet: I was driving past this place last week and figured we would give it a shot. It popped up out of nowhere and I am always intrigued by a new restaurant in King of Prussia. There are so many eateries and some go above and beyond and last and others fall short and are out of busine...

history.name: Revolution Chop House
history.snippet: I have dined here when it was LP Steakhouse and it was good, but a little too pretentious for what it was. The service was good and the food was decent, but the layout represented something you should find on Market Street, not in a private casino in Valley Forge. Revolution Cho...

user_persona: Saint Petersburg diner who likes Cuban sandwiches, healthy fast-casual bowls, neighborhood bars, date-night atmosphere, pizza-deli lunches, and swift pleasant service.

history.name: Mike's Pizza & Deli Station
history.snippet: I checked this place out for lunch today and I gotta say I was pleasantly surprised. The place has that hardcore lunch/dinner atmosphere, but had tons of options on the menu. The staff was polite and surprisingly fast considering how many people were there. I got our sandwiches...

history.name: The Wayward Goose
history.snippet: A pretty spacious neighborhood bar. It has all of the finer bar life essentials including but not limited to; pool tables, darts and a digital jukebox. One of the really nice draws for this bar is that it's big enough to host a lot of local art shows which makes it a nice stop i...

history.name: The Living Room on Main
history.snippet: Fantastic atmosphere and decor at the living room. Went for dinner in a Sunday night w some friends and can defiantly see this as a place to take a date. The band up front was a bit loud but if you sit in the back you'll have no issues with noise. It's tapas styled but the meals...

history.name: Bodega
history.snippet: Best Cuban in Tampa bay. Portions are huge. Soda frescas are where it's at and the specialty sandwiches are always unique. Total Latin bodega feel without the bulletproof glass. Can't lie, I'm a regular.

history.name: Fresh Kitchen
history.snippet: Quality affordable good food that's good for you. The staff is always pleasant, service is always swift and there's always seating, even during the lunch and dinner rushes. The coconut rice is my favorite.

history.name: Florida Museum of Photographic Arts
history.snippet: This museum is fantastic and a staple of the Tampa bay photographic community. I've had the pleasure of being on both sides of the fence here. As a visitor the the fact that they have constant exhibits coming through both internationally and locally is a blessing. As someone who...

user_persona: Philadelphia-area diner who likes dog-friendly patios, top-tier burgers, overstuffed deli sandwiches, small sushi rolls, diner breakfasts, and polished steakhouse treats.

history.name: Bar Ferdinand
history.snippet: We sat outside, which was very comfortable and a nice atmosphere with so many people out and about. The whole area is very doggie friendly and some people even brought their dogs with them to dinner. -which was nice to see. The drinks... We happened to stumble into Bar Ferdinand...

history.name: Village Whiskey
history.snippet: My go-to spot in the city for a great burger. I have tried them all and this one is supreme. The real secret to this place is the Kentucky Hot Brown, yum! Open face turkey on buttery brioche with bacon and a mornay sauce. If you are not in the mood for the world's perfect burger...

history.name: Famous 4th Street Delicatessen
history.snippet: The sandwiches are so ridiculously overstuffed and delicious! This is the only deli in Phila that I have stumbled upon that truly feels like a NY jewish deli. The corned beef is homemade and very lean. Be careful when ordering fries, the portions are as large as the sandwiches.

history.name: The Corner
history.snippet: Went here with the ladies for Center City Sips Happy Hour event. Being a mild summer evening the roof top deck was packed and we were not allowed up. The restaurant has their own happy hour menu downstairs, so we decided to stay. We weren't too crazy about the Lady Belladonna; b...

history.name: Barclay Prime
history.snippet: This place could have easily gotten 5 stars from me ( and would have if I reviewed them after my past visits). I specifically come here about once a year or so and look forward to ordering literally the BEST steak I have ever had: the Kobe filet mignon. (& worth every penny) Dur...

history.name: Dublin Square
history.snippet: Meh, this place is just OK. The servers in kilts is a little silly and the menu is uninspired. I mean it doesn't take much to step up some potato skins... My chicken was overcooked, but the shepherd's pie was good. The evening that we were in there were more servers (most standi...

history.name: Tokyo Tasty
history.snippet: One of the best sushi restaurants in the area, albeit a little pricey. Rolls are a little small, but I actually prefer that. We get takeout from here often and its always fresh and delicious. Service is quick and friendly. Dont forget to bring your frequent diner card!

history.name: Meadows Diner
history.snippet: This is a solid Sunday morning diner breakfast choice. We arrived around 9:30am and this place was packed. We were seated immediately and the food came out super fast. French toast, pancakes, home fries, scrapple and eggs were all tasty. The portions are HUGE, so you will be fin...

history.name: SEPHORA
history.snippet: Your typical Sephora. Nothing too special here except the fact that it is never crowded since its in the Deptford Mall.

history.name: U.S. Airways Club
history.snippet: Yeah, this place is OK. I am not sure its worth the membership cost unless you travel alot or can expense it. There aren't too many USAirways clubs, but you will have access to other Start Alliance Gold clubs. *Even if you aren't a member, if you have a USAirways frequent flier...

user_persona: New Orleans visitor who likes lively Bourbon Street drinks, famous breakfast recommendations, fried chicken biscuits, and crowded tourist hotspots with strong atmosphere.

history.name: Ruby Slipper - New Orleans
history.snippet: I ordered a bloody mary and the fried chicken on biscuits for breakfast because the waitress said it was the MOST recommended. I thought the bloody mary was great and the biscuits were AMAZING, but the overall dish for being the MOST recommended was not impressive. The biscuits...

history.name: Pat O'Brien’s
history.snippet: THIS PLACE IS THE PLACE TO GO! It was soooooooooo crowded and loud and def the hot spot on Bourban street. The drinks were good and the atmosphere outside is really cool with a super pretty fountain. Overall a good vibe and tons of nearby bars.

user_persona: New Orleans diner who likes fantastic breakfasts, berry French toast, friendly dive bars, spicy burgers, fast seafood service, and fair value.

history.name: French Toast
history.snippet: Absolutely fantastic from the omelette with side salad to berry French toast! This is our first breakfast in New Orleans and I'm not sure I want to try anywhere else.

history.name: Felix's Restaurant & Oyster Bar
history.snippet: Service was fast and friendly. Grilled oysters were good not great as was the gumbo. Food and drink overpriced for what you got.

history.name: New Orleans Botanical Garden
history.snippet: No comparison to the ones in D.C.or Columbus, Ohio. I know it's October, but even at other times of the year I can't imagine it's much better. Staff was the least friendly of anywhere we've been so far in NOLA.

history.name: Buffa's Bar & Restaurant
history.snippet: Great little dive bar with the hottest burger I've had in a long time. Bartender/server was friendly, funny and enthusiastic.

user_persona: New Orleans diner who wants chef-level service, hot Cajun classics, shrimp po'boys, gumbo, cornbread, cookies, and restaurants that apologize for mistakes.

history.name: The Black Sheep
history.snippet: I highly recommend this restaurant. The service was fantastic and the food was tasty at every bite. The Chef personally introduced himself at the end of our meal and the staff took the time to make sure we had everything we needed! The duck was amazing!

history.name: Salt Cave Santa Barbara
history.snippet: If I could give them zero I would. I purchased a green tea mask from them, so did my 5 girlfriends. Once we got on the train heading back to San Diego I noticed mine had been previously opened and the seal was removed. I called that and three days that followed with still no res...

history.name: Salt Cave Santa Barbara
history.snippet: If I could give them zero I would. I purchased a green tea mask from them, so did my 5 girlfriends. Once we got on the train heading back to San Diego I noticed mine had been previously opened and the seal was removed. I called that and three days that followed with still no res...

history.name: Coop's Place
history.snippet: I heard so much about this place from Locals! I was super disappointed. My husbands chicken was cold, my soup was luke warm- Gumbo and with minimal seafood. I wont be coming back

history.name: Stanley
history.snippet: Great food! We had the po boy (shrimp) and the gumbo. Both very tasty!! I would recommend.

history.name: Willa Jean
history.snippet: Meal was delicious fro. Start to finish. The cornbread. Omg... the cookies and milk were amazing and a must.

history.name: La Petite Grocery
history.snippet: The food was good but took a long time to arrive and when it did it was cold. They warmed it, at our request and returned, with no appology or offers anything to make it right. Spending $100 on a meal with such service is not acceptable. I WOULD not recommend.

user_persona: New Orleans comfort-food explorer who loves great pizza crust, barbecue, fried chicken, catfish po'boys, gelato, pralines, birthday sushi, coffee, and live bars.

history.name: Tower of Pizza
history.snippet: All pizza starts with a good crust. This place has a great one. Plus fresh ingredients and you can watch Pizza being made right in front of you. This is probably one of the top pizza places I've had! I would suggest only eating in because it will be very soggy if you take it hom...

history.name: Mulate's
history.snippet: Was not impressed! The food was average at best, and did not fit the very expensive price. So it is way over priced and fish was not of good quality. Crab gratin was more bread crumb and cheese than crab. Plus twice baked potato was made a month ago and freeze dried, shipped to...

history.name: Katie's Restaurant & Bar
history.snippet: I very rarely give 5 stars but I have to because of the food!! Guy Fieti agrees, he has been there and raves about it. I have to start with the chargrilled oyster, I've tried them at several different places this place by far has the best! Second the blueberry ribs! Yes I know y...

history.name: Amici
history.snippet: Great location! Very pretty decor, and the wait staff were very nice and accommodating. The pizza I would give 3 out of 5. Not. Big fan of the sauce and toppings were kind of minimal. Coal fired crust is very good though and I enjoy a good crust!

history.name: Angelo Brocato
history.snippet: Best Ice cream/gelato I've had in New Orleans. The desserts are just as good. Step back in time and I'm telling you, you can not go wrong. It is right down the street from our church and we have gone last two Sunday's! What a fantastic treat for family! On top of everything, the...

history.name: Portside Restaurant
history.snippet: I have found paradise for food! Incredible onion rings! Fried chicken is the best I've had in Louisiana! Huge cat fish po-boy! Just order some stuff and split it! You will not be sorry! Actually calculated how far it is to here from my office so I can come for lunch!!

history.name: Bud's Broiler
history.snippet: Classic old school burger joint. Don't come here if your looking for McDonald's! They cook your burger over hot charcoal just like the grill in your backyard. So has that smoky flavor and chili is really tasty! I will return to try the smoky sauce. I didn't hear about how good i...

history.name: The Joint
history.snippet: Ok I never put 5 stars but I had too for this joint! I never have religious experiences for food but today I think I did! The most incredible BBQ that has ever passed my gums! Just incredible! It's all good!! So anyone visiting NOLA stay away! So there is more for me!!!!!! Moooo...

history.name: Brooklyn Pizzeria
history.snippet: Other than forgetting me to bring my pizza out. This is as close to getting. True New York pizza! I've had pizza in Manhatten and this is just as good! Big bubbly crust and good toppings. Place looks a little rough but it should for a true New York pizzeria! I will be back!

history.name: LA Smokehouse
history.snippet: Really nice set up and the service was excellent. It did not knock my socks off though. Meat was flavorful but really dry. Sauces were good. I enjoyed the spicy one. They were out of the house version. The sausage had more of a hot dog flavor than a smoked sausage flavor. Best i...

history.name: District Donuts Sliders Brew
history.snippet: This place is hip and fun! The donuts are top notch and the smell of yeast when you come in is wonderful! Haven't tried the sliders yet but I will soon!!

history.name: Ma Momma's House of Cornbread Chicken & Waffles
history.snippet: This is the place to come and get your chicken and waffles fix! Staff was beyond amazing and nice. Made sure we had everything we needed. The cornbread is so good! I could just order that and coffee and I'm good to go! I will be here quite a bit!

history.name: Blue Line Sandwich
history.snippet: Food is ok, not a big fan of the potatos. Plus it is very pricey. Over $30 for breakfast and you have to order at the counter?? Good quality of food and staff is nice..

history.name: Nirvana Indian Cuisine
history.snippet: Good quality food, not as much a selection for lunch buffet as I'm used too but it is very good quality and authentic flavored. Still looking for my favorite carrot halwa I know it's in this city somewhere!

history.name: Tee Eva's Famous Old Fashioned Pies & Pralines
history.snippet: Nothing like tee Eva! When you come here you are family. Yum she is a hoot! Her Pralines are fantastic, you can tell she knows her stuff! A must see if your visiting our fair city!!

history.name: Fat City Diner & Po Boys
history.snippet: New ownership has taken this diner over. They are doing a good job! Food is diner delicious! I have been here for breakfast and dinner and this should be on anyone's go to place for a quick quality meal.

history.name: The Velvet Cactus
history.snippet: Brought my brother here while he was in town. Had a pretty long wait but was not to bad, really enjoyed bar area and pre dinner drinks. The service was very good, our waiter was attentive and joked with us. Had the burrito and it was very good, not the best but good. Soup excell...

history.name: Sake Cafe Uptown
history.snippet: What a let down! I was excited driving by to come and check this place out! I always go by but never stopped. So this night I took the dive and stopped. The decor and facility is not bad, very comfortable and seemed to be busy for a week night. I had a pre dinner cocktail and it...

history.name: Rock-n-Saké Bar & Sushi
history.snippet: Came here for my daughters 16th birthday, the staff is top notch! The manager made arrangements for my daughters birthday and was so happy to see us! Made us feel special, she is a keeper!! We ordered several rolls and boom! "Sushi Bliss" they were incredible!!! Best sushi I hav...

history.name: Outlaw Pizza
history.snippet: Outlaw pizza what a great place to hang. It's pizza with a play attached. Great beer menu and tried the outlaw pizza, surprisingly was really good! Who would've thought crispy onions could make a pizza!! Not a big fan of the dough though, seemed to process and not much yeast lik...

history.name: French Truck Coffee
history.snippet: Great little coffee shop, easy parking and quick in and out. They take their coffee seriously! This makes it better than most coffee. Don't look for comfy couches and board games, just coffee.

history.name: Fry and Pie
history.snippet: Some really good stuff to hit my mouth! It's only two things ... Fry's and pie's, say what! Fantastic options and will leave you wanting more!!!

history.name: Red's Chinese
history.snippet: Ok so had the generals chicken and it had a great fry with lots of flavor! Kind of bummed it only comes with white rice, was hoping for fried rice but oh well. Had the ginger drink it was good but not balanced. The service was excellent, my waitress was very attentive. I finishe...

history.name: Bayou Bar
history.snippet: If you want to see true New Orleans magic come watch Phil on a Friday night.. This place is the best place in the city!!

history.name: The Company Burger
history.snippet: Nothing that would make me come back. It was just ok. There is a lot of salt and did not like the American cheese. Good service but just kind of missed the mark of how good I thought it would be. Onion rings are pretty good..

history.name: Pho Tau Bay Restaurant
history.snippet: Had a high hopes when I came here. Emeril said he loved the place. After visiting the fusion of pho and Asian just does not go well. I left hoping for more!! Don't think I'll be back or listen to Emeril anymore..

history.name: My Sister's Table
history.snippet: 45 minutes to get our food, people getting up to leave and one waitress for over 10 tables! Total dysfunction of a restaurant. The food I would give it a C- I ordered the packages and and each one had the taste of the other food being Cooked on the griddle. Never had a pancake w...

history.name: Eat New Orleans
history.snippet: Just walked in and had a delicious breakfast. Very courteous staff who accommodated us for breakfast, was in a hurry. Had ok coffee but the meal was excellent!

history.name: La Boulangerie
history.snippet: Best croissant and traditional French bites. Great coffees and service. I will return asap...

user_persona: Indianapolis day-trip diner who likes sports tavern lunches, helpful server recommendations, brewery patios, free parking, and casual meals after city events.

history.name: Coaches Tavern
history.snippet: We had a great lunch here. Stopped by after Indy 500 parade. Amber was our waitress, she gave me a great recommendation on the taco salad. It was sooooo tasty. Seasoned beef, I can't say about the chicken option. My hubby had his 3rd pork tenderloin sandwich in 3 days and said t...

history.name: Tow Yard Brewing
history.snippet: We stopped for lunch here, after riding our rental bikes around town. Great free parking if you're driving. We sat outside, had great service from sweet Melissa. I had the fish and chips. I am picky about fish and chips, it's either great or yucky. This was great. Hubby had the...

user_persona: Tampa traveler who likes high-value brunch, strong ambience, fair prices, surprisingly good airport meals, and lovely attentive waitresses.

history.name: Noble Crust
history.snippet: Stay still my heart! This restaurant has it all. Great food, great ambience, great prices great everything. While nothing on the menu costs more than $20, you would be fool to only order one item. This is gourmet food at it's best, in the guise of an informal bistro. Don't let t...

history.name: Payless Parking and Car Rental
history.snippet: Be very careful when you rent a car at this establishment. Or better yet, don't rent one. The staff is very dishonest. You have to initial extras you want on your contract. But if you say you don't want a service, and don't initial that you do, you still might find the charge on...

history.name: Green Iguana
history.snippet: I never go to airport restaurants expecting a good meal. So I was pleasantly surprised that the food was very good and our waitress lovely. My only complaint was that I don't believe restaurants in Florida know how to make a rare hamburger. Every time I've ordered one, it comes...

user_persona: Reno customer who values helpful retail service, quick quality Chinese food, immediate diner seating, and rejects rude drive-thrus or careless fast food.

history.name: Fabric Care Specialist
history.snippet: I've been a customer for several years. I have several reasons for rating them so highly, but one particular situation stands out. I left a wool trenchcoat for cleaning with them, and I ran into some financial challenges that prevented me from picking up the coat. Fully 2 years...

history.name: Journeys
history.snippet: Store had exactly the item I was looking for, and staff was super helpful and engaging. Even though I'm not their target market I plan on shopping here again.

history.name: Denny's
history.snippet: Went by for a treat since we normally have breakfast at home. Got there just before 'prime time' (9 AM). Was seated immediately and set up with coffee and water. Service was excellent and attentive the whole time. We noticed the staff was quick to handle throngs of people (lots...

history.name: Starbucks
history.snippet: If you want to be treated as an important customer at the drive up, go to another Starbucks. This morning the person who gave me my order (2 coffees) didn't offer a carrier even though it was clear that I needed one. And when I gave her extra for a tip she didn't even acknowledg...

history.name: Subway
history.snippet: I would give this location 0 stars if I could. Wanted a roast beef with chicken breast sub. The guy behind the counter said he "couldn't hear" me, while he was training someone else and speaking some sort of foreign language. I don't mind that, but it would have been nice if he...

history.name: Carl's Jr
history.snippet: Went in on a Saturday at 3:15. No one in the store (should have tipped me off). Ordered a double burger no cheese, fries and a soda. Burger was lukewarm, fries over salted and soda was watered down. And it took a while to get the food. I did complain to HQ and guest relations is...

history.name: Egg Roll King
history.snippet: Always have great food and quick service. Dishes are well seasoned, with higher quality than you would expect from a quick serve Chinese restaurant. We've gotten food there for years and think this is one of the best in town. Recommend highly. You won't be disappointed

history.name: Fabric Care Specialist
history.snippet: The pandemic forced the store to close. One of thousands of small businesses all over the country that were unjustly punished by a ham handed government.

user_persona: Reno traveler who likes inexpensive casual Indian buffets, great service, excellent lattes, and businesses that respond professionally to remote requests.

history.name: Safari Stores
history.snippet: In attempting to purchase an RV long distance, I wanted to hire a company to do a pre-purchase inspection. I was referred to this outfit. When I saw the yelp reviews, I decided to call to have a direct conversation with them. Well, the reviews are accurate, I'm afraid to say. Th...

history.name: Flavor of India
history.snippet: Wonderful Indian food! Great service. Buffet bar. Not expensive. Nice, casual atmosphere. Will be back!

history.name: Raging Sage
history.snippet: Down from Prescott for the gem and mineral show in Tucson. We just spent hours searching for, buying and tossing some of the worst lattes on the planet. We gave it one more try at Raging Sage. Wonderful! Good, full-bodied Latte and a delicious house crafted Chai Latte. No toxic...

user_persona: Philadelphia diner who wants complete salad orders, timely delivery, friendly fast-food service, and short waits that justify casual meals.

history.name: Taco Bell
history.snippet: Absolute poor service. Unfriendly staff. I've waited 40 minutes for my food now and it just wasn't worth the wait. I would suggest not eating here.

history.name: Francesco Steaks
history.snippet: I ordered the Fresh Greek Salad. They forgot my anchovies and eggs as listed in the ingredients in the menu. Also, the delivery driver seemed timid and uncertain if he had the right order. Overall, a pretty bad experience. I don't think I'll be ordering from here again.

user_persona: Reno social diner who likes welcoming Asian desserts, board games, craft beer, escape rooms, endless chips, strong margaritas, and satisfying burgers.

history.name: Asian Garden Restaurant
history.snippet: I had green tea ice cream here a couple weeks ago and it was yummy! The servers are welcoming and very nice as well. Make sure you go!

history.name: The Glass Die
history.snippet: Good games and good beer. I would definitely come back again. Especially for the cider that tastes like apple pie

history.name: Puzzle Room Reno
history.snippet: We escaped King Tut's tomb!!! The Puzzleroom is so much fun! The max amount of players is 6 people and you have 1 hour to escape any of the different scenarios alive. I'd definitely recommend going.

history.name: Steak ’n Shake
history.snippet: I came to this Steak 'n Shake location for the first time hoping to have a delicious burger, fries, and milkshake. Little did I know, not all Steak 'n Shakes are to be treated equally. The fries were cold and my milkshake still had chunks of solid ice in it. I do appreciate that...

history.name: Miguel's Mexican Food
history.snippet: When the chips are endless and the margaritas are strong you know you've come to the best kind of Mexican restaurant. Although I do hate paying extra for guacamole, it was pretty delicious and comes in in its own tortilla chip bowl. I got the strawberry margarita and the tequila...

user_persona: Indianapolis diner who wants transparent pizza pricing, friendly morning service, reliable early fast food, family-oriented Mexican restaurants, and self-serve salsa bars.

history.name: Piezanos Pizza
history.snippet: I called up an order for a 16" around the world, 20 wings, and an order of loaded breadsticks. Came out to $90.00+ I asked how they came up with that total and they told me half the special online weren't being honored due to changing of the owner. How is that possible, never or...

history.name: McDonald's
history.snippet: Worst customer service ever, two mornings in a row the cashiers has an attitude before I can open my mouth, then the second window drive thru boy, why work in customer service if you can't do your job, and you want $15/hr huh. What a joke.

history.name: Salsa Verde Mexican Restaurant
history.snippet: Their salsa bar is a draw for me. Get what you want when you want it. No need to wait for a server. Owners are very friendly, it's a family oriented place. If you like Mexican food this is your place. Generous helpings as well.

history.name: Burger King
history.snippet: 99% of the time in the mornings around 4:15 exactly their system goes down and they can't fulfill any orders. The few times they are up a drubbing about 90% of the time they're on a lunch menu at 4:15!!!!!! I keep trying it because it's in the way to work and I love BK breakfast...

user_persona: Tucson beer shopper who values craft selection, fair liquor prices, knowledgeable staff, strong brewery atmosphere, and reliable beer recommendations.

history.name: Total Wine & More
history.snippet: The best place in town for craft beer and liquor. Best prices in town. Staff is not that knowledgeable on beer, it's hit or miss, depending on who you talk to. My go to place for all things alcoholic!

history.name: BevMo!
history.snippet: Pretty good place. Not as good as total wine. Guys are more knowledgeable about beer than Total Wine. Usually higher prices, but sometimes lower. Always worth a visit!

history.name: Thunder Canyon Brewery
history.snippet: This place is awesome! Great beer selection, much better than northwest location. Atmosphere is great. Would definitely go back!

user_persona: Tucson diner who likes great breakfast coffee, customizable tacos, juicy burgers, friendly hosts, inviting decor, and Italian lunches with helpful staff.

history.name: Lure Fish House
history.snippet: I don't quite understand why this place has such good ratings. The food was just average, nothing to rave about. In fact, the food didn't have much flavor. I had to add lemon, tabasco, and salt to my Cioppino just to give it some flavor. My daughters ordered the Ceviche and the...

history.name: The Worker Bee Cafe
history.snippet: This place is awesome! The food is sooo good! You have to try the corned beef, so delicious! Coffee is magical, don't usually like mine black but could easily drink this stuff black. Definitely would go back!

history.name: Street- Taco and Beer Co.
history.snippet: Stopped here for lunch with my wife. This place was pretty dang good. Kind of like the Chipotle approach, where you choose want you want and add toppings. They have warm chips if you order more than 1 taco. They have a full bar! This place is pretty dang good!

history.name: Guillermo's Double L Restaurant
history.snippet: This place is just ok. Usually our fallback if Mi Nidito is to busy. The hostess staff was really friendly, but our server wasn't. Their chips come it nice and warm, but the salsa is just ok. I ordered the red chili carne, it was average. I wouldn't go out of my way to go here.

history.name: Graze Premium Burgers
history.snippet: Stopped in here yesterday for a quick bite to eat. The burgers are super tasty and juicy! Date I say, one of the best burgers I've had in a while. The sweet potato tots were delicious! Word of caution, the place only had a handful of tables; so if you are planning to eat in, you...

history.name: First Watch
history.snippet: Went here for breakfast this morning. The reviews are legit, this place is pretty dang good! I thought the decor was light and inviting, nicely done! I decided to try their Chickichanga. Very happy with that choice! The flavors work so well together, especially the chorizo and V...

history.name: Scordato's Pizzeria
history.snippet: Stopped in here for lunch on Wednesday. Great decor, friendly staff, and delicious food! We had a hard time deciding what to get because several items sounded so good. We ended up ordering the antipasto salad and two pizzas for 3 of us. Ended up being the perfect amount. The ant...

user_persona: Belleville regular who likes cheat-day comfort food, chicken fried chicken, family Mexican meals, familiar service, and barbecue that matches its price.

history.name: Texas Roadhouse
history.snippet: We went here for my cheat day and I got the chicken fried chicken and it had to honestly be one of the best ones I've ever had....it was delicious!! The rolls and the sides were good but definitely save your appetite for the main courses here!

history.name: Righteous Pig BBQ
history.snippet: Went there for lunch and when first arriving it smelled amazing but then seeing the prices I was like oh ok glad smells are free...then realizing they had me hooked we decided to eat anyway. The food was great even though the portions were small for price but by far the best thi...

history.name: Mariachis
history.snippet: I see all the bad reviews but I guess being a regular me and my friends and family get better service and better food who knows....but then again I get the nachos and it's hard to mess up chips with queso and chicken but the chips are always good and warm and the salsa is ok but...

user_persona: New Orleans celebratory diner who loves warm hospitality, superb pastries, breakfast sandwiches, dependable Lebanese favorites, polished Italian meals, and birthday-worthy experiences.

history.name: Jacques-Imo's Cafe
history.snippet: Came to New Orleans to celebrate my daughter's 13th birthday and have a girl's weekend with another mom and daughter duo. Jacques-Imos was recommended and we were eager to try a new place. We were greeted by the owner before walking in the door, whose hospitality was second-to-n...

history.name: Gracious Bakery + Cafe
history.snippet: Get your carb fix here! The pastries were superb--fresh and flavorful, disappointing to finish! The breakfast sandwich was delectable. The smoked cheddar and garlic aioli really took it over the top--I would 100% get it again. The avocado toast was tangy and refreshing and I wou...

history.name: Lebanon's Cafe
history.snippet: Hands down, our favorite Lebanese restaurant in our drivable vicinity. Probably our favorite restaurant in Nee Orleans. My husband and I started frequenting this restaurant when we lived in the area 15 years ago, and we still make a trip here when we can. The food is on point ev...

history.name: Vincent's Italian Cuisine
history.snippet: The best meal and experience I've had in a long time. Celebrating a birthday--we indulged and enjoyed ourselves. The calamari, Arancini balls, bruschetta, meatballs, were all cooked to perfection. All Of our party got a side salad with their meal with the house vinaigrette--the...

user_persona: Philadelphia food explorer who favors fresh Vietnamese, Asian fusion, seafood happy hours, giant thin-crust pizza, shareable small plates, desserts, and brewery afternoons.

history.name: Vietnam Cafe
history.snippet: My favorite Vietnamese restaurant hands down! I've lived in Philly and Atlanta, tried just about every Vietnamese restaurant in both cities and nothing comes close to the quality and fresh ingredients of Vietnam Cafe! I refuse to eat Vietnamese anywhere else until I come home to...

history.name: Oyster House
history.snippet: Great location in center city and the seafood is so fresh! I love the happy hour specials! Make sure you get here early because it fills up super fast for the after work happy hour, but if you have the time the late night happy hour isn't too full. They have cheap drink specials...

history.name: Lorenzo & Sons Pizza
history.snippet: A Philly tradition and must eat whenever you find yourself near South Street! The largest pizza slices I've ever had for $3 and they're NY style thin crust which is my favorite!

history.name: JJ Asian Cuisine
history.snippet: For a Harleysville restaurant you can't beat JJ's. I order their fried dumplings every time I'm there and could probably eat them everyday they're that good. The sushi rolls are great and the service is very nice.

history.name: Veekoo
history.snippet: My favorite Chinese restaurant! I've been coming here since I was a kid, it's an extremely friendly family run business with great serving staff. The food is always amazing no matter if it's Chinese, Japanese or Thai!

history.name: Serpico
history.snippet: The small tapas style dishes are great for ordering a bunch and sharing! It's a hard choice to order just a couple! Definitely recommend sitting around the open kitchen to see what they're preparing and get a better idea of what you want to order. The servers are knowledgable an...

history.name: Cucina Zapata
history.snippet: The short rib tacos are to die for, my favorite by far! So many flavors, it's worth the wait at lunchtime but get there early enough since they sell out fast! There's not a ton of short rib in the taco but there's a ton of everything else to make up for it.

history.name: Majolica
history.snippet: One of the best restaurants I've ever eaten at with menu offerings you won't find elsewhere, friendly staff and a romantic ambiance. Chef Andrew is truly an artist with his creations and pairings. It's always a treat to order off the menu and also receive exotic bites in between...

history.name: Bonefish Grill
history.snippet: Had a large group of people for brunch here recently. Enjoyed the Bloody Mary's and bang bang shrimp Benedict! Did not enjoy the slow service and the creme brûlée French toast was cold, small and flavorless.

history.name: SUGA
history.snippet: I recently watched a tv special about Susanna Foo, her son and this restaurant and was dying to try it as Asian food is my favorite. The location is prime on Sansom, the decor is beautiful, the customer service of the staff is some of the best I've experienced and the food was d...

history.name: Same Same
history.snippet: Thoroughly enjoyed eating here for lunch; the location, food and staff all deserve five stars! I highly recommend their outdoor seating, the garden in the back is very charming. We had the caramelized pork banh mi, the ba con heo lettuce wraps and the lemongrass ice tea. The ban...

history.name: Spuntino Wood Fired Pizza
history.snippet: Loved the menu of this place considering Margherita pizza is my favorite and they offer different variations of it. Called to order delivery but they only let you do that through the caviar app, which is good marketing for caviar but annoying to go through the download process j...

history.name: Sea Bar Restaurant
history.snippet: Good quality live seafood! Highly recommend asking about their specials because they are a steal! We ordered both specials the other day: 2lbs of crawfish for $10 and 1lb of stone crab for $10. It easily feeds 2 people and it's delicious! We had it done in the garlic sauce with...

history.name: T-Swirl Crêpe
history.snippet: Really cool crepe place with an awesome menu! Ordered the blueberry cheesecake and strawberry banana crepes, both delicious. I would recommend adding some chocolate to the cheesecake crepe, it wasn't terribly sweet. My favorite part was the custard cream and the chocolate sticks...

history.name: Sampan
history.snippet: I love Sampan purely for the location, atmosphere and food. I've had off experiences though like the ac not working or not being on full blast when it's hot outside. For some reason they use an ingredient that makes me dry heave after I eat, I thought it was something in the pad...

history.name: Luna Café
history.snippet: I would give their food four stars because we enjoyed it and it's a cute place with outdoor seating. The issue is that they are next to a dog adoption center that puts dogs in the front window so they are constantly barking and being loud while you're sitting next door. The serv...

history.name: Dim Sum Garden
history.snippet: Love the food here, they are always packed and the food comes out as its ready. A few must orders include the shrimp dumplings and pork noodles! They're famous for their soup dumplings which are yummy but are so big they will fill you up quickly. Also enjoyed the wonton soup, li...

history.name: The Franklin Fountain
