Used to bring houses guest to our favorite place, but now look for alternative restaurants. Last Friday nights burger was d...

history.name: Enzo's Italian Eatery
history.snippet: Pasta w oil, spinach and broccoli. Very good! Super casual....always big servings and price performer! Don't go for the atmosphere, but plan on taking some home....

history.name: IL Granaio
history.snippet: Mussels in red sauce.....fantastic. I let the waiter talk me into small portion....mistake!

history.name: Moro Restaurant
history.snippet: Used to love this place. What happened? First, ordered a house Cab.....response: "no red wines" Second, The giant vine collection in wall was gone. Finally they did provide a "red blend" Third, The signature dish, Short Ribs, was not available. No crowd, hardly anyone in restaur...

user_persona: Tucson diner who likes creative sushi fusion, fresh custom calzones, attentive service, local coffee artistry, kind employees, and restaurants trying something new.

history.name: Umi Star
history.snippet: This is not a sushi restaurant!! Everyone keeps saying that they go for the sushi and are disappointed, but that's because it's not sushi at all! It is a new twist on sushi that Tucson hasn't seen yet. The chef took sushi and our everyday cuisine and brought the two together. Ev...

history.name: Magpies Gourmet Pizza
history.snippet: Magpies is amazing. Let me start by saying the service blew me away! My waiter was kind and so helpful! He really paid attention to all of our needs and gave the best service I have had in a long time. But really the star of the show was that calzone. I made my own with fresh ba...

history.name: Savaya Coffee Market - Williams Centre
history.snippet: I love this place! Such a great atmosphere. I really believe that a good cup of coffee is an art form and they seem to have created a gallery of art within Savaya. I love to support a local business and the kind employees are just the cherry on top of this wonderful establishmen...

user_persona: Santa Barbara experience seeker who likes huge-flavor boba, knowledgeable wine tastings, bubbly bars, humorous service, and budget-conscious local help.

history.name: Santa Barbara Auto Stereo & Wireless
history.snippet: I searched for a long time for a way to put an auxiliary input in my car that was within my budget. Due to the complications with my car's stereo system, all the estimates I was getting were close to $1200. Kevin and Mike helped me find something more in the range of my budget w...

history.name: 805 Boba
history.snippet: If you're looking for an explosion of flavors and variety in your mouth, go here. They have a HUGE selection of boba drinks, and the ones I have tried are PHENOMENAL! If you're driving through Santa Barbara, stop and get a boba iced tea. If you live in Santa Barbara, stop by the...

history.name: Oreana Winery
history.snippet: Ty was the man! Great sense of humor, great service. We stopped by for a quick tasting and ended up staying for another glass. He was quick to answer our questions, and was very knowledgeable about the wines.

history.name: Santa Barbara Winery
history.snippet: Jason was the MAN! He was hilarious and knowledgeable about the wines as well. I would highly recommend coming here for the jokes and ambiance

history.name: Champagne Room
history.snippet: Great list of bubbles ranging from champagne to prosecco & everything in between. I would recommend it to couples to check out before or after dinner or to groups that are looking to start their night off right in SB. Scott, who may have been the owner, guided us into the most d...

user_persona: Nashville lunch worker who wants reliable early breakfast, saucy Italian sandwiches, fair-value barbecue, drink refills, and seafood that is safe and prompt.

history.name: McDonald's
history.snippet: apparently only open for 23 hours and 50 minutes. Went in the early morning to grab some breakfast before work, the drive thru was open, but no one answered to me at the menu board. I drove to the window and had an employee greet me. I told them their speaker must not be working...

history.name: Jack's Bar-B-Que - Nashville
history.snippet: Honesty I was a bit disappointed. The BBQ wasn't much better than cafeteria food considering how much money you pay for a meal here. It was good but not worth bucks me and my husband paid to have an entrée with two sides. Sorry, I won't be back unless the price is dropped reason...

history.name: Coco's Italian Market
history.snippet: Coco'a Italian market started a food truck across the street of my work on west end, just off of Broadway. I went during lunch and got a sausage and peppers sub. I typically don't like dry sandwiches and I usually have to have a lot of sauce, but this was the exception. The saus...

history.name: Batter'd & Fried Boston Seafood House
history.snippet: Made me sick. When we first went in, it was very busy. They were quick to take out orders but it took forever for the food to come out. Plus they never once re filled out drinks so by the time we got our meal we had nothing to wash it down with. The people behind us never even h...

user_persona: New Orleans diner who likes unbeatable taco happy hours, house-made margaritas, reliable Cajun tables, takeout, and clean efficient counter service.

history.name: Araña Taqueria y Cantina
history.snippet: Aranas has definitely found its footing on Magazine street across from the iconic local Breaux Mart. The happy hour is out of this world! 11 dollar pitches half price tacos and other items. Can't be be beat especially since it's M-F 3-7pm! House made marg mix and quality house t...

history.name: Katie's Restaurant & Bar
history.snippet: Love this restaurant. Bar tenders can be snobby and absorbed in their own convos but TOGO or table seating is alway A+

history.name: The Big Cheezy
history.snippet: This is my third and last time eating here. Ordered the turkey pesto that was not "cheesy" and made with the cheapest quality deli turkey meat. BUT the worst part was the spicy pie sandwich came out with NO pepperonis on it. My wife discovered this after she had taken her first...

history.name: The Halal Guys
history.snippet: Service was lacking. Young man didn't change his gloves after he touched POS then went right to serving food and touching the food. Liked the vibe of the downtown store more.

history.name: Smoothie Time
history.snippet: I got the coconut supreme and it was watery. No thickness to the smoothie at all. The lady was sweet but helpless. She used sugary coconut syrup... loaded with sugar and calories. And she didn't have a lid for her blender. It's was all very weird.

history.name: Blue Line Sandwich
history.snippet: Can't rate the food because we walked out before we ordered. And that was AFTER standing in line for 15min. The poor lady behind the counter had no clue how to work the register and the young lady, Maddy, was too busy bussing tables taking to go orders and cleaning to assist her...

user_persona: New Orleans nightlife visitor who likes great-value pizza, wide draft lists, flavorful arepas, midnight Irish pubs, energetic karaoke, and bartenders who keep nights moving.

history.name: Slice Pizzeria
history.snippet: Slice is everything you're looking for when it comes to satisfying that pizza craving. The pricing is great, draft selection is vast, and the atmosphere is great. Definitely recommend for a nice afternoon to grab a slice of a NOLA.

history.name: Maïs Arepas
history.snippet: Yum yum in my tum!! Got the arepa sampler and it was great, just wish they were a bit bigger. My favorite was the pulled pork arepa, though the shredded beef and chicken were great too. Not too spicy, definitely full of flavor. I really liked the fact that you have the opportuni...

history.name: Molly's Irish Pub
history.snippet: Really dig the atmosphere, came by at midnight on a Tuesday and the bartender rocks. Suggested some Irish car bombs, and it was exactly what we needed to keep the night going. Definitely going to come back to Molly's next time we're in town! P.S. Bartender has a great taste in m...

history.name: Cat's Meow
history.snippet: Cats meow drew our attention while we were walking down Bourbon. The karaoke hosts do an awesome job of keeping everyone pumped and singing, that's for sure! One downside is how long it takes for your name to be called to sing but there's not much helping that, especially in the...

user_persona: Nashville diner who likes impressive cocktails, creative balanced entrees, rich-but-light food, standout bar programs, and restaurants where plates match the drinks.

history.name: Husk
history.snippet: Summary: Impressive cocktails, but odd choices on the entrees, make for an experience where the bar outshines the plates & service. What I Had: Cornmeal Porridge, Short Ribs, Duck. Cornmeal porridge was delicious, hands down the thing I enjoyed the most. This is a must order. Th...

history.name: Maurepas Foods
history.snippet: TL:DR Summary: Go for drinks, better food found elsewhere (e.g. Oxalis). Not the same Maurepas as a year ago. Maurepas used to be my go to spot in New Orleans. The food was creative, rich but not heavy, their menu well balanced, and their drinks outstanding. Before going a coupl...

user_persona: New Orleans visitor who likes affordable wings, memorable po'boys, brass-band nights, classic jazz bars, and local food without punishing waits.

history.name: Seersucker Restaurant & Catering
history.snippet: Visiting from out of town... enjoyed a Beef/Gravy Po Boy. It was reminiscent of a Chicago Italian Beef (Meat and Juice are thicker) so I certainly recommend trying one. It wasn't till later that night that I was wishing I had some more to chow down on. They were very accommodati...

history.name: Parkway Bakery & Tavern
history.snippet: Had a large Shrimp Po' Boy. Was overflowing with nice medium size shrimp. Wish they had a small order of fries to add on/make it a combo, as one order is enough for two people. You know there arent any locals coming here, as they wouldn't stand in line for 45 MINUTES to get wait...

history.name: Manchu Food Store & Chinese Kitchen
history.snippet: Visiting from out of town. You gotta love a place where you not only get panhandled while going in, but also while you are eating your food in your car, lol. Ordered a 10 piece wing meal for like $6. What a deal!! Comes not only with the wings, which are breaded with a nicely se...

history.name: D.B.A. New Orleans
history.snippet: TREME BRASS BAND!!! What a party band!!! Doesnt get any better! Just be prepared to eventually elbow your way into the main concert room... like a can of sardines :-)

history.name: The Maison
history.snippet: The Jazz Vipers were very good. Some great New Orleans Jazz, especially the Clarinetist... though I do believe the lineup changes from time to time.

history.name: La Boulangerie
history.snippet: I made a special trip across town to patronize La Boulangerie. Don't make the same mistake! I understand it is a Bakery/Patisserie and its first come first served until they run out, however it is also a business, and any business open for decades should have done better. The bu...

user_persona: Tucson home cook and pet raw-feeder who values fresh meat alternatives, informative staff, kind service, and dependable university-run specialty shops.

history.name: CCGA Meats -  UA Meat Sciences Lab
history.snippet: Get the dog food roll, great for raw feeders and homecookers for the pets also, really nice people that operate this cool university run business!!!!!!!

history.name: CCGA Meats -  UA Meat Sciences Lab
history.snippet: The people at the meat lab are so darn happy, at least when I go there, they are very nice and informative as well, will never stop going there to get meat and such, thank you U.of A. for this fresh meat alternative.

user_persona: Creve Coeur family diner who wants friendly Southern food, amazing chicken, biscuits, kid-friendly fries, clear hours, and service that handles waits.

history.name: Pei Wei Asian Kitchen
history.snippet: We were very disappointed. We eat here often and have always had great food and great service. I called with today being a holiday to see what time they closed. Was told 8:30, website & door say 9:00 pm. We arrived at 8:15 and chairs were up on the table. Other guest were orderi...

history.name: Lotawata Creek Southern Grill
history.snippet: Long wait due to carry out orders. The server that does drinks & apps while you wait, does carry out orders. So no drinks while you wait. Service was slow once we were seated. Server spilled an Jus all over the plate the French dip was on. Food was average at best. I don't think...

history.name: Southern
history.snippet: Outstanding. Not sure what we liked best. The chicken was amazing, you must get the biscuit, it's for 4 people. The chicken tenders were delicious. The kids loved the fries. The man & cheese was awesome! Extremely friendly staff. Highly recommend. Super fun & fantastic food. Did...

history.name: Arch Apparel
history.snippet: Very cool. Very fun. They take all our favorite parts is STL and put it on apparel. Shirts are great quality, super soft. Big store. Lots of cute shirts, hoodies, hats, stickers. Stop outside for a family photo. Outstanding selection, great website & super friendly staff.

user_persona: Tampa social diner who likes Cuban classics, mojitos, fresh Indian lunch specials, quick friendly value, good group drinks, and birthday outings.

history.name: Columbia Cafe on the Tampa Riverwalk
history.snippet: Food and drinks were incredible! No complaints. We had the famous 1905 salad, short ribs and mahi Cubana. Everything was perfect. We also had a pitcher of mojitos which were great. The only complaint here is whoever the mustached man in charge. He was GLARING with the most inten...

history.name: Cafe Masala
history.snippet: Amazing lunch special Monday-Friday. For $6.95, you get your choice of 2 curries, rice and a piece of fresh naan. There are 6 curry options to choose from including a variety of chicken and veggies. Everything tasted homemade and fresh and I will definitely be back! Quick, frien...

history.name: American Social
history.snippet: I went with a large group for Brunch on a Saturday. The service was pretty good considering how large our group was. The drinks at AmSo are always good. But I was extremely underwhelmed with brunch. It was super average and very overpriced. I paid $15.5 for a cold cut turkey san...

history.name: Kraken CycleBoats
history.snippet: I went on a paddle boat trip with a group of 16 for a friend's birthday. We had SO much fun. Our crew Shannon and Logan were super friendly and helpful and made sure everyone had a great time. It's BYOB and you can play or own music. They even let us stop at Rick's on the River...

user_persona: Brandon group diner who likes huge breakfasts, efficient servers, thin-crust Italian, martini deals, steakhouse portions, prime rib, and leftovers.

history.name: The Egg & I
history.snippet: Al ost didn't come here because of negative reviews!! My servicer was Mandy and she was efficient and left an extra carafe of ice tea on the table. My food came within 5 mi Utes and was Huge--an omelet big enough for at least three adults. The grits were fine so I have no compla...

history.name: Bernini Restaurant
history.snippet: Wow, first time eating here with a group of 16, every dish we had was fantastic and if you like really thin crust pizza here they make it. I had Duck Risotto that was fantastic and even good when I heated the left overs the next day. Our waiter-Paul-was fantastic, we had $2 mart...

history.name: Black Rock Bar & Grill
history.snippet: We went to Black Rock yesterday, my clam chowder had a shell in it and my lasagna, while it looked delicious, it tasted like someone forgot they already seasoned it and added a load more in the sauce--I couldn't eat it. My son and girlfriend had steaks and she had mashed potatoe...

history.name: Jesse's Steak and Seafood
history.snippet: Ate here with 9 friends yesterday--I had the prime rib, medium rare, that was delicious and their special was pot roast and mashed potatoes that I took home to hubby-there was about 2 lbs--crazy, we had dinner and enough left for about 3 more meals.

user_persona: Santa Barbara coffee explorer who likes artful cafes, cold brew, brunch sweets, subtle ice cream, breakfast burritos, coconut cupcakes, and welcoming local patios.

history.name: Old World Coffee Lab - Midtown
history.snippet: Great coffee! Highly recommend this coffee shop to all who enjoy a GREAT cup of joe. I had the Orange Blossom Latte, which is perfect for the fall season. It was still really warm outside to really appreciate it as much. Also tried the cold brew, which was very good and would ge...

history.name: Catalyst Coffee Bar
history.snippet: Love the coffee house and art mixture. It's a little ways out of downtown, almost hidden gem. I enjoyed the cold brew and the donuts that they have in the mornings (had the red velvet donut). My husband had an iced tea (can't remember the flavor) and he really enjoyed it. Love t...

history.name: Rooster - Downtown
history.snippet: Great spot for breakfast and/or brunch. We came here during a crazy conference, but the service and food was good. My husband enjoyed the caramelized bananas and nuts French toast. My nieces enjoyed the Nutella pancakes and strawberry and Nutella crepes; i ordered the rooster sl...

history.name: Sugarfire Smoke House
history.snippet: This place is BOMB! Great smokehouse located in the heart of downtown St. Louis. The employees are so kind and give a sample of debating between meats and sides. The portions are very large! I got a kids meal and it was more than enough for me. They have different flavors of BBQ...

history.name: Lucky Penny
history.snippet: Came here while we visited SB as it came recommended by a friend. There was no line when we arrived, but it was pretty late in the evening. They have outdoor seating and heaters if it gets colds. Overall it's a really cool ambiance and Covid compliant We ordered the OG pizza, th...

history.name: McConnell's Fine Ice Creams
history.snippet: First time trying out this place. To be fair, i am not an ice cream lover, so it's really hard to be impressed with ice cream. I tried the sweet cream caramel brownie and their vanilla bean. I expected it to be overly sweet and surprisingly it wasn't, the flavors were more subtl...

history.name: Cajé Coffee - Arlington St
history.snippet: Hands down, best experience from my SB trip. Husband found this place for me since he knows how much i love trying coffee houses when I travel. I personally was hesitant at first, their drinks didn't sound too good and the price is steep. But I'm so grateful he convinced me! Amb...

history.name: Los Agaves
history.snippet: Came here because it came highly recommended by so many as one of the best Mexican restaurants in SB. After trying it, I'm wondering what kind of Mexican food these people have had? Husband and I had an early dinner here. He ordered the Burrito Santo Domingo with mole negro and...

history.name: Crushcakes & Cafe
history.snippet: Came here on a recommendation from friends. We got 4 different flavors: Funfetti - don't recommend, overly sweet and icing overpowers the cupcake. (Husband said he wouldn't recommend it to his worst enemy lol.) Coconut - really good! Great coconut flavor and not overly sweet. My...

history.name: The Daily Grind Coffee & Tea Station
history.snippet: Great breakfast burrito! Ordered the spinach burrito and added bacon and it fed two people; we also enjoyed the zucchini carrot muffin. We got mocha and a coffee to enjoy alongside our food. Enjoyed eating outside and feeling like a local. It was definitely getting busy after 9a...

user_persona: St Petersburg traveler who likes nostalgic breakfasts, scratch-made Mexican sauces, generous gelato, spa-hotel service, refreshing birthday cocktails, oysters, and beer-bar snacks.

history.name: Cracker Barrel Old Country Store
history.snippet: As you walked into the store, it has rocking chairs out the front for sale. The store reminds me when i was a kid seeing all kinds of old fashion candies in Disneyland. There is a restaurant located at the back and their breakfast is amazing. I got their granola pancakes and it...

history.name: Red Mesa Restaurant
history.snippet: Upon arriving St. Petes, I was told on many different occasions one of the place to visit is Red Mesa. The food was so delicious. This isn't your typical Mexican food. The owner, who is the chef prepared all his sauces from scratch. We ordered: Shrimp fundido - yummy Calamari Tr...

history.name: Paciugo Gelato & Caffe
history.snippet: This has to be one of the top Gelato places I have tried. The sea salt and carmel is the best! These gelato gets shipped directly from Italy and the portion size they served is plenty and pretty generous for a small size. I'm drooling as I'm typing this review!

history.name: The Vinoy Renaissance St. Petersburg Resort & Golf Club
history.snippet: The services are top notch. I was upgraded to a spa room where the room is bigger and I had a jacuzzi out on my own patio. Their gym room is not bad. It gets the job done and have plenty of cardio machines. Adjacent to the cardio/weight room, they have a spin class with an actua...

history.name: Tryst Gastro Lounge
history.snippet: I celebrated my birthday at Tryst and they have tons of beer and drinks selection. One of my favorite drink which I can't stop talking about it is called green ivy. It's such a refreshing drink. Volka, cucumber and basil. It's so delicious.

history.name: The Oyster Bar
history.snippet: This place has pretty decent oysters, but not the best. When I hear oysters bar, I think of a different variety of oysters you can select from. In a way, I was disappointed. I ordered their oyster stew and it was delicious. It's quite rich and it can be an entree on its own. I a...

history.name: The Ale & The Witch
history.snippet: This dive bar reminds me of back home bar in San Diego O.Beach. If you are a beer lover (which I'm not), this place has soooo many beer selection. I would highly recommend this place to you. I ordered the mahi spread and it was delicious.

history.name: Steak ’n Shake
history.snippet: When you're in the mid-west, steak n shake is a must. I know living in California, we don't have a steak n shake so when I do come across one is like a gem when I see one during my traveling. I was actually surprised to see one this south in Florida. I love their burger and espe...

user_persona: Philadelphia Asian-food explorer who favors authentic Chinese, Korean, Japanese, hot pot, sushi, desserts, reservations, generous portions, and clean service over salty or fake dishes.

history.name: Kumo Asian Bistro
history.snippet: Fake Japanese food and expensive price. Food material is not fresh and the taste is greatly oily. Don't choose it no more!

history.name: DuBu
history.snippet: Nice tofu dish and steak！ I and my husband all love this restraunt. Not only the dishes are delicious, but also the waiters (waitresses) are super nice.

history.name: Pin Wei
history.snippet: This is a classic Chinese style restaurant near by King of Prussia. Recommend to the fish dish. It's very delicious. BTW, if you want order a kind of typical Chinese dessert, the fried cake is best.

history.name: Fiorino
history.snippet: For our anniversary, we chose this Italy restaurant that rank Number 1 in East Fall. There are just ten desks inside, but so many customers in the restaurant. Recommend highly the fried chocolate ice cream dessert.

history.name: Megumi Japanese Ramen & Sushi Bar
history.snippet: After watching a ad. from internet, my husband wanna eat noodles, so we go to this ramen restaurant. However, we just fell ok. This is because it is not very good.

history.name: Ooka Restaurant
history.snippet: This is a popular Japanese restaurant in Willow Grove. Remember to reserve table in advance before arrival.

history.name: H Mart - Elkins Park
history.snippet: If I must choose a best Asian supermarket, Hmart is my favorite. There are some Asian cafeteria in the upstairs. Strongly recommend!

history.name: Gogi BBQ
history.snippet: We chose B BBQ meal. There are 12 types meats you can choose. We tasted 6 types of them. You also can choose various seasonings and vegetable. Besides one stone pot steamed egg and tofu soup are free for every table. The taste is good but the price is a little bit expensive.

history.name: Spirit of Philadelphia
history.snippet: This restaurant is a cruise. The trip starts from Penn's Landing at 6pm, about two and a half hours on the water. You will experience good view, cool party, romantic night, but poor buffet.

history.name: Xi'an Sizzling Woks
history.snippet: This is a Chinese restaurant in University City. It is located in the corner of 40th street and chestnut street. The taste is ok.

history.name: Happy Lamb Hot Pot
history.snippet: Little sheep is a classic Chinese hot pot restaurant. We drove a half hour to arrive at the restaurant for a meal. Sure enough, it did not disappoint us.

history.name: Morton's The Steakhouse
history.snippet: Druing this restaurant week, I reserved a table yesterday. The restaurant is located in walnut street and 14th street near by City Hall. Dark environment, pretty waitresses and dressed up customers. I enjoyed the time. However, the food here is OK.

history.name: Ra's Very Eatery
history.snippet: This Sushi restaurant is close to ACME markets. The landlady is super nice. According to our requirement, she asked the chef to make specially a sashimi, caviar and seaweed salad platter for us. It's very delicious.

history.name: Umami Umami
history.snippet: Close to 276 highway. There are six tables here around 8:30pm. We ordered one seaweed salad,13pcs sashimi and one seafood yaki soba noodle. Although the sashimi is thin and the waitress is cheerful a little bit, we still feel good.

history.name: ZARA HALAL GRILL
history.snippet: This is a Middle East style restaurant. It is located in Philadelphia Franklin Mills near by AMC movie theater. Waitresses are super nice, patient and beautiful. We ordered a lamb curry and a combo over rice. The food is yummy and the price is reasonable.

history.name: Terakawa Ramen
history.snippet: Ordered a spicy ramen. I felt too hot to enjoy it's taste. Maybe other type will be better.

history.name: J-mart
history.snippet: This is a new Asian supermarket. It's very neat. There are many fresh foods and food ingredients here. I bought a $100 gift card, just to pay $90. The price is reasonable. I love it!

history.name: Ooka Japanese Sushi & Hibachi Steakhouse
history.snippet: It's close to J-mart, just to walk 3 minutes between the two stores. Food is good, but I prefer to eat in the other Ooka Japanese restaurant located in Grove Willow. The waitress is so nice and the price is reasonable.

history.name: Hippot Shabu Shabu
history.snippet: The hotpot soup is not fresh and too hot to taste! the sauce is oily. Not very nice! It's no good and not a normal Chinese style hotpot restaurant.

history.name: Jong Ka Jib
history.snippet: This is a great Korean style restaurant. I think it's best in Philadelphia, especially tofu's food. My husband and I always come here to enjoy delicious cate. If you love Asian food, this is a good place for food.

history.name: Bai Wei
history.snippet: I had a Thanksgiving meal with my friends in this renovation Chinese restaurant. There are not only fashion decorations, but also very delicious food. This restaurant is very neat and dainty. Highly recommended！

history.name: Nagomi
history.snippet: Today is Black Friday. My husband and I didn't have a lunch until 3:00pm. We ordered some lunch special. It's delicious we felt. Just the sashimi were thin a little bit.

history.name: Hachi
history.snippet: This restaurant located in New Britain Village Square. Actually, the chefs and waitresses are Chinese. It's not a real Japanese restaurant. The food is good, and the price is reasonable.

history.name: Thai Kuu
history.snippet: This is a Thai style restaurant near by chestnut hill station. We ordered a fried rice, a curry and two deserts. It's delicious. We enjoyed the lunch very much.

history.name: Seorabol Korean Restaurant
history.snippet: This is a traditional Korean style restaurant. There are lots of famous photos hanging on the wall. The food tastes good.

history.name: Xiang Gourmet
history.snippet: This is a great Sichuan cuisine restaurant in Chinatown. It's closed to Jefferson Hospital. The food is delicious and authentic. Highly recommended!

history.name: Terakawa Ramen
history.snippet: Ordered a spicy ramen. I felt too hot to enjoy it's taste. Maybe other type After one year, I tried the small size miso noodle. This is better than before. The price is reasonable.

history.name: Roberts Block Restaurant & Bar
history.snippet: This restaurant is located at Glenside station. We ordered three appetizers and one toasted Duck Breast with plum. The food tastes great, but the price is expensive a bit.

history.name: Dim Sum & Noodle
history.snippet: This restaurant has various kinds of Chinese food. There are dim sum, noodles and dishes. The dim sum is better in this restaurant.

history.name: Dan Dan
history.snippet: This is American style Sichuan cuisine restaurant. A friend commended it. We ordered fish, beef, kale and Dandan noodles. All of all, I feel the dishes so so. However, I like this loft style decoration and the waitress is so friendly.

history.name: Ooka Restaurant
history.snippet: This is a popular Japanese restaurant in Willow Grove. Remember to reserve ta in advance before arrival.

history.name: Ting Wong Restaurant
history.snippet: A friend commanded this Chinese restaurant. I took out a rice with sauced pork from the restaurant. It's with big portions, but small prices. The taste is good, but a bit dry. If there is a soup with the rice, it will be perfect.

history.name: Papaya
history.snippet: This restaurant posts Thai style. It is actually a Vietnamese style restaurant. Recommend the Blueberry Cheesecake that's a classic French dessert.

history.name: Ooka Japanese Sushi & Hibachi Steakhouse
history.snippet: It's close to J-mart, just to walk 3 minutes between the two stores. Food is good, but I prefer to eat other Ooka Japanese reslocated in Grove Willow. The waitress is so nice and the price is reasonable.

history.name: Megumi Japanese Ramen & Sushi Bar
history.snippet: After watching a ad. from internet, my husband wanna eat noodles, so we go to this ramen restaurant. However, we jufell ok. This is because it is not very good.

history.name: Manayunk Brewery
history.snippet: In the Manayunk Restaurant Week, we chose the Manayunk Brewery Co. The restaurant looks like a factory nearby Schuylkill River. We had one for each of us according to the restaurant week menu. I preferred the Sirloin steak & desserts. Highly recommended!

history.name: Chubby Cattle
history.snippet: This is a hot pot restaurant, but not a authentic hot pot. The restaurant style just cater for Americans tastes.

history.name: Marzano Ristorante
history.snippet: A zabaglione restaurant is located at Jenkintown district. There are three parking lot around the restaurant. Parking is easy. We ordered a mussels soup, pear salad, roasted salmon, whole fish and desserts. I strongly recommended the pear salad and tiramisu.

history.name: Pho Street
history.snippet: It rains suddenly. I happen to be sheltering from the rain in this restaurant. The restaurant is close to Reading Terminal and clear. Just using machine to pay, and then picking up from the bar. It's very convenient. The taste is good and the price is reasonable.

history.name: Terakawa Ramen
history.snippet: Not bad! There is a kind of the small size noodle in the restaurant. Suitable for lunch.

history.name: Surah
history.snippet: A Korean BBQ restaurant is close to AMC 309 Cinema. We ordered the combo #B that's the all you can eat. We chose seven kinds of foods to roast. We were very full. The price is reasonable.

history.name: Fogo de Chao Brazilian Steakhouse
history.snippet: This weekend, my husband brought me to the Brazilian steak restaurant. Some waiters who hold kinds of steaks were walking through in the hall to cut meat for customers. Interestingly, you need to use a sign card to show them. Green side means you want meat. On the contrary, red...

history.name: Fogo de Chao
history.snippet: My husband and his colleague came here for a business dinner. That's wonderful. Everyone loves the food. The restaurant environment is good. The price is a little more expensive than the other one where is close to King of Prussia.

history.name: Café Soho
history.snippet: Actually, this is a sports bar, not a real Korean restaurant. The food is not good. Won't be back!

history.name: Cantina Feliz
history.snippet: The Mexico style restaurant is located at the corner of 276 and 309 road. Today is the Memorial Day. Some restaurants are closed. We chose this one because it has great reviews and still been open. The food and environment are Ok.

history.name: 2nd Story Brewing
history.snippet: For European Rugby Champions Cup, We chose this sports bar to eat and drink. There are 4 screens on the first floor. We sat near the bar. It's convenient to watch TV. The food and the beer are great.

history.name: Fat Salmon
history.snippet: My husband's college-mate come to Philadelphia for a business trip. We reserved this Japanese restaurant for dinner. The food is delicious, especially is the green tea tempura ice cream.

history.name: Amada
history.snippet: The Spanish style restaurant is nearby Penn's Landing. It's located at Old City and very popular. My husband and I ordered 7 dishes. The taste is good but the price is a bit expensive.

history.name: Cozara
history.snippet: The restaurant is located at chestnut in University City. The food is too salty to eat.

history.name: Zakes Cafe
history.snippet: This is a homemade restaurant located in Fort Washington. The food is delicious, especially the desserts. We two spent $90, not cheap.

history.name: Subway
history.snippet: The location is great near Market street. The food is a kind of regular fast food, but better than the anthers. It's good for lunch.

history.name: The Metropolitan American Diner & Bar
history.snippet: After the class, my husband and I hadn't enough time to cook. We chose the restaurant that the reviews are great. Highly recommended for the 28days dry ribeye!

history.name: Wendy's
history.snippet: It's the dirty floor and tables. Waitresses are not nice. One star is just for the vanilla frosty.

history.name: Sushi Hatsu
history.snippet: The restaurant is located in Ambler. You can park nearby parking lot. The food is fresh, but the dishes are soooo small. And, honestly, we didn't have enough dinner. In that way, the price is expensive.

history.name: Shaxian Delicacies
history.snippet: The merienda store is close to Friendship Arch and located at Chinatown Main Street. There are two spaces, one for taking out, one for dining in. I ordered a Beijing meat sauce noodle. It's good. The price is reasonable. The other dinner time, I also ordered a noodle. This one i...

history.name: Frozen Rolled Ice Cream
history.snippet: The cool ice cream restaurant is nearby Chinatown Friendship Arch. I ordered a S'more with three topping. There are mochi, toasted mush mallow and some gummy bears. But I should change the gummy bears to a kind of fruit, I think it's better.

history.name: Terakawa Ramen
history.snippet: I wanted a reman from University City before. Unfortunately, the reman restaurant is close. I was looking for the other one in Center City. Sure, I come here again. This time, I ordered a shuyo reman and a rice roll. Feels very full!

history.name: Terakawa Ramen
history.snippet: This one Terakawa restaurant is located in University City same as the Chinatown one. I ordered a signature ramen and a Yakoyaki. The taste is okay. I prefer to the other Terakawa in Chinatown.

history.name: House of Thai Cuisine
history.snippet: I ate dinner alone tonight. Maybe because I don't spend much money, the service is so offish. In addition, the food is not very tasteful, and the hygienic conditions are not good. However the Thai style restaurant is so so. 'Cause it's not gonna be me anymore.

history.name: Pho Viet Hoa
history.snippet: Ordered a P11 Ph. The taste is so so. After half an hour, I feel very salty in my mouth.

history.name: Dim Sum Garden
history.snippet: The Chinese dim sum restaurant is unsanitary. There are some tin foil inside the meat. Be careful!!! If you eat tin foil, the consequence is not conceivable. It's terrible!

history.name: Tamarindos Restaurant
history.snippet: Delicious food, great wine and wonderful music. I love this restaurant, but it's so Inefficient. I hope that efficiency will be high in the future.

history.name: Nadia's Thai
history.snippet: The mushroom soup is salty a little bit, but the steaming salmon taste is so light that is like no flavour. P.s. A waitress gave me a wrong dish. I just took a bite, the waitress came over and took the wrong dish into the kitchen. However the waitress immediately came out from k...

history.name: The Concourse at Comcast Center
history.snippet: Comcast center has two floors and one basement. There is a circle theater at the second floor. It's worthy to watch.

history.name: Nan Zhou Hand Drawn Noodle House
history.snippet: The Chinese style ramen noodle restaurant is located in Chinatown. I ordered a mixed beef noodle soup to take out, but when I came home the noodle already have been stuck together. Especially, everyone must pay cash. This is a cash-only store.

history.name: Marzano Ristorante
history.snippet: The restaurant is very popular. Unless you book a seat, there is no seat. Today, we ordered Cozze Mediterranean, D Bistecca and Baramundi. The amount of food is full. Very nice!

history.name: E Mei Restaurant
history.snippet: The Sichuan cuisine restaurant is remolded. The style is more fashion than before, but the dishes are less.

history.name: Le Pain Quotidien
history.snippet: The cafe' is nearby Art Museum. The decoration style looks like a factory loft. We ordered some tea and dessert. I like the chocolate mousse cake. The aftertaste is great.

history.name: The Drake Tavern
history.snippet: This is a sports bar and located in Jenkintown. Cross the street is my favorite Italy restaurant. Today is my birthday. The waitress sang a birthday song for me. I am really happy! BTW, the tuna salad is delicious. Highly recommended!

history.name: R2L
history.snippet: It's my birthday meal at R2L. Beautiful view and nice service, it's a wonderful night!

history.name: The Drake Tavern
history.snippet: My friend and I came to the restaurant for lunch yesterday. We ordered the tuna salad again. A waitress recommended a kind of cheese stick that taste is delicious. Nice happy hour!

history.name: Scarpetta
history.snippet: Today is Restaurant Week last day. I made a reservation for 5 people. The restaurant has good view, but our table was not next to windows. We dependent on the restaurant week menu to order some food that is delicious, especially yellowtail salad and chocolate cake. That's good!

history.name: Zavino
history.snippet: The bar is located in University City nearby Drexel University. My friend and I need a place to wait for a friend. The bar has a wonderful outside tables, and there is a great view and space. We ordered two juices that's good, but the outside environment is great.

history.name: Plaza Artist Materials & Picture Framing
history.snippet: The art materials store is close to Drexel University. There are lots of art materials to choose. I always come here twice a month.

history.name: Xi'an Sizzling Woks
history.snippet: The Chinese restaurant is located in Chestnut St. and 40th. St. Close to the free library. My friend and I were from City Hall to come here. It's lunch time, so we chose the restaurant. We ordered some buns and Liang Pi in sesame sauce. The food is delicious, especially BBQ lamb...

history.name: U-Town
history.snippet: U-Town is a Korean restaurant in University City. There are lots of kids noodle, stone pots and BBQ food. The taste is good and the price is more expensive than the ramen restaurant across the street.

user_persona: Philadelphia food shopper who wants reliable coffee, good grocery prices, efficient checkout, strong pizza, reasonable beer lists, and Manayunk spots worth their cost.

history.name: Dunkin'
history.snippet: Well. If you are looking for poorly prepared orders, and being hastled by homeless drug addicts while getting your morning fix; you're in luck. This particular location appears to have an issue with "Medium Coffee, cream and sugar please". When i finally got my coffee it was med...

history.name: Pathmark of Germantown
history.snippet: Not a terrible place to shop. Prices are good, selection is good. Parking lot is somewhat easy to get in and out of. Downside: Out of 11 cashier lanes, I've never seen more than 3 open at any one time (including lines 10 deep in all of them). Management seems slow to respond to...

history.name: The Couch Tomato Bistro
history.snippet: went here with a few friends. Beer selection is "Meh". We had a pizza which while not terrible, is no where near as good as In riva. Prices were comparable to local eateries in the area. Its manayunk, probably not good to expect cheap food here. \Bottom line, decent place in a c...

history.name: In Riva
history.snippet: This place is amazeballs. Their pizza alone is worth the trip. Now shut up and eat here.

user_persona: Indianapolis diner who likes vegetarian-friendly cocktails, house-made Mexican, cupcakes, food trucks, fair pricing, and polished service that waits until everyone finishes.

history.name: Black Market
history.snippet: Wow! Great cocktails, good service and tasty food. They don't have a ton of vegetarian dishes but the ones that are are slam dunks. One of my favorite spots in town.

history.name: The Flying Cupcake
history.snippet: I just eat their cupcakes 3 days a week, no big whoop.

history.name: Bakersfield
history.snippet: Get here. as soon as you can. The food is really tasty! Simple, quality, and reasonably priced. Our server told us everything's made in house and it definitely comes through in the food and especially in the tortillas. Solid service - I love when you're waiting for a table and a...

history.name: R Bistro
history.snippet: The food is excellent, no doubt about it. And it's reasonably priced for what you're eating. My only complaint is that the servers and/or bussers clear plates before everyone at the table is done. It's a minor detail but when I'm out at a nice place where I'm *dining* it's a bum...

history.name: Duos
history.snippet: Not just the best food truck in town but some of the *best food* in town. I love them so much their truck is catering our wedding! Find this truck (or their cafe at Meridian and 30th) and RUN to it as quickly as you can!

user_persona: Philadelphia happy-hour diner who likes pork banh mi, kimchi skewers, outdoor bars, hotpot, wagyu beef, enoki mushrooms, and attentive servers.

history.name: Sampan
history.snippet: Awesome happy hour.5-7pm 4 dollar pork bahn mi was my favorite. And bbq kimchi skewers though a small portion is worth every bite!! Will come back more for the happy hour here and maybe even sit outside at the graffiti bar area. Tip: sitting at the bar outside (@grafitti bar) is...

history.name: Chubby Cattle
history.snippet: Great hotpot place in philly. David was a great server. would recommend this spot! Gotta get enoki mushrooms, wagyu beef and the tomato oxtail soup base.

user_persona: Philadelphia comfort-food seeker who loves clean friendly takeout, large soul-food portions, water ice, cheesesteaks, Cubanos, Vietnamese fusion, Indian food, and Jamaican oxtails.

history.name: Giuliano's Deli
history.snippet: I've been going here for breakfast and lunch for years, and have never been disappointed. My coworkers love their hoagies so much, that I find myself ordering hoagie trays on most occasions. Keep up the great work guys!!!

history.name: Jim & Rita's Fine Cuisine
history.snippet: Good Lord this place is amazing!!! Great food with very large portions. I don't take pics of food anymore, but maybe next time I will. Great job folks. I'll be back very soon.

history.name: Shere-E-Punjab
history.snippet: I found the food bland, the meat tough and the tandoori shrimp was dicey. Won't come back here. I've gotten better Indian food from the gas station near me.

history.name: Joseppi's Water Ice
history.snippet: Great service and a very clean establishment. Excellent water ice and ice cream. I got a strawberry shortcake sundae and it was amazingly good. I usually don't partake in that type of dish.

history.name: Ms Esthers Breakfast
history.snippet: I ordered a omelet from here at 1:30 in the afternoon and the food arrived at 2:53. And as soon as my fiancé took a bite into the omelet she got a mouthful of eggshells. Do not order from here!!!!

history.name: Pagano's Steaks
history.snippet: Don't believe the hype. I went here twice and got below average food. Service was great the first time but the second time it was sub par. I've gotten better steaks in the burbs.

history.name: Jimmy G's Steaks
history.snippet: What a difference a year makes. This used to be my favorite cheesesteak establishment. Last year the steaks were great and so was the customer service. I don't know what happened in the last twelve months. I just got back from the eagles game and decided to swing by here on my w...

history.name: Peerless Pest Control
history.snippet: My experience with peerless was more like a love hate relationship. I hated dealing with their customer service. They are rude, condescending, arrogant and all round a bunch of assholes. I actually hung up the phone and was ready to walk away from my deposit. However, when the e...

history.name: Oregon Steaks
history.snippet: I've been here twice at 4 am in the morning. Very friendly and quick service. I'm not a very big fan of over chopped up cheesesteaks and this is my place of choice. The cheesesteaks here are addicting for the most part.

history.name: Papaya
history.snippet: Wow!!!! Now I know what crack feels like. The food here is amazing!!! If you are looking for a big bowl of pho you will be seriously disappointed. What you have here is a fusion of Vietnamese cuisine mixed with a plethora of other cultures. Excellent service and a friendly staff...

history.name: Pura Vida
history.snippet: I've been getting their Cubano sandwich for years and they have never disappointed me. Best Cuban sandwich I've ever had. Keep up the great work guys!!!

history.name: Wendy's
history.snippet: They are very slow here and do not seem to care. Furthermore, they screwed up everybody's order.

history.name: Hong Kong Kitchen
history.snippet: I ordered food for take out last week and got the worst attitude over the phone. It was almost like they didn't want my business. When I arrived to pick up the food the cashier was very unpleasant. He seemed shocked that I didn't tip him. Nasty service=no tip!!!

history.name: Biryani Bowl
history.snippet: Very clean establishment with super friendly service. Every time I order from this restaurant I'm always pleased with the food. I highly recommend this place over all other Indian restaurants in the immediate area.

history.name: Il Boscaiolo Italiano Ristorante
history.snippet: I ordered take out from this new establishment this past Friday for lunch. I must say the food was incredible, the service was excellent, and I'm extremely impressed with the cleanliness of the restaurant. I'm looking forward in eating here every week and getting take out when I...

history.name: Jamaican D's
history.snippet: Very clean establishment with excellent service. And quite frankly, the best Jamaican food I have ever had. I'm coming back tomorrow for the oxtails.

history.name: Sang Kee Peking Duck House
history.snippet: This place has gone so far downhill over the years that i find it shameful to actually admit I was here. The service was fast, but the food was bland and unappealing. I've gotten better Chinese food in the suburbs to be quite honest with you folks. I ordered spicy squid that you...

history.name: Ron's Caribbean Cuisine
history.snippet: Ewww!!! I got attitude as soon as i placed my order. Let's see if the food was worth the grief.

history.name: McDonald's
history.snippet: The workers here are the dumbest bunch of idiots I have ever laid eyes on. Every time I come here it's always a clusterfuck with these idiots.

history.name: BBQ Chicken PA
history.snippet: Personally, I enjoy kfc or Popeyes over what this establishment serves. I felt like the food hear was not tasteful nor original.

user_persona: Meridian shopper who likes charming farmers markets, ripe local produce, friendly owners, clean open kitchens, chewy pizza crust, quality cheese, and helpful staff.

history.name: Wagner Farms
history.snippet: This place is so stinking adorable I wish it were in my backyard. It's a charming farmers market with a great selection of in-season local produce that is ripe and delicious!!! My sense is that the selection changes throughout the year so if you are looking for specific produce...

history.name: Marco's Pizza
history.snippet: The staff is wonderfully friendly and helpful. The pizza tastes great. Nice chewy crust, good quality cheese. Their sauce is also some of the best I've had. I love that the owner often greets you. You can see into the cooking area and everything is clean. I think it's a family o...

user_persona: Sparks diner who craves sushi, cucumber-roll flexibility, supportive takeout service, standout Benedicts, perfect eggs, and creative rolls with bright citrus flavors.

history.name: Sushimi's
history.snippet: Had dinner there last night. Requested that sushi be rolled in cucumber instead of rice, and the chef said they dont do that, which I was surprised at. Sushi was ok, service was ok. Took awhile to be acknowledged and offered beverages.

history.name: Ijji Sushi
history.snippet: I ordered today as a to go because I was CRAVING sushi. During the call the person who worked with me provided me with best deal possible for what I ordered and was very supportive when I asked for extra sauce, and minor modifications to my order. I really appreciate our local s...

history.name: Squeeze In
history.snippet: So this place is tiny. But man alive the food is amazing. I got the Bacon Benedict and I regret nothing. The eggs were perfect and the potatoes I got had cheese and sauteed onions on them y'all. SAUTEED ONIONS. Food was just outstanding, and even though we were socially distanci...

history.name: JJ Sushi
history.snippet: Cannot recommend this place enough. Must try: (Obviously EVERYTHING) but the Pink Lady, the Knight, and the Tasty Pop. Cannot tell you beautiful they are! The Tasty Pop and the Night have this beautiful lemon zest to them, and taste BEAUTIFUL with a Bottle of Butter Chardonnay (...

user_persona: Edmonton local shopper who values gluten-free comfort food, authentic Mexican, vegan poutine, specialty tea, local boutiques, cupcakes, and exceptionally helpful service.

history.name: Fuss Cupcakes
history.snippet: Fuss rules! I stopped in during busy Christmas shopping to pick up some goodies and the guy working was so helpful! He helped me pick out a great gift and while talking about my favourite cupcake (the flapjack), encouraged me to take a break from my frantic shopping and enjoy on...

history.name: Lush Cosmetics
history.snippet: Lush is the best! They recently changed their hours (and I didn't know) so I showed up with only minutes to spare. The staff were awesome and let me come in and grab my necessities. She encouraged me to take my time and still let me get everything I needed, even though they were...

history.name: Mars & Venus
history.snippet: Shop local. You can't find this level of service at the mall! The Mars & Venus team made my Xmas shopping. My man had come in shopping for me (everything he bought I loved of course) and mentioned he really liked a shirt they had. I went in a few weeks later and Jo showed me the...

history.name: El Cortez Mexican Kitchen + Tequila Bar
history.snippet: I've been meaning to check out El Cortez since they opened and finally made it in yesterday for a late lunch! Some inconsistency with their hours online vs on the door vs what they actually were and we were killing time until they opened and happened to walk by and see they were...

history.name: Acquired Taste Tea Company
history.snippet: Super cute little shop with friendly and knowledgable staff! Popped in for a refill on my favourite Earl Grey and ended up leaving with so many fun new flavours! I love that all of the teas are available to smell at my own pace without having to ask for help unlike some of the b...

history.name: Szechuan Sweet Mango
history.snippet: I love Sweet Mango! It's hard to find take out food with gluten free options, they have so many choices on the menu. I'm always happy with my celiac friendly food and my friends who can eat the gluten also like the food. The Lemongrass Veggies are unreal and I order the Pad Thai...

history.name: HUMA Mexican Comfort
history.snippet: Hands down, my favourite restaurant (not just Mexican, over all favourite!). If you are looking for authentic, this is the place to go! Everything I've had there is amazing, the service is great, and super helpful and it doesn't hurt that the decor is super cute! I'd suggest mak...

history.name: La Poutine
history.snippet: Thank you for existing. Thank you for being you! Thank you for having vegan and gluten free poutine and for being there when I need you. You never let me down and I don't know what I would do without you!

user_persona: Philadelphia nightlife visitor who likes hidden alley bars, moody outdoor ambiance, worthwhile drinks, and expects simple fast-food orders to be accurate.

history.name: Graffiti Bar
history.snippet: This is a great little behind the alley bar. Nice ambiance with minimal lighting, black accents and gaffitti walls. The outdoor space creates a nice brace way. Drinks are a little pricey but overall a great pit stop on your journey through the night.

history.name: Sonic Drive-In
history.snippet: My simple order of Sonic burger with mayo, fries and a Coke were too hard for these workers. I received Sonic burger with mustard, Tator tots and a Coke.

user_persona: Santa Barbara shopper who wants personalized tennis gear, fast pharmacy service, excellent Italian pizza, pandemic reliability, and longtime favorites to keep quality.

history.name: The Tennis Shop of Montecito
history.snippet: My favorite tennis shop! Purchased a new tennis racquet and had it strung to my specs within 20 minutes. It was amazing to be able to select a racquet based on my personal needs (zero vibration was a must so as to not aggravate old injuries) , then be able to talk to the stringe...

history.name: CVS Pharmacy
history.snippet: This cvs is the best. I used to go to cvs in montecito because it was closer to my home. But the cvs in Carpinteria is much more accommodating , friendly, and fast. No long lines. And the pharmacist - very professional. And for the record, they are next to a large Albertsons.

history.name: Via Vai Trattoria Pizzeria
history.snippet: Great restaurant. During COVID-19, it is our go-to place. Their pizza is to die for. Chicken parmigiana is amazing. And their Italian wines... great place to go.

history.name: The Nugget
history.snippet: Since the Nugget changed management, the food has been all down hill. Thus used to be a favorite, but no longer. So sad.

user_persona: Treasure Island traveler who likes reliable beach restaurants, generous portions, excellent breakfast gravy, Italian markets, desserts, coffee, deli foods, and grouper sandwiches.

history.name: Ricky T's Bar & Grille
history.snippet: This place has served us well twice now, 1st time we came 2 years ago was great ! In area again & searched this out because the food is excellent, staff wonderful, portions fantastic!

history.name: Sea Turtle Restaurant
history.snippet: Great breakfast , very reasonable, excellent biscuit & sausage gravy, rarely find this good ! Omelette with beef hash was absolutely amazing.

history.name: Mazzaro's Italian Market
history.snippet: This is a Must Stop , has the best desserts , coffee bar , fun food finds , excellent deli & ready to take home food . Has a Shop too next store , be sure to visit.

history.name: Frenchy's Rockaway Grill
history.snippet: Very good food , excellent beach location, nice people, average prices , the grouper sandwich was spectacular!

user_persona: Reno diner who likes reliable Chinese lunch deals, sushi, Mexican food, strong margaritas, group reservations, and servers without attitude.

history.name: Peony Chinese Cuisine
history.snippet: Always delicious. This is a great restaurant and I'm glad they're in the NW part of Reno. Usually go for dinner but just went last week for lunch and they have incredible lunch deals. I might have to go back tomorrow for the ahi tuna salad - so good! I love this place.

history.name: Babe Matteri's
history.snippet: My experience was alright here, not outstanding - just OK. A group of 7 ladies went here last weekend for a dinner. We had reservations, the table was ready for us. Server was kinda snotty - that needs to change. "I've never split a bottle of wine 7 ways" in a rude tone. Maybe j...

history.name: Abel's Authentic Mexican Cuisine
history.snippet: So happy to have finally dined here. Delicious Mexican food! And wow - the margaritas are bueno too. Really like this place. Yes, another poster mentioned the decor... it is kinda strange when you walk in looks like it wants to be a sports bar - but there's an entire back area t...

user_persona: Indianapolis service customer who values exceptional digitizing support, attentive historic dining, period ambiance, fried oysters, Madeira chicken, drinks, and staff who please customers.

history.name: ScanCafe
history.snippet: ScanCafe's customer service is one of the best I've ever encountered. They go out of their way to please their customers. I highly recommend them for all your digitizing needs.

history.name: City Tavern Restaurant
history.snippet: Beautiful historic restaurant where staff where period costumes. Great ambience, very attentive staff, and delicious food and drinks. I had the cornmeal fried oyster appetizer and chicken breast Madeira entree and both were delicious. They have an excellent drink menu. Highly re...

user_persona: Tampa diner who likes energetic bartenders, warm managers, clean casinos, attentive beer pubs, generous Thai portions, and retro ramen variety.

history.name: Islamorada Fish Company Restaurant
history.snippet: We sat at the bar , our bartender Ty hit the ground running, she was extremely friendly and energetic she also told us about the food specials and also explained the weekly happy hour prices !! The manager Frank also had a warm vibrant personality We will definitely be back , we...

history.name: Seminole Hard Rock Hotel & Casino Tampa
history.snippet: Awesome clean hit the ground running food and service !! Only complaint some female bartenders at the main bar are stuck up and think it's all about them instead of the customers, bartender from Hawaii not very friendly and the blonde that works with her !! Everything else and a...

history.name: World of Beer
history.snippet: A great place for family, friends and your fur babies !! The manager Amanda hits the ground running !! Amanda and Kristen our two of the best servers and are very attentive !!

history.name: Thailand Restaurant
history.snippet: Great atmosphere. The food is unbelievable it not only looks good but tastes even better, the portions are big and the prices are unreal this restaurant has had the same owners for over 40 years !! The service is awesome!! I will be back !!

history.name: Kiwami Ramen and Sushi
history.snippet: Great retro atmosphere, if you love ramen this is the place for you, they have a lot of varieties and a lot of ways to add on from mushrooms to spice the meat !! This place is located in a area that looks almost abandoned, I had to use my GPS to find it !! Very unfriendly staff...

user_persona: Fishers diner who likes bug-free patios, smoked wings, mini grilled cheese, great servers, and restaurants that treat customers fairly before payment.

history.name: 101 Beer Kitchen
history.snippet: The food is delicious! Try the mini grilled cheese and the the smoked wings! My serve was awesome and the patio was free of bugs!

history.name: Food King
history.snippet: This place is the worse! The service is very poor and the owner doesn't not prepare the food until they receive payment when you are a person of color! This has happened to me 3 of the 3 times I have ordered here! Will not order from here ever again.

user_persona: Franklin family traveler who needs kid-friendly service, fast breakfasts, barbecue lunches, frozen custard, music bars, reasonable prices, and unique local stops.

history.name: Hard Rock Cafe
history.snippet: Service was incredibly slow. Food was good. Nice kids menu. $7.50 for a kids meal. Not bad for the location. On a positive note, it's in a really cool area - old train station.

history.name: Buffalo Wild Wings
history.snippet: Place was packed on Saturday night. We had a big group which they handled well. Buffalo chicken wrap is a great choice if you don't want wings. They gave our 8 year old the trivia console which kept her occupied. Ambience at bww is always blah. Reminds me of a place more for spo...

history.name: Slippery Noodle Inn
history.snippet: Didn't stay long. Had a beer. Played foosball with colleagues after work. Bar is broken up into sections. Looks like it would be a fun place with live bands. Sign stated it is the oldest bar in Indiana so we fell for it. Beer was good which made it worth it. Only blocks from foo...

history.name: Harry & Izzy's
history.snippet: Went there after work. Beer selection was good. We skipped the shrimp cocktail. Filet looked great. Tasted bland. Not sure how you can mess it up, but I wasn't impressed. I also didn't like the mushrooms side order. Sat outside on a nice evening. It's on the corner which could m...

history.name: Whit's Frozen Custard
history.snippet: In town to see friends . Good prices. Nice size for kids. Great toppings. Not much seating. Had to stand to eat.

history.name: Merridee's Breadbasket
history.snippet: Cute place for brunch. In town with friends who moved here. Has an old school woodsy feel which we really enjoyed. We were a big group of 9 (5 kids). Found tables together easily which was surprising considering the line to order was almost out the door. Line moved reasonably fa...

history.name: Puckett's Grocery & Restaurant
history.snippet: Here is an interesting place for a Chicagoan to visit. 8 of us sat outside for lunch on a Monday and it was packed. user_persona: Nashville food explorer who constantly tries new spots, loves brunch, hot chicken, cocktails, Asian food, patios, happy hours, and hates slow service.

history.name: O'Charley's Restaurant & Bar

history.snippet: The food was good but the service was on the slower side on a Saturday nite. I got the pork chops and it was very flavorful. My friend got the ribeye and it was nice and tender. Plenty of food to fill you up.

history.snippet: Love coming here for my laos comfort food. I always end up getting the same thing everytime. I know what is good here and I just stick with it. The staff is always nice and attentive. They can get very busy during lunch and dinner hour but I usually time it right and they always...

history.snippet: Well I can't say much about the food since i didn't eat here but the restaurant itself is beautiful!!! The ambience is more of an intimate place. And the roof top is spacious. I will definitely have to come back to try the food bc everyone has been raving about it.

user_persona: New Orleans diner who likes healthy vegan cafes, inventive ice cream, salmon salads, friendly teams, chill patios, and delicious neighborhood spots.

history.name: Carmo
history.snippet: Carmo is one of my favorite cafes in New Orleans. I strongly recommend it. Plenty of healthy vegan options. It's in a wonderful neighborhood, and the team is friendly.

history.name: Parish Parlor
history.snippet: Very delicious and inventive flavors. Brilliant interior design, and chill patio. Kind and attentive team. Enjoy their delicious ice cream.

history.name: Joey K's Restaurant & Bar
history.snippet: This is one of the most delicious restaurants ever. I strongly recommend it. I ordered the salad with salmon, it was one of the best salads I've had in my entire life.

user_persona: Reno casual diner who supports dependable local Italian, patio dining, family pizza, friendly service, and portions big enough for kids.

history.name: La Cucina Italian Eatery
history.snippet: Great restaurant and great food. Before COVID-19 we enjoyed eating on the patio. During the pandemic we continued to order and support this business.

history.name: MOD Pizza
history.snippet: The pizza is great and service was great. The kids devoured it. However I wish the largest size was bigger.

user_persona: Nashville local-flavor visitor who likes chef-led food tours, accommodating guides, songwriter venues, roomy inexpensive alternatives, and learning through live hospitality.

history.name: Walk Eat Nashville
history.snippet: I took the soBro tour and LOVED IT! So much fun. Had a chance to meet and chat with chefs and managers. Tour guide couldn't have been more accommodating and kind. She definitely researched this tour and saw to our every comfort. Great time! Learned a lot about Nashville and help...

history.name: Listening Room Cafe
history.snippet: Beautiful alternative to Bluebird Cafe. Great song writers in a Round format where each writer takes turns singing a song they have written. Roomy, beautiful venue. Younger scene than Bluebird if you cant get tickets there. Loved it. Two shows a night. Inexpensive and you can us...

user_persona: Sewell diner who favors fresh Italian seafood, coffee and dessert, Greek salads, Korean barbecue, fresh lettuce, shrimp, and non-fast-food local choices.

history.snippet: Woohoo! The best in Gloucester county, NJ. Great Italian restaurant, lots of fresh fish on the menu. I get the impression that the owner and the chef shop daily for fresh food to prepare. Food is expertly prepared and delicious. Great coffee and dessert.

history.snippet: Not many dining choices in Glassboro if you avoid fast food, so I appreciate having Think Greek salads and gyros. history.name: Nine Ting
history.snippet: Red meat, and lots of it. The lettuce was so green and fresh, and large, delicious shrimp. Ummmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmmm

user_persona: New Orleans safety-conscious visitor who wants outdoor seating, careful staff, welcoming Cajun brunch, great burritos, pubs, and casual comfort food.

history.snippet: I cannot speak more highly of this restaurant. I'm visiting NOLA Fri FL & the staff is welcoming and great, all adhering to CDC COVID precautions. history.name: Sovereign Pub
history.snippet: very nice environment, came here during my visit to NOLA and found the service great, would definitely recommend :)

history.name: Dat Dog
history.snippet: Nice spot with great food, great staff, and outdoor seating. Definitely would recommend.

user_persona: Indianapolis diner who likes shared Japanese plates, ramen, refined Mexican, mole, cocktails, pork belly biscuits, and good service at quick lunches.

history.snippet: I am so in love with this restaurant. The food is so good! I'd call it "refined" Mexican, though many of the dishes are very rustic and home style. history.name: Ixca
history.snippet: I am so in love with this restaurant. The food is so good! I'd call it "refined" Mexican, though mu of the dishes are very rustic and home style. So different from the run-of-the-mill Mexican restaurants. My favorite fish is the Chicken Mole. The service has been exceptional eve...

user_persona: Nashville local who loves authentic Italian beef, independent film, historic music venues, bargain chicken tenders, weekly neighborhood bars, sandwiches, and pizza.

history.snippet: This place is completely authentic and great. They serve a lot of things besides Italian sausage, beef, and Chicago pizza, but I don't know why anyone would ever order any of those things. This is a place you come to when you want real, delicious Italian beef. The concept, state...

history.snippet: Very simply one of the best venues in the country (in the world). Full of history, beautiful, amazing acoustics. Anything you see here will be worthwhile.

history.name: Belcourt Theatre
history.snippet: To some extent it doesn't matter what the Belcourt is like, since they have a monopoly on independent film in Nashville. history.snippet: These are extremely good sandwiches at a good price. Great soups and cookies and specials to boot. There's everything to love about Sloco. Any review less than 5 stars is lying to you.

history.snippet: I decided to try this place out since it was nearby, knowing nothing about it, and was pleasantly surprised to receive the best pizza I've had in a while. Castrillo's should be a frequent stop for anyone in the greater Sylvan area.

history.snippet: Look, the Stone Fox is great. We eat here every week. Everything on the menu is tasty, the specials are always good, and the drinks are well-done and well-priced. user_persona: Boise diner who likes friendly American brunch, brussels sprouts, shared tables, good food, and pizza shops with hospitable, interested staff.

history.snippet: Do yourself a favor and go to the Broadway location or even Meridian. The staff downtown is so rude, disinterested and inhospitable that people walk out disgusted without ordering multiple times nightly. We get the types you're trying to hire and the vibe you're going for, just...

history.snippet: Good food, friendly staff. Try the brussel sprouts! The shared tables can be fun, but give yourself plenty of time as they get booked out quite along wait kno the weekends

user_persona: Marlton diner who likes fresh hot Italian, Mexican service with full chips, buffalo shrimp, flatbreads, homemade ice cream, and transparent pricing.

history.name: Los Amigos Restaurant
history.snippet: First time after hearing great things and we were not disappointed. Food, service, atmosphere exceeded expectations. I was a little shocked at the final bill as they charge for everything.

history.snippet: One of my favorites! The Buffalo shrimp is a sure hit followed closely by any of the flat breads for starters. Went with a group of friends, the ahi tuna, turkey burger and meatloaf were all great. The dinner was topped off with the strawberry shortcake and banana cream. AWESOME!

history.snippet: Nothing better... From the homemade ice cream, to the water ice and the various types of soft serve (which includes non fat, low fat and sugar free)... Non fat cookies and cream is my favorite!

user_persona: Marcus Hook celebratory diner who values friendly regular service, excellent local pizza, anniversary-worthy seafood and steak, and staff who remember customers.

history.name: Italiano's
history.snippet: Had a pepperoni pizza to go last night. Best pizza I've had in this area in years. This will be Trudy's and my pizza place from now on, in addition to our regular Italian restaurant. Staff always well attending and friendly. Mario always greets with a big hello. Good people.

history.name: Harry's Savoy Grill
history.snippet: My wife and I celebrated our anniversary last night at Harry's. Top notch service, excellent food, and we will definetly be back. It took us over 30 years to return from our first visit. Not this time!

history.snippet: My go-to place for mussels, sweet potato fries, and beer!!! user_persona: Nashville diner who prioritizes fair-priced Mexican, great patios, shrimp and grits, hot chicken, drunken noodles, business lunches, and clean Southern meat-and-three.

history.name: Cinco De Mayo Mexican Restaurant - Whitebridge
history.snippet: If you want hip an trendy, go to Rosepepper. If you want GOOD Mexican food for a fair price go to Cinco. By far the best fajitas you will find in Nashville. They have a great patio for warm weather dining. Service is fast and friendly. Another HUGE plus...check out their Health...

history.name: Calhoun's
history.snippet: Great local spot for plain old American food. Try instead of applebee's or o'charlies. Get the spinach Maria side...SO good. Awesome patio with room to move.

history.snippet: Feel free to go here for trendy atmosphere and good margaritas but if you want good Mexican food...or good food period...do not eat here. The food is terrible. history.name: The Yellow Porch

history.name: Red Pony
history.snippet: Excellent service, atmosphere and food. Had the ahi tuna and it was amazing! A top pick for the Franklin area.

history.snippet: Best place in Cool Springs for a business lunch. Wait staff is incredible and attention to detail is their specialty. My favorite is the California Burger.

history.name: Thai Phooket
history.snippet: 100% satisfied with $8 drunken noodles and pad Thai. I like a little heat but couldn't take the medium...I know...pathetic.

history.snippet: This place has it going on. They have mastered their product down to the plating (white bread to soak up the sauce...so so good). Awesome hot chicken!

history.snippet: My favorite place to eat in Cool Springs. Food is awesome, service is great and it's super clean. THE BEST turnip greens and tartar sauce EVER.

user_persona: Camby fast-food diner who wants better-than-average burgers, buttery pizza crust, fair prices, and consistent wait times at quick casual chains.

history.snippet: I've been here a couple of times and I think the food is pretty good. Better than an average McDonalds. The only issue with this place is the wait time. Sometimes its fast and other times its incredibly slow.

history.snippet: I feel like taste of the dough better than little ceasars ,but little ceasars I'd also way cheaper. The buttery crust is good but sometimes the pan pizza is too oily.

history.name: Main Event on 96th
history.snippet: One disclaimer it is a smoking bar, but with that being said the food and service are some of the best in town. If your looking for a hometown bar look no further. They have darts pool and my favorite golden tee. Service is always excellent!!!

history.name: Crema Coffee Roasters
history.snippet: Crema is easily one of the best coffee shops in Nashville. The Baristas that work here are truly artists with what they do... You can taste the quality of their product every single time. I'd highly recommend stopping by next time you're downtown.

history.snippet: Hatch is one of the "must-show" places in Nashville for people that come to visit us. VERY friendly staff, great posters for sale. Parking can be difficult but that's downtown and certainly not Hatch's fault. You absolutely need to stop by if you're in the area.

history.snippet: Chaps has fantastic food and very friendly staff. They are doing well with all the restrictions. We love everything but their hummus platter, Tuscan chicken, homemade balsamic salad dressing, and soups are especially phenomenal.
