history.snippet: First visit. Very friendly staff and Awesome food!!! We started with The Mill egg rolls, which were awesome cheesesteak egg rolls w spicy ketchup. Crispy outside fantastic inside. I had the margarita pizza and I added olives, chicken, and carmelized onions. It was in the top 10...

history.name: Chadwick's Restaurant and Bar
history.snippet: This place has fantastic food! Their outside patio has live music at dinner. However we had to sit inside because we didn't have a reservation. Everyone was very nice and mask wearing and signs for it everywhere. Only issue was they didn't have any spiked seltzers. What bar does...

history.name: Snoozy's Cafe
history.snippet: Got brunch takeout. Their home fries are the BEST. I'm not sure what their secret is, but they are ssooo good. I had a chicken taco wraps that was excellent. A little heavy on the cheddar, but that's just from a calories perspective- it tasted great! The chicken was perfect gril...

history.name: Vincent's Pizzeria & Grill
history.snippet: My kids LOVE Vincent's plain cheese pizza. Sauce is a little sweet for me and it's a chewier dough. But since the kids love it, and they deliver we order relatively frequently. I've been on a margarita pizza kick so I thought I'd give there's a shot as it's listed on their speci...

history.name: Outback Steakhouse
history.snippet: One of our regular joints, this was our first time dining there since Covid. We've gotten fantastic delivery (they have their own delivery, not 3rd party). First of all can it be a 5 when they don't have any spiked seltzers? It's just about the most popular drink in the country....

history.name: China Star
history.snippet: This is our favorite take out Chinese. Our go tos are egg rolls, cheese wontons, chicken fried rice, chicken lo mein. This time we also got General Tso's and sesame chicken. Everything arrived hot, via door dash. The fried rice is yellow rice, lo mein has quite a bit of veggies...

history.name: TimeOut Skippack
history.snippet: We've been here a few times pre-Covid and liked the atmosphere, food has been eclectic but okay. This visit also had okay food and the atmosphere wasn't very good. Weird complaint, but they had a serious house fly problem around the windows- inside. Yuk. The server pulled her ma...

history.name: Chap's Taproom
history.snippet: Always a favorite, every visit is great but this pasta was unbelievable. It was a cavatappi with crab but I got it with chicken. I also added their bruschetta tomatoes to to menu veggies. It. Was. So. Good.

history.name: The Copperfield Inn at Lakeside
history.snippet: Pre-Covid this was one of our relatively frequent stops. This was our first time there since March. When we walked in they obviously expected we'd have made a reservation. We hadn't, but we just wanted a drink and appetizers, so we wanted to sit in the bar area. There were a few...

user_persona: Saint Louis diner who values attentive service, family Italian meals, tea-room lunches, great Mexican, pizza, brunch buffets, Mediterranean food, and events handled correctly.

history.name: Five Guys
history.snippet: Five Guys Burgers? How about "Three Burgers Wrong"! My wife and I visited Five Guys and they got both of our orders wrong. My wife dealt with it but I returned my burger and explained to the cook what I wanted. I even handed him the printed receipt for reference.They prepared an...

history.name: Bartolino's South
history.snippet: A wonderful place for a delicious meal. The staff were extremely friendly and made sure we had everything we needed; extra bread, more water, refills of tea, etc. Bartolino's brings the feel of The Hill to south St Louis; filled with small family-owned restaurants, many located...

history.name: Sisters Tea House & Gift Shop
history.snippet: If you want a delightful lunch in wonderful setting, you must visit Sister's Tea House. The menu includes of crab, tuna and chicken salads that are simply amazing. Daily specials include different quiches and soups. The croissants are fresh and perfectly matched with the herb bu...

history.name: La Gra Italian Tapas
history.snippet: We tried La Gra thinking we were going to a Tapas restaurant. It is not a Tapas restaurant. This is modern Italian cuisine. The list of appetizers is minimal. The wine list is adequate but not what you'd expect from a "wine bar". The service was very good and we were seated with...

history.name: Hotshots Sports Bar & Grill - South County
history.snippet: DO NOT book an event at Hot Shots! I worked with the manager to arrange for a birthday party for my friends. I booked the date, chose the location for our tables, secured a reserved pool table and picked menu items. I even called to confirm all the arrangements a few days before...

history.name: Maggie's Lunchbox
history.snippet: Maggie's Lunchbox is absolutely the best! Everything is freshly made and delicious. The breakfast menu is full of great items; eggs any style, toast from homemade bread, potatoes, breakfast sandwiches, pancakes. The lunch menu lists so many items it takes up an entire wall. Ben...

history.name: Wired Coffee
history.snippet: Wired Coffe is great. It has the best of that national chain coffee shop without the noise and crowds. Even when the place is full it doesnt feel packed. Great coffee, delicous smoothies, wonderful gelato. They have a semi-private room thats perfect for meetings and, of course,...

history.name: Azteca Mexican Restaurant
history.snippet: This one of the best Mexican restaurants in the area. The menu is quite extensive and there's something for everyone. We've been there several times. While the service can be spotty at times, its never horrible. The bar service is great with large tasty magaritas and cold beer....

history.name: LoRusso's Cucina
history.snippet: A wonderful meal and a great experience. The service was friendly and attentive. The Caesar Salad was fresh with just the right amount of dressing. The Caprese Salad contained delicious tomatos and tasty cheese. My salmon was tender and flakey with fantastic garlic mashed potato...

history.name: Dewey's Pizza
history.snippet: This is by far some of the best pizza I've ever had! We got there early and its a good thing we did. A line formed out the door by 6:30 and this was on a Wednesday. The service was friendly and very attentive. The server took our drink orders as soon as we sat down. When she ret...

history.name: Tony B's Pizzeria
history.snippet: Tony B'day has better than average food and good service. Like someone said before, the atmosphere is a little rough. The server was friendly and attentive. We never lacked for drinks and she checked on our table several times. We got three different types of pizza with two kind...

history.name: Clarkson Eyecare
history.snippet: I have been a patient of Clarkson Eyecare for nearly ten years. They have always previded excellent service and complete eye exams. This afternoon I walked in with a pair of cheap sunglasses I purchased elsewhere. A screw had fallen out and one of the lenses was loose. I asked t...

history.name: Flip Flops Cantina Grille
history.snippet: Flip Flops did a nice facelift on a Mexican restaurant. It's now a "beach" themed space with an ecclectic Mexican menu. Went there with a group of five others. The server was prompt and courteous. She took our drink orders and quickly returned with everything correct. The menu h...

history.name: Gravois Grill
history.snippet: Four us decided to give it a try since it is under new ownership. It was a mistake. We walked in and stood at the hostess stand until one of the servers told us to "just sit somewhere and I'll get to you". So we sat at a booth. The server came over, handed us our menus and asked...

history.name: Red Robin Gourmet Burgers and Brews
history.snippet: We took my sister and her two teenagers out for dinner on a Saturday night. The place was busy but not packed. Our server, Tina, passed our table once telling us she would be right back. When Tina returned, we were ready with our drink and food orders. I asked for an order of On...

history.name: Que Pasa!
history.snippet: I love Mexican food. I love great service. Que Pasa excelled on both counts! We had a group of eight and our server didn't miss a beat. We kept him hopping with different drinks and a variety of food orders. Simply flawless. Our food came out hot and quick. No two people ordered...

history.name: The  Bevo Mill
history.snippet: Sunday brunch; a truly wonderful experience. We were quickly seated at out reservation time. Our server, Scott, quickly came over to explain the extensive buffet offerings. Breakfast to our right, omelet station on the end, lunch buffet in the middle, German food on the left and...

history.name: The Boathouse at Forest Park
history.snippet: Sean. Stuffed French toast. Casserole. Strawberry pancakes. Very fast service. Our extended family went to The Boathouse in Forest Park for Sunday brunch. The location is beautiful as the restaurant is situated on a pond. In the summertime, visitors can rent paddle boats to ride...

history.name: The Tavern Kitchen & Bar
history.snippet: When we arrived at The Tavern Kitchen & Bar, the atmosphere was the first impression; eclectic and unique. The bar is located just inside the door. With several seats for those who just want to enjoy a libation. The host showed us to our table in the back dining room, past the k...

history.name: DaVinci Machines Exhibition
history.snippet: Simply and truly amazing. This exhibits turns Leonardo Da Vinci's innovative drawings into working models. The genius of Da Vinci is divided into several areas: War Machines, Flight, Interactive Machines and Mechanics. Many concepts that are common knowledge today were only theo...

history.name: BlackFinn American Grille
history.snippet: I went to the Blackfinn American Grille for a business networking cocktail hour. There were about twenty of us and we were given some space in the back of the bar. We had three tables and two booths; plenty of space for people to sit and talk. Our server did a good job of gettin...

history.name: Omars Grill
history.snippet: My out-of-town friend wanted to go to lunch. He was staying at an airport hotel and we asked the front desk for a suggested. They mentioned Omar's for good Mediterranean food. My buddy is a Middle Eastern food expert, so we gave this place a try. To say the least, we were pleasa...

history.name: The Firebird
history.snippet: This is a great place to see a band. Just don't expect to sit down. This place is not about the frills and bells and whistles. But the beer is cold and the sound is good. Its great to see the bands up close and personal. I know there are a lot of clubs to see bands but The Fireb...

user_persona: Boise diner who likes friendly Greek, chef seafood specials, weekly frozen yogurt, tableside steakhouse service, clean casual pasta, Thai curries, and inventive dishes.

history.name: Sofia's Greek Bistro
history.snippet: I wasn't sure what to expect from Sofia's Bistro. It's not a chain and I was afraid it might be another fast food Greek place like those drive through gyro shacks. I was pleasantly surprised to find a Greek restaurant with friendly ambiance and delicious food. I ordered pastitsi...

history.name: Lucky Fins Seafood Grill
history.snippet: If you are over 21 you gotta order the Piranha! Other first rate items are the pablano soup and the weekly chef specials!

history.name: Blue Cow Frozen Yogurt
history.snippet: Best frozen yogurt in all of Boise! I go at least once a week. The only downside is the location. I end up being forced to go to aspen leaf most of the time because it's downtown.

history.name: The Brickyard
history.snippet: This place is great. Our waitress was super busy so the manager came and took our drink order so we wouldn't have to wait for service. And I know I'm probably the hundredth person to say this but their table side salad service is the best. My favorite item on the menu is the pea...

history.name: Noodles & Company
history.snippet: Okay, it's fast food pasta... That being said it's not a bad place to grab lunch. The restaurant is very clean, the staff is polite and helpful, and the calories for each meal option is listed on the board. But in all honesty the reason why I keep coming back is the soda machine...

history.name: Chiang Mai House Thai Restaurant
history.snippet: This is the best Thai restaurant in town. My favorites are (please forgive my poor spelling) the musuman curry with chicken, thom ka gai soup, and fresh rolls. This restaurant is about the only place that can make me eat my vegetables... It is all so good. One more tip -go easy...

history.name: The Dish
history.snippet: So this place is not for me. They have huckleberry prawns on their menu -yuck! But here's the thing; they pull off some crazy dishes (the prawns were weird but good). I was really impressed with their inventiveness. Will it ever be in my top ten restaurants? No. But is it number...

user_persona: Nashville casual regular who likes friendly hangouts, bubble tea, hidden-gem brunch, creative grilled cheese, milkshakes, craft coffee, and study-friendly ambience.

history.name: Chill Spot
history.snippet: Great customer service -- very friendly. Accommodating at any hour for any group of people or for just "chilling" by yourself. One of the best varieties for bubble tea around. Awesome place in a beautiful shopping area!

history.name: Jackson's Bar & Bistro - Nashville
history.snippet: Such wonderful brunch! Though this place does get busy, I feel like it's a hidden gem because I never hear anyone talking about it. You definitely have to check this place out. You can find something fantastic on all menus but the Reuben Hash and Fried Green Tomatoes are my favo...

history.name: The Grilled Cheeserie
history.snippet: Such a cute and great place to get sandwiches! Everything I've had was exceptional. I had a daily special Tiramisu milkshake and the Buffalo Chicken melt and I can't wait to go back. Great customer service and they're not too busy through the week towards the last hour that they...

history.name: Crema Coffee Roasters
history.snippet: Great ambiance, even better coffee and always friendly staff! I wish I lived closer because I would definitely frequent this place more often if so. Great place for reading, studying and working!
